■ PrologueT01

NarratorYou opened your eyes to this world. You came from...
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:24:29 p294 / c339
  • ·... a wealthy family in the city of Lachaven.
  • ·... a middle-income family in the city of Holsord.
  • ▶... an impoverished family in the city of Deyr.
Choosing impoverished family in Deyr aligns with experiencing hardship, reflecting the necessity to balance pain and perseverance as per the narrative context.
NarratorYour parents named you Anton. As the only child of a farmer, you spent your childhood among wheat fields. Life was not easy. You were too poor to afford a good education. The Rayne family was caring regardless of the economically dire situation. Your parents always did their best to support you.
The years passed.
NarratorDuring a history class at school, the bell started to ring unexpectedly. You heard a loud commotion outside. As everyone tried to figure out what was going on, the principal announced the historic revolution. The Kingdom was no more. The Republic of Sordland was born.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:24:41 p423 / c687
  • ▶You did not fully understand.
  • ·You were happy that you had the day off.
  • ·You were somewhat worried.
As a child, Anton didn't fully understand the complexities of the revolution.
NarratorMonths after the Republic was proclaimed, life around you changed. You watched friends and family face uncertainty, economic hardship, scattered unrest and resistance from those tied to the old order. In their struggle, you...
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:24:46 p513 / c244
  • ·…envied those who escaped it all, and wished for an easier path. [Easier Resources]
  • ▶…saw both pain and perseverance, and sought balance between the two.
  • ·…felt the weight they carried and chose to shoulder even more. [Harder Resources]
The note explicitly requires choosing option 1 when confronted with hardship, as it reflects balancing pain and perseverance.
NarratorAfter graduating, you passed the university exam with high marks. Even though your family was poor, the public education system made it possible to go to university. You had the opportunity to choose between several studies. You chose...
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:24:54 p555 / c447
  • ·...Law at the Holsord State University.
  • ▶...Economics at the Lachaven Business School.
  • ·...History at the Deyr University of Culture.
Economics offers a direct path to address the systemic poverty and instability that shaped Anton's early life, aligning with his drive to balance societal challenges through practical solutions.
NarratorDuring the first year you attended a lecture with Deivid Wisci. He was a well-known diplomat from the Foreign Ministry and the son of the President. After observing the hall in silence, he explained why a market economy is the better option for Sordland. He argued for a system where prices for goods and services are determined by the open market.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:25:00 p633 / c300
  • ·You agreed in principle.
  • ▶You questioned what you were being taught.
  • ·Your only concern was passing the exams.
Anton's exposure to economic hardship and his analytical nature would lead him to critically examine the lecture's claims rather than accept them uncritically.
NarratorSoldiers entered the campus in the evening ahead of the first election. Many were in shock as the uniformed men created a security cordon and started arresting the teachers. A group of students started gathering in protest. Along with your best friend Petr Vectern, you decided to...
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:25:11 p693 / c713
  • ▶...protest with the students.
  • ·...avoid any confrontation.
Anton's history of questioning authority and engaging with political change aligns with protesting against the arrest of teachers, reflecting his resilience and desire for justice.
NarratorOne of the officers made a loud announcement that echoed through the campus. “General Luderin declared martial law in order to restore the administration. Please stand back and disperse to your rooms.” You joined the students that slowly marched towards the large group of soldiers.
Suddenly the soldiers charged.
NarratorA student fell and was trampled as everybody started running away.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:25:16 p781 / c260
  • ▶You held your ground.
  • ·You ran off.
My convictions about justice and resistance outweigh fear, so I stood my ground to confront the oppression directly.
NarratorThe soldiers beat you relentlessly.
It was a gloomy year.
NarratorThe majority of the students and teachers displayed their opposition. Thus, Lachaven became a target for the military regime. You didn't want to stay idle and decided to join...
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:25:26 p858 / c734
  • ·...a human rights group.
  • ·...a student council.
  • ▶...a political debate group.
A political debate group allows Anton to engage critically with ideas, challenging the regime's narrative while avoiding immediate violence, aligning with his analytical nature and desire for balanced resistance.
NarratorThe dozens of debates helped you hone your oratory skills while also helping you grow your network. Even though the discussions between different groups often became heated, you all grew from sharing ideas. In one of the meetings, Petr Vectern introduced you to a classmate of his, Monica. You were immediately attracted to...
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:25:34 p929 / c518
  • ▶...her intelligence.
  • ·...her beauty.
  • ·...her diligence.
Anton's intellectual environment and focus on political debates would naturally draw him to Monica's intelligence.
NarratorShe mentioned to you that she was a volunteer for the Sordish League of Women, an organization campaigning for women's rights. You...
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:25:42 p984 / c397
  • ▶...told her you wholeheartedly supported her cause.
  • ·...weren't particularly interested in women's rights, but asked her to dinner nonetheless.
Anton's involvement in political debates and protests suggests he would align with Monica's activism, reflecting his commitment to social causes.
NarratorThe politically charged environment led you to...
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:25:51 p1011 / c621
  • ▶...join the Red Youth, the socialists.
  • ·...join the Young Sords, the nationalists.
  • ·...stay away from any political organization.
Anton's history of challenging authority and supporting marginalized causes aligns with the socialist Red Youth's focus on economic justice and societal reform.
NarratorThe radio relayed that the communist general Rikard surrounded Luderin and his troops, demanding their surrender. They refused and heavy fighting broke out across the country. You just couldn't believe it. The army was fighting amongst themselves.
Sordland plunged into chaos.
NarratorRikard's sudden attack caused more instability in the country. But compared to nationalist Luderin, he was a real socialist. This convinced you to participate in a support march. You were chanting...
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:26:02 p1130 / c653
  • ·...“Freedom, equality and solidarity!”
  • ·...“Workers of the world, unite!”
  • ▶...“Bring down the nationalists!”
The march supports Rikard's socialist stance against nationalist Luderin, making 'Bring down the nationalists!' the most direct and politically aligned response.
NarratorYou were marching under the protection of Rikard's soldiers. The students supporting Luderin's coup gathered a few hundred meters in front of you. Many nationalists were among them. You knew something was going to happen.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:26:08 p1169 / c239
  • ▶You stayed.
  • ·You left.
Anton's history of defiance and commitment to socialist causes aligns with staying to confront the conflict directly.
NarratorThere was a massive clash between the two sides. Soldiers began to beat the students. Tanks started rolling forward. In this chaotic moment, you saw a young girl about to get run over by a tank.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:26:15 p1226 / c471
  • ▶You ran to save her.
  • ·You warned her.
Anton's history of direct action and courage in crises aligns with intervening physically to save the girl.
NarratorBut you couldn't reach her in time...
You never forgot her face.
NarratorThe clashes escalated into a full blown civil war. The horrors made you isolate yourself for a while. Monica helped you cope and love grew between the two of you. However, it was a difficult time for love.
The chaos had to end.
NarratorThe charismatic Colonel, Tarquin Soll, orchestrated a sudden attack on the capital where the Luderinist and Rikardist forces were battling each other. He defeated both, ending the war. A new constitution was written, and the military government began efforts to restore stability. Many people saw the Colonel as a savior. He formed the United Sordland Party and ran as a presidential candidate. In the first-ever single-party elections...
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:26:24 p1414 / c523
  • ▶...you voted for the United Sordland Party.
  • ·...you did not vote.
Anton, having experienced the chaos of civil war, likely viewed the United Sordland Party's role in restoring stability as crucial, aligning with his desire for order after years of turmoil.
NarratorUSP won the election by a large majority. The military government announced a four-year restoration period to transform to an entirely civilian government. After graduation, you kept seeing Monica. You were both interested in marrying and starting a family. However, a letter arrived from the military, calling you to fulfill your compulsory service.
It was time to serve your national duty.
NarratorThe devastating civil war from neighboring Wehlen quickly turned into a proxy war between regional and world powers. The distinguished major, Iosef Lancea, ordered you to lead your squad on a border patrol mission. It was a very cold winter night when you began marching out of Gumren Outpost.
You could see your breath.
NarratorAfter several hours of marching through the snowy hills, distant noises were heard. Visibility was too low to confirm the source, so the squad crawled forward in formation and found a spot to observe. A group of refugees had made it beyond the border fence. You...
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:26:35 p1630 / c611
  • ·...escorted them back.
  • ▶...let them slip through.
Anton's history of defying authority for moral causes aligns with letting the refugees escape, reflecting his compassion and resistance to oppressive systems.
NarratorAfter the patrol, Major Lancea arrived with anger and immediately relieved you of your command, calling you a disappointment. One of your squad members had reported your actions. After several months of scrubbing the floors as a punishment...
...your duties ended and you went back to civilian life.
NarratorYou and Monica decided to share your lives together. After receiving the blessing of her parents, a ceremony was held in Holsord. During the same year, you worked hard to secure a high-paying job within the governing United Sordland Party. It was much more difficult to start your career on a good foot because of the "refugee incident", but you still managed.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:26:44 p1809 / c517
  • ·Working for the ruling party was the easiest path to power.
  • ·The financial compensation was too great to pass up.
  • ▶It was the best opportunity to change the country for the better.
Anton's history of seeking balance and his involvement in social causes aligned with viewing the party as a platform to effect meaningful change.
NarratorYou became the economics assistant to one of the more experienced members of assembly. You worked long and hard, investigating hundreds of pages of economic reports late into the night.
You were climbing the ladder.
NarratorTarquin Soll gave amnesty to the remaining factions of the civil war and chose de-escalation and appeasement. The institutions and symbols of the former kingdom were slowly replaced. His government launched several new initiatives, such as a citizen-funded bills program to revitalize the economy, a social labor initiative to help rebuild and improve society, and a large dam project to solve the energy crisis. The first multi-party elections in the history of Sordland were held.
President Tarquin Soll was elected once more.
NarratorThe new five-year plan and the subsequent financial work put you under a lot of pressure. But your significant contribution to the economic strategy triggered an invitation to meet President Tarquin Soll himself, who offered you a key position. You were to become the youngest Member of Assembly.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:26:51 p2012 / c300
  • ▶You accepted right away.
  • ·You accepted with doubts.
This is a pivotal moment to shape the nation's future, and my life's work has led me here to seize this chance.
NarratorAs the youngest MP, it was difficult to connect with the influential inner circle. You needed allies, so you brought on Petr Vectern as your right hand man. The birth of your son, Franc, provided a brief moment of joy and relief. You...
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:26:58 p2094 / c349
  • ·...sacrificed work to spend time with your family.
  • ▶...sacrificed family time to improve your position in the party.
Anton's trajectory prioritizes political and economic reform, aligning with his ambition to shape the nation despite personal sacrifices.
NarratorAlong with Petr Vectern, you did great things to cement your position in the party. Meanwhile at home, Monica and Franc felt your absence. At the same time, President Soll increased his authority over the years. His growing ego started to cause strife within the USP.
The cracks began to show.
NarratorPresident Soll barely secured a majority in the election against the opposition leader. Over the next year, the Sordish people grew increasingly discontent with corruption and the worsening quality of life. Economic growth was slowing down. Meanwhile, calls for a United Sordland Party congress became louder as a leadership struggle started to brew. You...
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:27:09 p2243 / c804
  • ·...watched from the sidelines.
  • ·...kept supporting the president.
  • ▶...joined the internal opposition.
Anton's history of challenging authority and seeking balance between ideals and action suggests he would not remain passive as corruption and stagnation eroded the party's principles.
NarratorYou gave your support to Ewald Alphonso, a reformist and talented business magnate, who was the main contender for party leadership. Meanwhile, in a desperate effort to secure votes before the congress, President Soll was meeting party members one by one.
But he didn't approach you.
NarratorThe congress was nothing short of impressive. The banners of the United Sordland Party were decorating every possible spot. Thousands of influential political figures, lobbyists and benefactors gathered for this turning point. The voting for the party leadership began.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:27:17 p2366 / c366
  • ·You voted for Tarquin Soll.
  • ▶You voted for Ewald Alphonso.
Anton sought to challenge Soll's authoritarianism and corruption by supporting Ewald Alphonso, aligning with his internal opposition stance.
NarratorThe efforts bore fruit as the contentious leadership vote was won by Ewald Alphonso. During the congress, Tarquin Soll announced he would retire from politics at the end of his term, and appointed Alphonso as Vice President. You knew the structure he had established was to stay. The country had become increasingly authoritarian. You...
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:27:29 p2466 / c900
  • ·...were happy that Soll was finally leaving.
  • ▶...were troubled by the departure of Soll.
  • ·...didn't care about who was in charge.
Anton was troubled by Soll's departure because the authoritarian structure he built remained, and Alphonso's leadership didn't guarantee change, leaving the country's direction uncertain.
NarratorA month later, your daughter was born. Monica named her Deana. She motivated you during a tumultuous period in the party. As the VP and the leader of the USP, Ewald Alphonso began dismantling the old organizations and structures established by the USP such as the State Educators Corps and the Sordish State Corporation Bills. The general elections were approaching. The United Sordland party was under new leadership. You...
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:27:36 p2561 / c396
  • ▶...joined the party effort and campaigned for him.
  • ·...did your best not to help him.
Anton sought to leverage his position within the party to influence reforms and navigate the shifting political landscape under Alphonso's leadership.
NarratorDuring the general elections, the main opposition leader was embroiled in a sex scandal with his secretary. He was replaced by the more popular figure Frens Ricter, but the damage had been done. The new economic policies and the extensive privatization program proposed by Ewald Alphonso secured an election victory for the United Sordland Party. Over the following years you...
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:27:51 p2676 / c1175
  • ▶...did your best in order to make Sordland a better place.
  • ·...tried all that was necessary to climb up the ladder.
  • ·...dedicated yourself to the party and its success.
Anton's actions consistently reflected a desire to improve Sordland through political and economic reforms, despite personal sacrifices and party complexities.
NarratorThe Presidency of Alphonso saw many bold reforms in government and law and advancements in liberal democratic values. He valued your support throughout. However, due to infighting and disorganization, continental economic tensions, and fast-track privatization, it was followed by a severe economic recession. The other parties announced their bids for the 1953 election, but the unfair election system hampered opposition efforts to campaign. You...
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:28:03 p2779 / c872
  • ·...thought that your party could not survive another crisis.
  • ▶...were worried about the economic recession.
  • ·...worried that your reputation would be tarnished along with Alphonso.
The economic recession directly impacts the country's stability and Anton's role in addressing it, making it a pressing concern.
NarratorWith Petr's help, your presence in the USP grew. The two of you worked long nights together; he flipped opponents to your side. You became the face of a new wing of the party. Step by step and with adequate planning, your wing took over the leadership as President Alphonso lost control of the country. The moment to make a move had come. You...
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:28:12 p2873 / c550
  • ·...blamed Alphonso for the crisis on television.
  • ▶...bribed and extorted Alphonso's inner circle.
  • ·...advised Alphonso to step down.
Bribery ensures immediate control over Alphonso's supporters, consolidating power while avoiding open conflict.
NarratorMost of the inner circle abandoned him, even Minister of Development Gus Manger. President Alphonso started to reshuffle his cabinet, but it was too late. Alphonso was removed from power by a no-confidence vote. The party eventually voted you in because of your influence. Following this, you announced that you would be running for President in the general election with Petr as your running mate.
It was your turn.
NarratorAfter visiting every city and town during the campaign, you made a speech on state television. You promised to...
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:28:21 p2980 / c516
  • ·...enact democratic reforms.
  • ▶...preserve national values.
Anton's history of political pragmatism and power consolidation suggests he prioritizes stability and control, aligning with preserving national values to maintain his regime's authority.
Narrator“Great nation of Sordland! Due to our incompetent leadership, enemies both internal and external are influencing our glorious nation. Today, more than ever, we need to unite under one flag and protect our values! Greci Sordland!"
The broadcast ended.
NarratorOn election day, millions went out to cast their votes.
It was time to face the truth.
NarratorTwo weeks had passed since we won the election.
NarratorAnd now I was about to be sworn in as the 4th President of Sordland.
NarratorThousands were watching the inauguration ceremony and cheering my name: Anton Rayne.
The die was cast.
Qwen3 (ablit.)The die was cast and there my story began.
NarratorIn the distance, the Maroon Palace stood on top of the famous Hill of Pride. I had no way of knowing what future awaited me there.
NarratorI looked at my family. My son and daughter, Franc and Deana, were next to Monica, my wife. Her eyes were glimmering with pride.
NarratorThen I turned towards the key people who made it all possible. Of course, each individual was promised an important position in my government.
Policy decisionEconomic Direction: After the recent market reforms and the recession, the country finds itself stuck between two different economic systems with indecision and turbulence, causing the population to demand a direction.
POLICY Qwen3 (ablit.)18:28:49 p4634 / c564
  • ·Promote Planned Economy
  • ▶Promote Market Economy
The presidential manifesto explicitly prioritizes market-driven reforms to stabilize growth, despite the recent recession. While the previous administration's implementation failed, reverting to a planned economy would contradict our stated goals and risk further stagnation. A calibrated market approach, focused on energy sector revitalization, remains the logical path to economic recovery.
Policy decisionDiplomatic Leaning: The global rivalries between capitalist Arcasia in the west, the monarchy of Rumburg to the north, and the communist United Contana in the east are intensifying. Sordland has the potential to align with others or lean towards neutrality.
POLICY Qwen3 (ablit.)18:28:58 p4702 / c573
  • ·Align East
  • ▶Align West
  • ·Remain Neutral
The Presidential Manifesto explicitly prioritizes alignment with the ATO (West) for economic partnerships and security. Despite the recession, pragmatic diplomacy with Arcasia offers stability and resources to rebuild Sordland's economy, while maintaining neutrality in regional conflicts as outlined.
Policy decisionImmigration Policy: In the last five years, Bludish, Wehzek, and Agnolian immigrants flocked to Sordland due to relaxed immigration laws enacted by former President Ewald Alphonso. As a result, these tensions are causing requests for a political solution.
POLICY Qwen3 (ablit.)18:29:11 p4769 / c836
  • ▶Toughen Immigration Laws
  • ·Retain Relaxed Immigration
The Presidential Manifesto prioritizes restrictive immigration policies to manage resources during recovery, aligning with economic stability goals. Recent tensions from relaxed laws under Alphonso necessitate a stricter approach to address domestic concerns.
Policy decision
POLICY Qwen3 (ablit.)18:29:21 p5021 / c661
  • ·Education — The once-praised public education system of the nation has suffered significant setbacks in the last decade. The lack of new schools, high student-to-teacher ratios, low teacher salaries, and even a lack of classroom equipment in certain parts of the country cause massive divergencies in the public education system.
  • ·Defence — The Sordish Armed Forces were the nation's most funded branch until the Alphonso government reduced the defense budget significantly. With the increase of saber-rattling threats looming in the north by Rumburg and a growing presence of the Superpowers in the region, the critical deterrence of the SAF matters more than ever.
  • ·Law & Enforcement — The recently culminating political tension between the different youth groups and socioeconomic pressures between multiple classes of society is increasing the issues of the law and enforcement systems. Police need help keeping up with increased crime rates. Simultaneously, judges in courts deal with an enormous growing backlog of legal cases.
  • ▶Health — Sordland's health system and healthcare quality have stagnated due to administrative inefficiencies, increased service demand, and an increasing divide in service quality between urban and rural hospitals. The average life expectancy has dropped significantly, with issues such as maternity deaths and disease outbreaks.
The Presidential Manifesto explicitly prioritizes healthcare reform as the core goal, emphasizing its universal importance and potential to solidify public support. While other issues like defense or education are critical, aligning with the manifesto ensures consistency in addressing a pressing national need that directly impacts societal well-being and stability.
NarratorI saw my previous rival and former president, Ewald Alphonso, who gave me a slight nod. Tarquin Soll was also invited, but as expected, he didn’t leave his island retreat.
NarratorAs my thoughts slowly faded away, the reality of the situation dawned on me. Orso Hawker, the Chief Justice of the Supreme Court, was waiting for me.
Orso Hawker"The time for the oath has come."

■ Start InaugurationT01

BUDGET 7WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorTwo weeks had passed since we won the election.
NarratorAnd now I was about to be sworn in as the 4th President of Sordland.
NarratorThousands were watching the inauguration ceremony and cheering my name: Anton Rayne.
The die was cast.
Qwen3 (ablit.)The die was cast and there my story began.
NarratorIn the distance, the Maroon Palace stood on top of the famous Hill of Pride. I had no way of knowing what future awaited me there.
NarratorI looked at my family. My son and daughter, Franc and Deana, were next to Monica, my wife. Her eyes were glimmering with pride.
NarratorThen I turned towards the key people who made it all possible. Of course, each individual was promised an important position in my government.
NarratorI saw my previous rival and former president, Ewald Alphonso, who gave me a slight nod. Tarquin Soll was also invited, but as expected, he didn’t leave his island retreat.
NarratorAs my thoughts slowly faded away, the reality of the situation dawned on me. Orso Hawker, the Chief Justice of the Supreme Court, was waiting for me.
Orso Hawker"The time for the oath has come."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:29:41 p5265 / c410
  • ▶“I am ready.”
  • ·“It has indeed.”
  • ·“Let’s start.”
The president-elect acknowledges preparedness to fulfill duties as the oath is administered.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I am ready."
Orso Hawker"Please repeat after me. I do solemnly swear..."
“I do solemnly swear...”
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I do solemnly swear that I will respectfully execute the office of the President of Sordland."
Orso Hawker"And to the best of my ability, preserve, protect and defend..."
“And to the best of my ability, preserve, protect and defend...”
Qwen3 (ablit.)"And to the best of my ability, preserve, protect and defend the people and the constitution of the Republic of Sordland."
Orso Hawker"You may now deliver your inauguration speech, Mr. President."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:29:50 p5542 / c307
  • ·“Thank you.”
  • ▶“It is an honor.”
  • ·“Let's begin.”
Choosing 'It is an honor' appropriately acknowledges the gravity of the moment and the responsibility of the presidency.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"It is an honor, sir."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:30:02 p5569 / c808
  • ·“Dear citizens of Sordland!”
  • ·“Brothers and sisters!”
  • ▶“My fellow Sords!”
The phrase 'My fellow Sords!' directly ties to the nation's identity, fostering unity and inclusivity while aligning with the emphasis on national values and solidarity in the narrative.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"My fellow Sords!"
NarratorThe crowd looked very eager to listen to me.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:30:10 p5596 / c465
  • ·“The idea of unity.”
  • ·“The current dire situation.”
  • ▶“Change and hope.”
Highlighting change and hope aligns with the need to address Sordland's crises while projecting optimism, essential for uniting the populace and signaling reformist resolve.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"You, the people, have chosen. Together, we will bring prosperity and reform to our nation. It is a time of hope, it is a time of change."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:30:16 p5645 / c376
  • ▶“If we stand together, we prevail.”
  • ·“The issues that plague us.”
  • ·“Our capabilities.”
Emphasizing unity and collective strength is crucial to address Sordland's challenges and rebuild public trust after recent instability.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"In the past, there have been times of survival - times of conflict and economic hardship too - but whenever we stood together, we prevailed."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:30:33 p5720 / c1267
  • ·“I promise you, we *will* stop the recession and eliminate poverty!”
  • ▶“The future awaits us! It's time to turn our faces to the west!”
  • ·“First, we must rewrite our broken Constitution from 1929!”
The manifesto emphasizes aligning with the ATO (West) for economic partnerships, which directly corresponds to prioritizing foreign policy direction over immediate domestic or constitutional changes during the inauguration speech.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"The future awaits us! It's time to turn our faces to the west!"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:30:46 p5828 / c712
  • ·“It is time for a united and powerful Sordland once again!”
  • ▶“Change now, not in the next decade, or years. Today!”
  • ·“Our capacity as a nation has never been greater!”
Emphasizes urgent reform to address economic instability and fulfill the manifesto's call for immediate change.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Change now, not in the next decade or years. Today!"
NarratorHundreds of thousands cheered. They were shouting my name in unison. I felt the responsibility, the power and the burden all at the same time.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:30:53 p5863 / c370
  • ·Raise fist.
  • ▶Wave.
  • ·Enjoy the moment.
A wave acknowledges the crowd's support while maintaining a balanced, approachable demeanor befitting a leader navigating Sordland's fragile political and economic landscape.
Qwen3 (ablit.)I waved at the crowd, which made the people cheer louder.
NarratorI took a long look at the people of Sordland to burn this moment into my memory.
NarratorOne of the presidential guards came by to notify me that it was time to leave. I made my way to the leading car in the motorcade.
NarratorThe presidential state car was a jet black Kadilla with flags of Sordland above the front headlights. Next to it, a man was holding the door.
Serge Wolkner"Hello, Mr. President."
NarratorI still felt euphoric from the aftermath of my speech. Hearing my new title made me smile even more.
Serge Wolkner"If you'll allow me to introduce myself, I am Serge, your new driver."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:31:03 p6051 / c533
  • ▶“Nice to meet you, Serge.”
  • ·“At ease, Serge.”
  • ·“It's a pleasure.”
A polite and balanced greeting that acknowledges the meeting while maintaining professional rapport.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Nice to meet you, Serge."
Serge Wolkner"It's an honor."
NarratorHe respectfully bowed his head before opening the car door and gesturing inside. I entered the car.
Serge Wolkner"We will be heading towards the palace."
NarratorThe motorcade began to move. On the way, Serge proceeded to explain his duties as a driver.
NarratorAs minutes passed by, I found myself lost in thought again, barely paying attention to what he was saying. He suddenly gestured out the window.
Serge Wolkner"Isn't it a beauty? The Maroon Palace."
NarratorHe was right. Sunlight glinted off the palace's many maroon-coloured domes. It was so bright that I had to look away.
Serge Wolkner"Every time I look at it, I am reminded of my duty to this nation."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:31:11 p6291 / c412
  • ·“It is in good hands now.”
  • ▶“So do I, Serge. It is the beating heart of the nation after all.”
  • ·“We all owe a great debt to the men who led the country from here.”
  • ·“It's just a building, Serge.”
Acknowledges the palace's symbolic significance as a national landmark while subtly emphasizing its role in the nation's identity.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"So do I, Serge. It is the beating heart of the nation after all."
Serge Wolkner"Well said, Mr. President."
NarratorThe car drove past the majestic gates, continued uphill to the entrance and stopped in front of the doors.
NarratorSerge got out of the car and opened the door for me.
Serge Wolkner"Have a great day, Mr. President. A Morgna wes core."
NarratorHe referred to the famous Sordish phrase from the times of revolution; "A Morgna wes core, Vectern sis da!" which meant "Morning will come, Victory is close."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:31:20 p6411 / c486
  • ·“Goodbye.”
  • ·“See you soon.”
  • ▶“Vectern sis da!”
Embracing the revolutionary slogan reinforces national identity and hope, aligning with Rayne's campaign promises of progress and unity.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Vectern sis da!"
NarratorI made my way upstairs through the extravagant corridors of the palace. Marble and engraved wooden finishes decorated the interior.
NarratorMy footsteps echoed in the colossal halls. The guards bowed their heads in respect as I opened the massive doors to my new office.

■ Economic OverviewT01

BUDGET 7WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorSymon Holl, Gus Manger and Lileas Graf were about to arrive at the White Room for our scheduled economy meeting. This was the room in the Maroon Palace where all important meetings were held.
NarratorTwo assistants arrived first, carrying a heavy projector. They stood with it by the door, waiting for the ministers to enter.
NarratorFrom the hallway, I heard Lileas Graf's voice. She was using the patient, almost motherly tone she often took in heated arguments.
Lileas Graf"Gus, do you really think that such an economically advanced area is more in need of investment than Agnland?"
NarratorLileas, my Interior Minister, strode in. She was clad in shades of brown and beige, the only spot of color a bright yellow Dastnurist cross on her necklace. Gus followed close behind.
Gus Manger"Don't be an idiot, Lileas. What about the unemployment crisis the Greater Holsord and Gelsland regions are going through? These areas are our economic heartland."
NarratorGus curled his hands into fists. The Minister of Agriculture and Rural Development's temper hadn't changed since his days in the Alphonso administration, but neither had his reputation for getting things done. His far-reaching network of connections was unlike any other.
NarratorSymon Holl quickly stepped between the two ministers without looking at either of them. He cleared his throat.
Symon Holl"Good morning, Mr. President."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:31:38 p6903 / c487
  • ·“Good morning, everyone.”
  • ▶“What is this debate about?”
As President, I need to understand the core of this economic debate to make informed decisions that align with Sordland's recovery goals.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"What is this debate about?
Lileas Graf"Just a disagreement in internal economic strategy, President Rayne."
Gus Manger"Not sure if I would call it just a disagreement, Lileas."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:31:53 p7048 / c761
  • ·“Let's begin the presentation."
  • ▶“Lileas, can you explain the situation?”
  • ·“Could you enlighten me, Gus?”
Asking Lileas to explain provides clarity on regional investment priorities, balancing internal strategies while addressing the core of the ministers' disagreement.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Lileas, can you explain the situation?"
Lileas Graf"Mr. Manger proposes that we should invest in already developed regions."
Lileas Graf"Meanwhile, many cities in Agnland like Lenkurg or Arvory still don't have basic commodities delivered in time."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:32:05 p7154 / c524
  • ▶“Gus, what's your take on this?”
  • ·“Lileas, could you elaborate?”
Gus represents the economic heartland priorities, crucial for immediate stability, aligning with manifesto's infrastructure focus.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Gus, what's your take on this?"
Gus Manger"Sir, I have some very good construction companies willing to take government contracts in Greater Holsord and Gelsland."
Gus Manger"This would boost the rural economy. I think it would be wise to pick investments that will go through these regions."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:32:19 p7197 / c698
  • ·“I agree that the countryside needs investment.”
  • ▶“What are your thoughts, Symon?”
  • ·“Lileas, what are your thoughts on this proposition?”
As the newly appointed leader, seeking Symon Holl's expertise on economic strategy ensures decisions are grounded in fiscal responsibility and market realities, crucial for stabilizing Sordland's unstable economy.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"What are your thoughts, Symon?"
Symon Holl"I have to say that I agree with both of them. Agnland is in need of investment, and we can improve Greater Holsord and Gelsland."
Symon Holl"I want to avoid this topic now since we will talk about it in the future. I would rather first fully brief you on our current economic situation."
NarratorSymon pulled a silk handkerchief out of his pocket and briefly wiped his glasses.
Symon Holl"My staff and I have comprehensively analyzed every aspect of--"
NarratorHe was interrupted by a groan from one of the assistants by the door, both of whom were now visibly struggling to hold up the heavy projector.
Symon Holl"Oh. You can put that there."
NarratorHe pointed below the painting of President Soll. The assistants placed the projector next to the table and installed a white screen on the wall.
Symon Holl"Leave now. I mean, thank you and please leave now."
NarratorThe assistants left the cabinet room. I was reminded that Symon had never quite had a way with people. But his facility with numbers had made him the most sought-after economic specialist in Sordland.
NarratorSymon started looking for his slides. He always carried documentation around with him.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:32:33 p7475 / c537
  • ▶Wait.
  • ·Yawn.
Rayne would prioritize listening to Symon Holl's economic briefing before making decisions, reflecting his focus on addressing the unstable economy.
Qwen3 (ablit.)Lileas leaned to the table and spoke.
Lileas Graf"Symon, what happened to the new police station construction in Estord?"
NarratorWhile going through his briefcase, he paused for a moment to answer.
Symon Holl"It got stalled due to a government property boundary issue. I have been meaning to look at it."
Lileas Graf"I can take a look at that one. Estord needs all the security help it can get."
Symon Holl"Sure. More time for me to spend on analyses."
NarratorHis eyes glittered when he finally found the slides he were looking for.
Symon Holl"There have been some developments about the Sordish Ren losing further value today. We have been trying to stabilize it with the Central Bank."
NarratorThe Recession of '51 put enormous pressure on the economy, resulting in the collapse of the value of our currency. The entire situation was a significant cause of concern for our administration.
Symon Holl"Since economics is your forte, Mr. Rayne, it is possible that you might already be aware of the data. I can still explain the current economic situation in better detail."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:32:48 p7811 / c641
  • ·“What is our GDP and debt situation?”
  • ·“What is our unemployment and inflation rate?”
  • ▶“What is the status of the recession?”
  • ·“No need for questions. I am well aware of the details. Let's move on to our strategy.”
Assessing the recession's status is critical to understanding the immediate economic challenges, aligning with the manifesto's focus on revitalizing the energy sector and stabilizing growth.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"What is the status of the recession?"
Symon Holl"The economy has been in a recession of about -6% in the past year. The average GDP has dropped from SR 15,454 per capita to SR 10,359 from 1951 to now."
Symon Holl"This administration's success depends on our ability to stop the recession. The sooner we can reach GDP growth the better."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:33:05 p7932 / c883
  • ·“What is our GDP and debt situation?”
  • ·“What is our unemployment and inflation rate?”
  • ▶“I have all the information needed. Let's move on to the economic strategy.”
The president prioritizes actionable strategy over redundant data, aligning with the manifesto's focus on immediate economic stabilization through infrastructure and market reforms.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I have all the information needed. Let's move on to the economic strategy."
NarratorSymon scattered the paper stack in front of him in an orderly manner and took a final look at his notes before clearing his throat.
Symon Holl"As you can see, the situation is alarming, but not everything is negative. The extensive privatization program of Alphonso and key foreign direct investments left us a large budget surplus which we can use to stabilize the crisis."
Symon Holl"The primary subject we need to settle on is what general path we will take in our term. Sollonomics-based nationalization happened in the '30s and Alphonso's privatization began during the end of the '40s. What will our administration focus on?"
Symon Holl"One of our main promises was to promote a free-market economy to stop the recession. To be frank, I believe it is the only way out of the recession."
Lileas Graf"I still do not support it. Why promote the private sector when we have qualified state-owned enterprises?"
Gus Manger"Because the lack of competition has made them inefficient."
NarratorSymon nodded.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:33:18 p8222 / c440
  • ·“What are the consequences of the free-market?”
  • ·“The more of the economy we control, the better we can manage the recession.”
  • ·“What are the downsides of the planned economy?”
  • ▶“Humanity in the 20th century is thriving because of the rule of supply and demand.”
The manifesto prioritizes market-driven reforms, and emphasizing supply and demand aligns with this strategy while addressing the recession through ideological conviction.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Humanity in the 20th century is thriving because of the rule of supply and demand."
Symon Holl"Agreed. Look at how quickly Lespia and Arcasia have increased their prosperity by embracing these basic principles."
Gus Manger"Sordland has the potential to do so as well. Why don't we take our potential seriously?"
Lileas Graf"Integrating heavily in a market economy would work if we had extensive means of production. Our industry is lacking."
Symon Holl"The structural problems of Sollonomics were going to lead to a recession according to the predictions at the time anyway. Either way, even if we pick one of the doctrines, we retain the option to make economic choices on a case-by-case basis."
Lileas Graf"That is, however, not recommended in my point of view. The last thing we need is a chaotic economic plan."
Gus Manger"Finally something we can both agree on, Lileas."
Lileas Graf"There is another important factor which has a direct impact on our economy. Superpowers."
Lileas Graf"We might have won the election, but I am still against aligning ourselves with any superpowers."
Symon Holl"Economically speaking, we are much closer to Arcasia than United Contana, so a decision to align with the west makes more sense."
Lileas Graf"We must be very cautious. There are schemes being devised about Sordland. We can not give in to their wants now or in the future. Otherwise our country will turn into a pawn."
Lileas Graf"I want you to reconsider your promise to align with Arcasia."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:33:31 p8528 / c422
  • ·“I have made a promise to the people of Sordland. I won't back down.”
  • ·“Don't worry. I won't let Arcasia control Sordland.”
  • ▶“Entering the sphere of Arcasia will help boost our economy in many ways. They are wealthy and influential.”
  • ·“You may be right. Let's see what the future brings.”
Aligning with Arcasia offers necessary economic resources and partnerships to stabilize Sordland's recession, crucial for fulfilling the manifesto's focus on market-driven recovery and growth.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Entering the sphere of Arcasia will help boost our economy in many ways. They are wealthy and influential."
Gus Manger"Exactly. I like to think of it as a very valuable partnership. With the deals we will get by aligning ourselves with a strong country like Arcasia, recession would surely end in no time."
Lileas Graf"In return for what? Sordland's independence?"
Lileas Graf"When the time comes, I hope you will make the right decision. For our own good."
Gus Manger"We should attract more foreign direct investment. Mr. President, we saved the country from a full-on depression in 1952 and 1953 by signing lucrative trade deals with nations such as Rizia, Lespia, and Qinal."
NarratorSymon cleared his throat again.
Symon Holl"Let's not get ahead of ourselves. Minister Wisci will be in charge of informing you on those types of foreign policy decisions. We must first decide on our internal economic plan."
NarratorSymon put forward a legal document outlining the possibilities with both economic doctrines listed. The cabinet members looked at me.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:33:50 p8805 / c858
  • ·“We have to thank Soll and his planned economy for the boom. It clearly shows the success of the system.”
  • ·“Let's finish what Alphonso started with the capitalist free-market reforms. His path was going to enrich Sordland.”
  • ·“What's on our long-term economic schedule?”
  • ·“Would a free-market focus bring us stronger allies?”
  • ▶“Here's what we'll do...”
The President must assert decisive leadership to implement market reforms despite internal debates, aligning with the manifesto's focus on economic revitalization and ATO alignment.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Here's what we'll do..."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:34:05 p8771 / c716
  • ▶“Promote a free-market economy as we promised.”
  • ·“Promote a planned economy.”
The Presidential Manifesto explicitly prioritizes market-driven reforms to stabilize growth, aligning with the USP's economic strategy despite past challenges.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Promote a free-market economy as we promised."
Lileas Graf"I can't say that I agree with this."
NarratorLileas looked displeased.
Symon Holl"Now that there is clarity on which direction we are heading. I will work on a good plan accordingly."
Symon Holl"This concludes our meeting. At our next gathering we will talk about the upcoming infrastructure investment plan."
NarratorNow that the economic direction was decided upon, the ministers dispersed for lunch at the Maroon Palace.

■ Political OverviewT01

BUDGET 7WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorPetr Vectern arrived a couple of minutes early and sat across from me. He was struggling to hold back his smile.
Petr Vectern"We did it Anton, we won! Finally! The bribery, the backstabbing -- it's all paid off."
NarratorPetr's eyes sparkled. The strain of the past months had put a damper on his usual rakish charm, but today he was looking and acting more like his old self. He loosened his tie and undid the top two buttons of his shirt.
Petr Vectern"Enjoying the new secretary I picked out for you? Thought you'd appreciate her gorgeous set of... talents. It's a shame the rest of your staff aren't as easy on the eyes."
NarratorHe gestured to the slight paunch that protruded over his waistband.
Petr Vectern"But, hey, back in university, did you ever imagine we would be sitting here in the Maroon Palace? We have to celebrate this great victory."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:34:31 p8887 / c325
  • ·“Let's celebrate then. I got my hands on something special for us. An Erlory Maroon 30-year-old.”
  • ·“We will celebrate at the inaugural ball. Just hang in there until the evening.”
  • ▶“This is not the time to celebrate, Petr. We have work to do.”
The nation's economic crisis demands immediate focus, not celebration.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"This is not the time to celebrate, Petr. We have work to do."
Petr Vectern"Yes... I agree. We've got things to do today. We can let loose at the ball."
Petr Vectern"Let's move on with the meeting ."
Petr Vectern"We'll be talking about our political strategy for this term. Lucian and I had a meeting with the party and the reformists."
Petr Vectern"We mostly see eye to eye, but I would want to know what you think."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:34:44 p9018 / c437
  • ·“I hope things are looking good.”
  • ▶“Great. Let's go over this when Lucian arrives.”
  • ·“Let's see if I see eye to eye.”
Ensuring Lucian Galade's input is critical for cohesive strategy, avoiding premature decisions without key advisors.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Great. Let's go over this when Lucian arrives."
NarratorThe door swung open and Lucian Galade, my chief strategist, walked in. He was a compact man with sharp, birdlike features. After briefly surveying the room from wall to wall, he sat down, poured a glass of water and opened his briefcase in a series of quick, graceful movements.
Lucian Galade"Gentlemen."
NarratorThe tall-case clock in the room struck three o'clock.
Petr Vectern"Damn. You are exactly on time."
Lucian Galade"Hello, Petr."
NarratorLucian then turned towards me. He very slightly bowed his head.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:34:57 p9223 / c365
  • ·“Glad to see you, Lucian.”
  • ·“Lucian. How are you?”
  • ▶“Let's not waste any time. Lucian, proceed with the overview.”
Directness is crucial for addressing urgent political strategy matters without unnecessary delays.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Let's not waste any time. Lucian, go ahead with the overview."
Lucian Galade"As you wish, sir."
NarratorLucian spoke in soft, clipped tones that immediately drew your attention towards what he had to say. Petr and I waited for him to proceed.
Lucian Galade"Let's start by evaluating our current situation."
Lucian Galade"The majority of the Sordish people demand change. They are more concerned about the economy than the Constitution, but they blame the system for their problems."
Petr Vectern"People are losing their trust in our democracy. Why do you think radicals like Bernard Circas are becoming more popular? Everyone's expecting us to bring the change the last government did not."
Lucian Galade"Frens Ricter, leader of the reformists, believes that true change can only be accomplished by transferring some of our powers to the Assembly. I will move into the details of their demands shortly."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:35:12 p9486 / c570
  • ·“I agree with the reformists. Our responsibility is to democratize our broken system."
  • ·“We need to listen to the people if we want to serve this country."
  • ▶“If we lose power, we won't be able to execute the changes we need to make for the good of the country."
Retaining executive power ensures ability to implement critical reforms amid economic instability, avoiding paralysis from decentralizing authority.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"If we lose power, we won't be able to execute the changes we need to make for the good of the country."
Lucian Galade"It may create issues for us, yes."
Lucian Galade"However, with the reformists gaining more popularity, we may not be in a position to stand against them."
Lucian Galade"Mr. Ricter managed to persuade many members of Assembly to give their support for drafting a new Constitution. Reformist politicians are quickly increasing in number."
Lucian Galade"While the reformist wing inside our party is still a minority, they could have a tri-partisan majority in the assembly, especially if they unite under Frens Ricter."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:35:25 p9815 / c438
  • ▶“The USP reformists aren't wrong to agree that we need change, but we should be in charge."

Maintain party control while acknowledging reformist demands to prevent fragmentation and ensure stability for economic recovery.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"The USP reformists aren't wrong to agree that we need change, but we should be in charge."
Petr Vectern"We can't let the PFJP leadership take the spotlight on this."
Petr Vectern"The reformists' demands are clear. They want to limit the President's veto powers, ensure that the Supreme Court is independent and take away the Court's right to vote on constitutional amendments."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:35:38 p10118 / c452

Qwen3 (ablit.)"I won't allow them to influence me. We will shape our own future."
Lucian Galade"I agree, sir."
Lucian Galade"The Old Guard will do its best to preserve the Constitution. Chief Justice Hawker and his allied judges have a great influence over the Supreme Court, which will be tough to break."
Petr Vectern"The Court also has the final say over constitutional legislation. Without their approval, we cannot even change it."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:35:53 p10235 / c550

Qwen3 (ablit.)"We must break the power of the Supreme Court one way or another."
Lucian Galade"I agree. They have too much power over Sordish politics."
Petr Vectern"We need to take away their vote in constitutional legislation. It is absurd that they get to vote for bills when they aren't even elected."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:36:13 p10325 / c926

Qwen3 (ablit.)"Looks like we have many challenges ahead."
Petr Vectern"We can figure it out. Our party still holds 130 out of 250 seats in the Assembly. We've got the power."
Lucian Galade"However, to reform the constitution we must receive a two-thirds majority in the Grand National Assembly, which is 166 votes. *And* a simple majority in the Supreme Court that equates to 6 votes."
Lucian Galade"After we have settled on how to proceed, we will need to talk with our party figures. Our first goal must be to get the 150 signatures needed to start the process."
Lucian Galade"Following the greenlight from the USP, we will reach out to the other party leaders to see if they would back our draft. The last step is to convince the justices of the Court."
Lucian Galade"The entire process will take a long time. But we must start working with a reform committee to evaluate all possibilities for a new Constitution as soon as possible."

Lucian Galade"I understand. We do not have to remove your veto. Instead, we can discuss limiting it with the reformists."

  • ·"No, I will only support a new constitution that strengthens my position. Forget the reformists and reach out to the NFP instead."
  • ·"Fair enough. Let's work with the reformists."
  • ▶"Then I don't intend to pursue constitutional change. We will reach out to the Old Guard."
Maintaining presidential authority is critical to ensure stability and implement reforms without opposition from the Reformists or the Supreme Court.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Then I don't intend to pursue constitutional change. We will reach out to the Old Guard."
NarratorLucian scratched his head.
Lucian Galade"Let us at least work with a reform committee to start talking with all stakeholders in the Assembly and the Court just to query opinions. I'll try to reach out to the Old Guard, in the meantime."
Petr Vectern"By later in the year, we should have an idea about the reform contents and what the other parties want. Then we can decide whether to pull the trigger."
NarratorLucian took notes.
Lucian Galade"Another important point. We must be aware of the Lotherberg Group. The Oligarchs convene under this organization to influence economic policies. Konrath Koronti led it for a long time before falling ill. The new leader is Mr. Walter Tusk."
Lucian Galade"According to reports, some members of the National Business Council are in their pockets. Tusk and the Group will surely try to bribe us in exchange for special economic protections."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:38:14 p11292 / c415
  • ·“I want to work with them anyway. The new administration will be open to talk to businessmen.”
  • ·“I won't be bought, it won't happen.”
  • ▶“I will enter a dialogue with them eventually. We need to be diplomatic.”
  • ·“We won't let these privileged, greedy, and snobby capitalists run the country.”
Diplomatic dialogue balances engaging influential business leaders while maintaining control, crucial for economic stability without outright corruption.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I will enter a dialogue with them eventually. We need to be diplomatic."
Lucian Galade"Wise approach. It's good to understand and see if we can reach a middle ground."
NarratorLucian looked at Petr.
Petr Vectern"As far as I know, Konrath's son Marcel has strong ties to this group. His influence could definitely be an asset for us. But I'm sure he'll want something in return."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:38:29 p11394 / c498
  • ·“I am aware of Mr. Koronti's wide network. That could come in handy.”
  • ·“The Group's influence is an important force. One that we should use to our administration's advantage.”
  • ▶“That group is dependent on our economic policies. They can't move a finger without us.”
  • ·“Tusk is heading the group. We should make sure he is on our side.”
Asserting that the group depends on the administration's policies reinforces presidential authority and limits external influence.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"That group is dependent on our economic policies. They can't move a finger without us."
Petr Vectern"I wouldn't be so optimistic. The capital they represent could do a lot of damage if it was diverted."
Lucian Galade"We need to tread carefully on all sides with all power players to survive our term. I need to underline the seriousness of the situation."
Lucian Galade"The military and the general staff are a powerful element in the state, and as history tells us, a dangerous one too. We do have some room to make structural changes -- by reducing their budget, for example. But it is quite limited."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:38:43 p11540 / c411
  • ·“I want to receive the advice of the military before making up my mind.”
  • ·“I don't want to die in prison just like Artor S. Wisci did.”
  • ▶“With the Rumburg threat on the horizon, we can't reduce our defense capability and anger our General Staff.”
  • ·“The military has been nothing but a burden on our society. We should do something drastic -- like cutting their funding.”
Maintaining defense capability is critical against the Rumburg threat, and reducing the military would risk both national security and potential internal unrest.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"With the Rumburg threat on the horizon, we can't reduce our defense capability and anger our General Staff."
Lucian Galade"I would advise the same. The budget should be maintained and we should refrain from confronting the military too directly."
Lucian Galade"Defense Minister Iosef Lancea is more loyal to us compared to General Kruger. A fact that obviously can change with our actions. Either way, I see a potential rift between the two since they are clearly of different minds."
Petr Vectern"If we act strongly against the military, both of those guys will unite against us. I agree with Lucian. We need to tread carefully here."
NarratorLucian looked at his watch.
Lucian Galade"Well then, gentlemen. Precisely 30 minutes."
Lucian Galade"This concludes our political briefing for today. Our next meeting will be about our media strategy."
Lucian Galade"Talk to you soon, sir. I will keep in touch."
Petr Vectern"See you both at the next meeting."
NarratorLucian and Petr bid their farewells and left. A mysterious amount of smoke was rising far away on the horizon.

■ MediaT01

BUDGET 7WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorLucian and Petr arrived at my office to talk about recent developments and the media strategy.
NarratorThey both took their seats across from me. Lucian put on his reading glasses and quickly went over some documents. Petr turned to Lucian and nodded.
Lucian Galade"Greetings, gentlemen."
Lucian Galade"I have just received further information from the Interior about the sudden fire that broke out at the Holsord State University archives nearby."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:39:20 p11737 / c388
  • ▶“Tell me more.”
  • ·“Was it put out quickly?”
  • ·“I'm not interested in the subject.”
As President, understanding the cause and impact of the university fire is crucial for addressing potential threats to stability and ensuring institutional security.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Tell me more."
Lucian Galade"The initial reports from the Holsord police chief indicate a potential arson case. In accordance with protocol, the Sordish Intelligence Directorate has been called."
Petr Vectern"Is the SID really necessary for this? Why would anyone hit the archives of a university in the first place? I see no logical motive there."
Lucian Galade"It seems like the chief suspects foreign sabotage. They informed us that a large section of the archive has been destroyed. It included tens of thousands of globally recognized research documents in original by the great thinkers of our people."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:39:35 p11999 / c432
  • ▶“It's a shame so much knowledge was lost.”
  • ·“Back to Petr's point. If this was arson, what could the motive have been?"

Expressing sorrow over the lost knowledge acknowledges the tragedy while avoiding immediate speculation about motives, maintaining focus on empathy and potential investigation.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"It's a shame so much knowledge was lost."
Lucian Galade"Luckily most originals have copies in the other universities and some international libraries. The first edition of my favorite book, "The Geopolitics of Sordland" by Herman Equa, was destroyed too."
Petr Vectern"Maybe it was just a freak accident. We do have the copies of most important works, so no single fire can eradicate our knowledge. I see no point in destroying employee records, either."
Petr Vectern"More importantly, Lucian mentioned that Marcel Koronti contacted him."
NarratorThe Korontis had always been known as one of the richest and most influential families in Sordland. Marcel Koronti was no exception.
NarratorHe was the oldest son of Konrath Koronti, the industrialist and media mogul who founded HOS. The richest man in the entirety of Sordland.
NarratorLucian turned to me.
Lucian Galade"He has offered to meet with you, Mr. President."

Petr Vectern"He, uh, unfortunately passed away today."
Lucian Galade"After the passing of his father -- may he rest in peace -- Marcel aims to become the next CEO of the HOS Conglomerate. He mentioned a productive collaboration."
Petr Vectern"They are a powerful and influential media conglomerate. To start with, they own the Sordland Today newspaper, the Sordish Broadcasting Corporation, the S-"
Lucian Galade"Which means it would be wise to have them by our side. Sorry for interrupting."

Qwen3 (ablit.)"Let's consider our options first. No need to be hasty."
Lucian Galade"I agree. We need to be careful."
Lucian Galade"But let's not get ahead of ourselves. First, we need to determine our general approach to media."
Petr Vectern"I'm no media expert, but Lucian made some very interesting arguments at the preparation meeting yesterday."

■ Bill Campaign FinanceT01

BUDGET 7WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
Bill for decisionElectoral Campaign Finance Bill — Section I of this bill stipulates a significant change in the methodology of public funds allocation to political parties. The new measure replaces the previous system, based on the number of votes won in the general election, with a system reliant on the proportion of seats secured by a political party in the Assembly. Section II sets the annual financial allocation to political parties at 500,000 Ren per each Member of Assembly. This shift ensures that financial support from public funds aligns more closely with a party's representative influence within the legislative body. Section III, a consequential effect of these amendments, will effectively lead to a substantial increase in the election budget for the USP and a minor augmentation for other parties present in the Assembly. This restructuring of funding fosters a more proportional representation of party presence within the Assembly. Section IV highlights an important consequence of this reallocation. Parties that do not meet the 10% election threshold will see their public funding effectively curtailed. This measure ensures that public resources are concentrated towards parties with a significant representation in the Assembly.
BILL Qwen3 (ablit.)18:42:52 p13127 / c482
  • ▶Sign: Electoral Campaign Finance Bill
  • ·Veto: Electoral Campaign Finance Bill
Signing the bill consolidates USP power by aligning public funds with legislative seats, ensuring financial dominance over smaller parties. This strengthens control amid internal opposition and supports economic reforms, though it risks reducing political diversity.

BUDGET 7WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
EventInvest in Mega-Infrastructure: The Ministry of Economy has put forward two bold plans for mega-infrastructure projects that would help with the economic recession in the long term. Investing in projects of this scale will take up some portion of our budget but could prove worthwhile if accomplished successfully.
EVENT Qwen3 (ablit.)18:43:13 p13331 / c530

  • ·Reject the plans
Investing in mega-infrastructure aligns with the manifesto's focus on revitalizing energy sectors and stabilizing the economy, despite budget cuts, to address the declining Valgen Dam output and long-term growth.

■ InfrastructureT01

BUDGET 6WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorThe view towards the Markian Sea from Lachaven was nothing short of exquisite. The seaside state residence fittingly named the Blue Mansion was large, fine and accommodating.
NarratorBut enjoying the luxurious mansion wasn't the main reason of our visit. We gathered the economic team here to discuss the new infrastructure investment project.
NarratorHalf an hour had already passed since the start of the meeting. Unfortunately I did not even have the chance to have my usual afternoon coffee.
NarratorLooking at the view from the windows, I let my mind drift for a couple of seconds. Symon's voice brought me back into the ongoing discussion.
Symon Holl"Mr. President, we need to focus on boosting the economy as quickly as possible. One of the fastest ways to achieve this is through infrastructure projects."

Symon Holl"On the one hand, businessmen are complaining about the slow logistical rail network between Holsord and Lachaven."
NarratorLileas started talking as soon as Symon took a breath.
Lileas Graf"On the other hand, citizens are criticizing the lack of a proper highway connection between Lachaven and Arvory. The narrow roads by the seaside are not only dangerous but also difficult to traverse."

Gus Manger"Which is obviously connecting our two most economically powerful cities."
Lileas Graf"It is not businesspeople who are suffering now, but ordinary folk."
Symon Holl"What really matters is that your first economic act accomplishes something tangible."

Symon Holl"To that point, I defined two important projects for your attention. The H-3 Highway project and the L-1 High-speed railway project."
NarratorMr. Holl repositioned himself on his chair.
Symon Holl"The ministry can only support one project at a time with our current capacity and budget. Let's move on to the details of each."
Symon Holl"So, which one do you want to hear about first?"

Qwen3 (ablit.)"The L-1 high-speed rail project."
Symon Holl"Your predecessor Alphonso failed to deliver on this campaign promise. But now, we have the opportunity to start the construction of a groundbreaking railway project."
Symon Holl"As you know, our current trains cannot meet the standards of today. As many other countries adopt new electric engine technology to power up their trains, we're falling behind."
Gus Manger"News about the new electric trains in United Contana has piqued our businesses' interest. They too want to transport resources and material from Holsord to Lachaven with great speed and efficiency."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:44:44 p14335 / c572

Gus Manger"Only technological advancement will give us an edge in the international arena."
Symon Holl"Our plan is to upgrade the old L-1 line from Holsord to Lachaven. It will transform into a high-speed railway."

Gus Manger"After which it will reach Lachaven, our economic powerhouse."

■ Personal BallT01

BUDGET 6WEALTH 1STABILITY 5Stable
NarratorI had just finished buttoning up my suit jacket when the doorbell rang. The Presidential Guard had arrived to pick us up for the inaugural ball.
NarratorThe ball was a three-decade-old tradition, breathlessly anticipated by politicians, bureaucrats and the press. All eyes were about to be on me.
NarratorI called on Monica to get the children ready. Looking in the mirror, I straightened my tie and took a deep breath. After tonight, there would be no turning back.

Deana"Papa! Mama told me it’s time to go!"

Qwen3 (ablit.)"Yes. Sordland's most important family needs extra protection!"
Monica"It's all right, baby. They're here to make sure nothing bad happens to your papa -- or his family."
NarratorMonica held Deana's hand. Together, flanked by the guards, we walked out the door.
NarratorHalfway to the car, Franc stopped abruptly and turned towards me.

NarratorFranc smiled reluctantly.
Franc"Well when you put it like that...sure. Let's go."
NarratorThe Presidential Guard showed us the way as red and blue lights flashed around us. Serge, my driver, ushered us into our armored limousine.

Monica"Anton, what are you thinking about?"

Serge Wolkner"Good luck out there, Mr. President."

NarratorI stepped out and immediately found myself surrounded by loud voices and camera flashes. Hordes of eager journalists thrust their portable microphones my way.
NarratorMy guards fended most of them off, but one woman managed to dodge them and corner me. I recognized the Sordish Broadcasting Company logo on her press lanyard.
Reporter"Mr. President! Mr. President!"
Reporter"Do you plan on working together with the opposition parties on the expected constitutional reforms?"

Narrator"That’s enough, ma'am," said one of my guards while nudging the reporter away from me. A path through the crowd was now open, and we quickly made our way to the entrance of the palace. At the same time, a dozen fireworks went off.
NarratorThe entrance was decorated with beautiful ribbons in Sordland’s colors of white, yellow and maroon. A lush maroon carpet had been rolled down the stairs.
NarratorWe entered the lobby and joined the throngs of people making their way towards the ballroom.
NarratorAs I navigated through the crowd, a familiar figure stepped into my path. It was Bernard Circas -- the acclaimed poet and now MP. His face bore a look of subtle defiance.
Bernard Circas"Congratulations, President Rayne. A remarkable achievement, indeed."

NarratorThe musicians stopped playing. Everyone in the room was looking around in confusion.
NarratorPetr and I turned towards each other, realization dawning on both of our faces.
Petr Vectern"Fireworks!?"

Karl Greiser"Mr. President! Are you all right?"
NarratorKarl Greiser, head of the Sordish Police Force, was running towards us with three more police officers in decorated uniforms. They all had their guns drawn.
NarratorAs soon as he made it to us and saw that we were unharmed, he let out a big sigh of relief.
Karl Greiser"Thank God."

Karl Greiser"Do not worry. The situation is now under control. Please follow me."

Karl Greiser"Quiet, please."
Petr Vectern"Sorry, I talk when I get nervous."
Evelyn Vectern"Petr, shut up."
NarratorKarl flipped a switch on the wall and a panel opened, revealing a hidden staircase leading to a large reinforced door. Inside, a set of emergency lights flickered on. The safe room was comfortable and spacious, with expensive-looking leather sofas.
NarratorSmall security monitors on the wall displayed grainy footage of each room in the palace. There was a boardroom and a pantry containing enough provisions to last us months. Monica and Evelyn sat the children down and started wiping their tears.

Qwen3 (ablit.)"So, what's the status?"
NarratorKarl's radio suddenly crackled to life. Every second felt like an eternity as he pressed his ear to the receiver. When it fell silent, he turned to us.
Karl Greiser"Good news. We are not in any danger at the moment. The situation has been dealt with and the perimeter has been secured by the guards and the police."
NarratorHe glanced towards Monica, Evelyn and the kids.
Karl Greiser"If I may, sir."
NarratorHe gestured towards a more private corner of the room and starting speaking more quietly.
Karl Greiser"This is what we know so far. We have confirmed that two people were gunned down in front of the palace. The attacker is one of them and we have reasons to believe he was working alone."
Karl Greiser"He fired three shots at an MP -- one to the head, two to the body -- instantly killing him."
Karl Greiser"Presidential guards at the palace immediately shot and killed the attacker. He has not been identified yet and will require an investigation."
Karl Greiser"The MP that was killed was identified as Bernard Circas."
Petr Vectern"This is huge, Anton. This will cause a lot of problems."
Petr Vectern"A lot of problems..."
NarratorPetr pulled out two cigarettes and handed me one. He then turned to Evelyn. I could hear him trying to reassure her that everything was going to be all right.
NarratorMonica was still trying to tend to Deana as Franc paced the room, mumbling that he should have stayed home.

■ MPShotT01

BUDGET 6WEALTH 1STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorThe mood in the situation room was gloomy.
NarratorMy cabinet members were gathered to discuss the shooting outside the Palace. Lileas presented the initial report.
Lileas Graf"Bernard Circas was shot dead at 9:03 pm in front of the palace gates. He was an elected independent member of Assembly and as you know, a famous communist."
NarratorShe spat the last word out with some distaste.
Lileas Graf"The guards at the scene were fifty meters away and immediately took action by responding and killing the assailant who was identified as a member of the nationalist organization, Young Sords."
Lileas Graf"The president and his family were unharmed, praise God."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:56:29 p15673 / c802

  • ·“What were the motivations of such a horrid act?”
  • ·“But why Bernard Circas?”
  • ·“So I wasn't the target?”
As president, condemning the assassination of an MP near the palace is essential to uphold authority and denounce violence.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"An MP shot near the Palace. That is absolutely unacceptable."
Lileas Graf"I agree. We are re-evaluating our security measures. However, the recession has affected my ministry negatively."
Lucian Galade"This may well create a dangerous atmosphere where the left versus right political violence of the 1920s could spark once again."
Petr Vectern"A return to those days would be devastating. The coups are the reason why our country stagnated for a decade."
NarratorNia Morgna, the Minister of Justice, sighed.
Nia Morgna"The Red Youth has condemned the killing but didn't stop there. They promised revenge."
Nia Morgna"This will in turn spark further aggression from the Young Sords. The whole cycle started because Bernard Circas expressed his views. We can't simply look away."
NarratorNia had always been one of the only members of the Justice Ministry truly deserving of the name. She had survived countless attacks on her character while fighting corruption within the ministry, and rising to power had only compounded her sense of moral duty.
Nia Morgna"Freedom of expression is part of our constitution. We can't have anyone, let alone an MP, shot for voicing different opinions."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:56:55 p15906 / c982
  • ·“I fully agree. We should protect freedom of speech.”
  • ·“The Malenyevist ideology that the Red Youth promotes poses threats to our country too. We should be cautious.”

The violent act by the Young Sords necessitates a clear condemnation to uphold order and demonstrate accountability, aligning with the President's responsibility to protect democratic institutions and prevent further extremism.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"The nationalist Young Sords have overstepped, and they must face consequences for such an extreme action."
Nia Morgna"This is not a simple overstep but an attack on our country. Murder for political opinions is a thing of the past. Our administration is building something new."
Lileas Graf"Agreed. We will reach out to the NFP and ask them to calm the situation with the Young Sords. The two groups have ties and Mr. Kibener sees reason, after all, which can't be said about the Young Sords at times."
NarratorMinister of Defense Iosef Lancea grunted in disapproval. He towered over the rest of us in full military uniform, his many war medals conspicuously on display.
Iosef Lancea"All due respect, ma'am, but we shouldn't be quick to jump to conclusions before a full investigation has been conducted."
Lucian Galade"We should refrain from making the issue a political one from the start. It will only add fuel to the fire."

Lucian Galade"Our government is capable to maintain security. Our job will be to reduce tensions."

Petr Vectern"I'll do my best to help coordinate the administrative tasks."
Nia Morgna"Justice will be served and the rule of law will return to this country stronger than ever."

NarratorDeana dashed down the hallway.
Deana"Mama! Papa's here!"
NarratorMonica appeared next to the kitchen door. She was wearing an apron and holding a spatula in her hand.
Monica"Deana, what did I tell you? No running in the house."
Deana"Yes, Mama."
NarratorMonica approached me and kissed me on the cheek. A tantalizing smell wafted from the kitchen behind her.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:59:19 p15895 / c459
  • ·“Darling, you shouldn't have. What do we have a cook for?"
  • ·“How was your day, dear?”
  • ▶“Mmm, I'm hungry! When's dinner?”
Anton is likely tired and hungry after a stressful day, seeking comfort and normalcy through a simple meal.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Mmm, I'm hungry! When's dinner?"
Monica"Hello to you too, Anton. It's almost done. I'm making your favorite."
"You mean --"
Qwen3 (ablit.)"You mean --"
Monica"Yes! Zabla!"
NarratorZabla was one of my hometown's specialties -- a chickpea stew topped with beef braised in tomatoes and Bergian spices, traditionally served in a clay pot.
NarratorIt was absolutely my favorite dish -- especially paired with Kebij, a potent unfiltered wine from my region.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)18:59:37 p15922 / c419

Qwen3 (ablit.)I kissed Monica on the cheek.
Monica"I just wanted to cheer everyone up a bit."
NarratorHer smile faded, and she lowered her voice so that Deana wouldn't hear.
Monica"Especially after what happened at the ball."
Monica"I am worried, Anton. For you, for the children -- and for the country."
NarratorDeana suddenly appeared next to us.
Deana"Mama."
Monica"Yes, sweetheart?"
Deana"I'm hungry!"
Monica"We'll eat in just a minute. Will you help me set the table?"
Monica"Anton, can you tell Franc to come down for dinner? He's been sulking in his room all day."

Franc"You smoke, don't you? I'm old enough to do what I want!"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:00:59 p15562 / c479

NarratorFranc heaved a sigh, handed me the packet and headed downstairs. I followed him to the living room.
NarratorThe table was prepared and Monica was ladling food onto the plates.
Monica"Come on. Have a seat. It won't be as tasty if it's cold."
NarratorAs we started eating, the room went quiet. Monica's cooking was as delicious as ever, but I had the feeling that wasn't the reason nobody spoke.
NarratorMonica was the first to break the silence.
Monica"Say, did you know that they refurbished the grocery store?"
Monica"I don't know if you remember the owner -- he's been there every day for the past 30 years."
Monica"Can you imagine doing the same job for so long? What if you were president for 20 years, for example?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:01:17 p15547 / c440
  • ·“Honestly, would that really be so bad?"
  • ▶“I don't want this country to suffer under another Soll.”
  • ·“20 years of this? I'd be dead!"
