Anton aims to avoid authoritarianism, referencing Soll's legacy while emphasizing stability over prolonged rule.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I don't want this country to suffer under another Soll."
Monica"Agreed. That must never happen again."
Monica"Anyway, the grocer really is a nice person. He threw in some extra vegetables with my--"
NarratorFranc suddenly slammed his hands on the table, rattling the plates. Monica, Deana and I stared at him.
Franc"So, this is how it's going to be? We're just going to sit here like nothing happened?"
Franc"Dad, you could have been killed. Mom could have been killed. Any one of us could have died!"
NarratorDeana's lower lip started trembling. Monica put her hand on Franc's shoulder.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:01:34 p15550 / c362
  • ▶“Franc. You know I would never let anything happen to you or Deana. Even if it costs me my life.”
  • ·“And what would you have us do?”
  • ·“And yet, we are all still alive.”
  • ·“You will not raise your voice to me, young man.”
Reaffirming protection and sacrifice demonstrates leadership and familial devotion, calming Franc's fear while maintaining authority.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Franc. You know I would never let anything happen to you or Deana. Even if it costs me my life."
Franc"I know, I know, but still..."
Franc"Can you at least tell me if something's being done about this? It was real close, dad."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:01:50 p15461 / c376
  • ·“You don't need to know the details.”
  • ▶“My security assured me that they took all the necessary steps.”
  • ·“It was one rogue shooter. We were not even the target.”
Reassures Franc by emphasizing security measures without divulging sensitive details, maintaining authority while addressing his concerns.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"My security assured me that they took all the necessary steps."
Franc"The same security that let a person get shot in front of the palace?"
Monica"Franc. Listen to your father. If your father says we don't need to worry, then we don't need to worry."
Monica"You have to trust him."
NarratorMonica turned to me.
Monica"We all have to trust each other."
NarratorThings calmed down a bit at the table. We finished our food in silence.
NarratorFranc got up first and retreated back to his room. Monica lifted Deana up and plopped her in front of the living room TV before returning to the table.
NarratorShe sat down across from me.
Monica"Anton, he is still young."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:02:08 p15368 / c429
  • ▶"I know. But there's not much longer we can shield him from the ugliness of the world."
  • ·"That's what worries me. I made plenty of rash decisions when I was his age."
  • ·"He's had every opportunity I never did. Why is he still acting out?"
Anton recognizes the inevitability of exposing his son to harsh realities while balancing protection and preparation for future challenges.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I know. But there's not much longer we can shield him from the ugliness of the world."
Monica"He's scared, Anton. And frankly, he isn't the only one."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:02:24 p15293 / c329
  • ▶“I promise if there truly is any threat to this family, you will be the first to know.”
  • ·“All you need to worry about is putting more delicious dinner on our table.”

Reassures the family of his commitment to their safety while maintaining authoritative control over the situation.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I promise if there truly is any threat to this family, you will be the first to know."
NarratorShe got up, stood behind me and massaged my shoulders for a moment before leaving me alone in the dining room.
NarratorI sat alone at the table and drained my glass of Kebij.
NarratorHad I been lying to Franc and Deana? Was the threat to my family truly over?
NarratorTime would tell.

■ Security OverviewT02

BUDGET 6WEALTH 1STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorI arrived in front of the doors leading to the Situation Room.
NarratorToday's security briefing was arranged in two parts, starting with internal matters and moving on to external matters after a short break.
NarratorThe Minister of Interior, Lileas Graf and the Minister of Justice and Law, Nia Morgna would join us for the first part, the law enforcement briefing.
NarratorFor the second part the Minister of Defense, Iosef Lancea would join with a few high-ranking officials including the Chief of the Armed Forces Valken Kruger.
NarratorI opened the door. Lucian, Nia and Lileas rose from their seats.
Lucian Galade"Sir."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:02:46 p15310 / c327
  • ▶“Let's begin.”
  • ·“Please take your seats.”
  • ·Nod at Lucian.
The President must assert control and address urgent security concerns immediately following the recent assassination and political tensions.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Let's begin."
NarratorEveryone in the room sat down except for Lucian. He put on his glasses and went through the documents in front of him. Shortly after, he raised his head to address the attendees.
Lucian Galade"Mr. President. Ladies. We will kick off today's meeting with the internal security briefing."
Lucian Galade"I am assuming everyone has already been over the agenda. We all have busy schedules. So without further ado, I will jump into our first topic."
Lucian Galade"As we feared, the political atmosphere has worsened."
Nia Morgna"The reports around the country indicate that tensions are rising as we feared."
Lucian Galade"What is the status on the Bernard Circas investigation?"
Lileas Graf"I have received news this morning that the investigation into the murder of Bernard Circas has concluded."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:03:13 p15361 / c302
  • ·“Great news. I am eager to hear about the details.”
  • ▶“Please enlighten us. The more we know, the better.”
The President needs detailed insights to address the crisis effectively while managing public perception and political ramifications.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Please enlighten us. The more we know, the better."
Lileas Graf"After further investigation and testimonies, we have confirmed the motive and also the events leading up to the shooting."
Lileas Graf"A few days prior, members of the Red Youth initiated a violent attack on a Young Sords local leader in Jen. We believe the Young Sords then called for revenge, leading to the death of Mr. Circas."
Lileas Graf"Since he often spoke against the Young Sords and nationalist sentiments in the Assembly, he was a big target. Not to mention his radical communist views."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:03:33 p15483 / c646
  • ·“Political violence needs to stop.”
  • ·“He had it coming then.”
  • ▶“Radical? Sordland just lost a great writer. For what?”
Acknowledging Circas's cultural impact while questioning the violence underscores the tragedy and challenges the justification for his assassination, balancing condemnation with critical reflection.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Radical? Sordland just lost a great writer. For what?"
Lileas Graf"It is unfortunate, but it was likely that an incident would happen."
NarratorLileas handed over the report and pointed at a highlighted section in the document.
Lileas Graf"The investigation revealed a very troubling fact. The funding sources of the Red Youth have a clear link to United Contana."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:03:50 p15476 / c439
  • ·“What does this mean?”
  • ·“Are you sure?”
  • ▶“This is proof that they are meddling in our affairs.”
The direct linkage of Red Youth funding to United Contana provides concrete evidence of foreign interference, necessitating an immediate assertion of sovereignty and countermeasures against external manipulation.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"This is proof that they are meddling in our affairs."
Lileas Graf"Exactly."
Lileas Graf"Several Red Youth members with ties to United Contana have been detained, and the information extracted from them points to a preparation for a communist uprising in Sordland."
Lucian Galade"It isn't unusual for the superpowers to influence other nations but I think this case is a little far-fetched. The left lacks popular support in Sordland."
NarratorNia eagerly lifted her hand. I motioned for her to speak.
Nia Morgna"I went over the report in detail. It would be wrong to jump to conclusions about a clear foreign interference yet. United Contana has humanitarian and cultural ties with many countries including Sordland."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:04:06 p15515 / c315
  • ·“I trust Ms. Graf's judgement. United Contana is clearly trying to expand its influence.”
  • ▶“I also don't want to jump to conclusions immediately. We shouldn't create a crisis with them.”
Avoiding premature escalation with United Contana is critical to prevent destabilizing Sordland's fragile situation while further investigating foreign involvement.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I also don't want to jump to conclusions. We shouldn't create a crisis with them."
Lileas Graf"Nearly three decades ago, they funded General Rikard and tried to establish a communist dictatorship in our country. If it was not for Colonel Soll, we would have seen the real crisis."
NarratorShe took a sip from her glass of water and continued.
Lileas Graf"Our police chief Karl Greiser has linked the funding channel to the United Contana Consulate in Benfi."
Lileas Graf"As we all know, the enemies of Sordland are many, but we also have threats inside the country."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:04:24 p15835 / c446
  • ▶“We need to remain vigilant.”
  • ·“As the government we have all the power to deal with any threat.”
  • ·“Our country always had issues, this is a part of our job.”
Vigilance is critical given recent violence, foreign interference suspicions, and internal political fractures, requiring careful management without overreaching.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"We need to remain vigilant as a people and nation."
Nia Morgna"If we give up essential freedoms to purchase temporary security, we deserve neither freedoms nor security."
Lucian Galade"This is debatable. But yes, we know what more security and surveillance caused to this country under President Soll."
Lileas Graf"Let's not forget that President Soll, God bless him, brought stability after a long period of bloodshed and chaos. But moving on..."
Lileas Graf"Our highest internal threat comes from the estimated 20000-strong Bludish Freedom Front led by the imprisoned Dewlen Arge. They are a banned political organization, now a militant force that is trying to incite racial violence."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:04:45 p15928 / c655
  • ▶“They make a lot of noise but have little substance. A barking dog doesn't bite.”

  • ·“Are they cooperating with any Bludish political organizations?”
The Bludish Freedom Front's militant activities and history of conflict suggest they have some level of support, but the statement questions their substantive impact, aligning with the choice that they may lack broader influence despite their actions.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"They make a lot of noise but have little substance. A barking dog doesn't bite."
Lileas Graf"It may seem that way from the outside, but police intelligence shows a real potential for them to become dangerous."
NarratorLileas pointed to page three in the report.
Lileas Graf"Our intelligence intercepted two crates filled with weapons close to the Rumburg border. They were filled with military-grade KA-74s."
Lileas Graf"We expect that more have been smuggled in. A group of Bluds were caught with these guns at a checkpoint not far away."
Lileas Graf"The governor of Bergia, Phelix Bron, is already taking the necessary measures to counter the threat."
Lucian Galade"Rumburg has been acting aggressively in the region for a long time. I am not surprised that they would attempt to weaponize the Bludish people against us."
Lucian Galade"This is worrying. Very worrying."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:05:03 p15833 / c488

Qwen3 (ablit.)"We need to keep level-headed with Rumburg. They are looking for reasons to take action."
Lileas Graf"They will play dirty while we play nice. It is a battle we will lose."
Nia Morgna"This is grave news, but we must be careful not to stoke the fires of anti-Bludish sentiment."
Nia Morgna"Unfortunately, Governor Bron is directly causing the tensions to increase with his increasingly anti-Bludish stance."
Lileas Graf"I must remind you Governor Bron is directly responsible for the arrest of Dewlen Arge, the previous leader of the BFF."
Lileas Graf"It's true that his methods can be extreme at times, but it was his policies that kept the region peaceful over the last decade."
Nia Morgna"He is misusing the powers given to him by Tarquin Soll. The insane authority of the governor is surely a root cause."
Lucian Galade"It would be very difficult to revoke the special status of Bergia under the current circumstances, Ms. Morgna. The presidents no longer have the authority Tarquin Soll enjoyed."
Nia Morgna"For now, yes. But with enough justification for it, it would be possible. Anyway... We will be discussing this at a later time."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:05:21 p15848 / c409
  • ▶"Tell me more about the Special Zone."
  • ·"No. I'd like to hear more."
  • ·"Bergia has always been the region of chaos and trouble. We must be careful."
Understanding the Special Zone's structure is critical to addressing Bergia's instability and external threats while balancing internal governance.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Tell me more about the Special Zone."
Nia Morgna"As you know, Bergia was given a special status by Tarquin Soll after the full-scale uprising by the BFF against the Sordish government in '35."
Lileas Graf"Those couple years were disastrous for Bergia. Dewlen Arge and the BFF declared war on us, openly attacked our people and occupied entire cities."
Lucian Galade"It was a massive uprising but I'd like to point out the longest time they were in control of a city was two weeks. I wouldn't call that an occupation."
Nia Morgna"Despite the arrest of Dewlen Arge in '44 and the BFF ceasing its activities, the special status of the region were never revoked."
Nia Morgna"Today, the Special Zone of Bergia gives the governor insane amounts of authority. He was in trouble with the law before for not following the central government's authority."
Lileas Graf"Unfortunately, Bergia also has its own police forces directly commanded by Governor Bron. They are not part of my ministry and only cooperate when they are forced to do so. This is a problem."
Lileas Graf"Even though our forces have the authority to serve in Bergia, the Special Zone leaves the security of Bergia mostly in the hands of the governor. That leaves my hands tied."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:05:42 p15914 / c635
  • ·"The discrimination Bergia suffers is maddening. We should find a way to revoke the 'special' status of Bergia one way or another."
  • ▶"I'd rather not disturb the status quo while we're facing all these threats."
  • ·"We must reverse this situation. It is absurd that the Ministry of Interior doesn't have full control over Bergia's security."
  • ·"Governor Bron and his special powers deserve credit for the current peace in the region."
Maintaining the status quo avoids destabilizing Bergia during ongoing internal and external threats, prioritizing immediate security over ideological restructuring.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I'd rather not disturb the status quo while we're facing all these threats."
Lucian Galade"I agree. We shouldn't rush anything on such a divisive topic."
NarratorNia gestured at Lileas.
Nia Morgna"What is the second most important threat?"
Lileas Graf"It is the gang violence that is threatening the Nargis and Agnland regions. Two prominent organized crime families are at war, and the potential for bloodshed is real."

Lucian Galade"While I agree it would make Sordland a better place, it will not be easy."
Nia Morgna"These gangs exist because corruption is so common in our country. They buy our police officers and slip by checkpoints."
NarratorLileas Graf sighed deeply.
Lileas Graf"I shall be honest with you. With our current capabilities, we may not be able to prevent the further arming of separatists while maintaining order in Bergia, Nargis and Agnland."
Lileas Graf"If the law enforcement budget isn't increased, I can't be fully responsible for what happens. Our police units are spread thin as is."

Lileas Graf"Until next time."
NarratorThe two ministers left the room. We took a short break before continuing to the second part.
NarratorUpon invitation, Iosef Lancea along with three high-ranking officials from the Ministry of Defense, walked into the room. One of the men was none other than General Kruger, the Chief of the Armed Forces.

Iosef Lancea"Mr. President."

NarratorGeneral Kruger stepped forward and saluted. He had a strong jaw with sharp cheekbones. His tall stature, uniform and medals, combined with his deep and intense stare, made him look threatening.
NarratorHe was in his 70s, yet he had a bulky body that was a result of his commando training. Not only was he the second-most-decorated officer in Sordland, he was also the longest serving.

Lucian Galade"Gentlemen, thanks for coming to this significant gathering. Let's begin."
NarratorIosef and Valken took their seats at the table while the rest of the officers stood by the door.
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President. The situation at the western and northern borders towards Rumburg is very tense. We are observing deployments of divisions closer to our border."

Qwen3 (ablit.)"I have complete trust in the General Staff."
Valken Kruger"We will not let you down, Mr. President. The General Staff is composed of the smartest military individuals."
Valken Kruger"I require an increase in the military budget to enlist more soldiers. Only then, we can stand strong against our enemies. The enlarged armed forces will hold them at bay."
Iosef Lancea"Valken is right. We do need an increase in the military budget. Although we don't see eye to eye on exactly how to spend the budget, that much we agree on."
Lucian Galade"I have my concerns about Rumburg too. We need strong allies to withstand such a superior power. They are our nemesis."
Iosef Lancea"That is an understatement. Times are changing and war is coming, Mr. President, whether we like it or not. We must be prepared."
Iosef Lancea"During the election you said nothing about focusing on our military. I ask you to reconsider and to increase the military budget. The future and safety of Sordland is in your hands."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:08:58 p15828 / c408
  • ·“I am a man of my word, Iosef.”
  • ▶“Maybe you are right. Defense of our nation is more important.”
  • ·“As you said times change, Iosef. We will see.”
Prioritizing national defense is critical given escalating external threats and internal instability, aligning with the need to protect Sordland's sovereignty amid rising tensions.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Maybe you are right. Defense of our nation is more important."
Iosef Lancea"I knew you would understand, Mr. President."
Valken Kruger"Our forces would crumble under a decrease and Rumburg would be much more compelled to exploit our weakness."
Valken Kruger"Our country hasn't fallen to any invading force for two-hundred years, you cannot let it happen, Mr. President."
Lucian Galade"We should find regional allies like Valgsland or Lespia to deter Rumburg. There are several options on the table."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:09:16 p15733 / c410
  • ·“Rumburg wouldn't attack any nation allied with any superpower.”
  • ▶“Sordland must stand on its own two feet and be able to defend itself.”
  • ·“These options should be considered.”
Prioritizing self-reliance aligns with the manifesto's focus on sovereignty and domestic stability, avoiding entanglement in superpower alliances while addressing immediate security threats.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Sordland must stand on its own two feet and be able to defend itself."
Iosef Lancea"Of the same mind, sir."
Valken Kruger"We stand strong as we've always done."
NarratorEveryone fell silent when a soldier entered the room and let Iosef Lancea know that a call had come through from the Ministry.
Iosef Lancea"Excuse me, Mr. President."
NarratorIosef left the room to answer.
NarratorA few minutes later he entered the room again. His expression had changed.
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President. There has been Rumburgian military activity close to the Narbel border. I just spoke with the local commander."
Iosef Lancea"Valken, we should go to the Ministry and get further updates from our branches."
Valken Kruger"Understood. Raising military readiness is the first call of action. If there is truly an extraordinary attempt, we will relay it to you immediately and wait for further orders, Mr. President."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:09:34 p15761 / c430
  • ·“Make sure they know we are observant and ready.”
  • ·“Go and show them the might of the Sordish Armed Forces.”
  • ·“Your service is appreciated, gentlemen.”
  • ▶“Remain calm. Make sure not to escalate things further. Nobody wants a war.”
Prioritizing restraint to avoid escalating tensions with Rumburg amid internal instability and economic fragility.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Remain calm. Make sure not to escalate things further. Nobody wants a war."
Iosef Lancea"We will exercise utmost caution, Mr. President."
Valken Kruger"Hail Sordland!"
Iosef Lancea"Hail!"
NarratorThey saluted and left the room.
NarratorRumburg was already testing us and the reports from the Interior indicate interference inside our borders. Communists, Nationalists, Bludish rebels, Rumburg expansionism? What was next?
NarratorSordland had always been a key piece in the chessboard of the global rivalry between Arcasia and United Contana. But in the recent years, there was no such aggression from Rumburg. Not at these levels at least.
NarratorAll it takes is one tiny spark to start the flames of war.
NarratorThe phone rang.
NarratorRumburg had decided to close their consulate in Lachaven.

■ Contract BribeT02

BUDGET 6WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorIt was late in the evening.
NarratorThe Maroon Palace was finally getting somewhat quiet when I returned to my office at the top.
NarratorI eagerly approached my chair and sprawled out on it.
NarratorIt had been an exhausting day, just like every day since I took office.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:10:10 p15398 / c566
  • ·Light a cigarette.
  • ·Pour some whiskey.
  • ·Lean back and relax.
  • ▶Prepare to go home.
The President needs to prioritize family and personal stability after a traumatic day, ensuring he can face upcoming crises with clarity and resolve.
Qwen3 (ablit.)I forced myself out of the chair to gather my jacket and briefcase. I really needed to make some time for my family.
NarratorI quickly glanced out the window before leaving.
NarratorSerge and the guards were outside by the main gate as usual.
NarratorI heard a couple of knocks on the door. My secretary, Livia Suno, entered.
Livia Suno"Mr. President, Mr. Tusk is here to see you."
Livia Suno"You had a meeting with the Lotherberg spokesman. I'm so sorry for not giving you proper notice."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:10:30 p15606 / c608
  • ▶"Don't worry about it."
  • ·“Do your job properly next time. Do you expect me to keep track of everything myself?"
  • ·"I was just about to leave. Maybe we can postpone the meeting?"
The President prioritizes maintaining composure and efficient leadership, acknowledging Livia's mistake to preserve trust and ensure critical meetings proceed without unnecessary conflict.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Don't worry about it."
Livia Suno"Thank you for your understanding."
NarratorAfter a moment, Mr. Tusk swaggered into the room.
NarratorI'd met Sordland's most prominent businessman once or twice, but never alone. He had large jowls, blindingly shiny shoes and the air of someone who was used to getting his way.
Livia Suno"Anything to drink, Mr. Tusk? Water, whiskey?"
Walter Tusk"Cognac. Preferably from before you were born, sweetheart."
Livia Suno"I-- I'll check the supply room."
NarratorLivia backed out of the office, shooting me an apologetic look as she did so.
NarratorWalter hung up his hat, which I could see was lined with mink. He fixed me with a long, hard stare, then broke into a toothy grin.
Walter Tusk"Good evening, Mr. President. I am here to congratulate you."
NarratorWe both sat down.
Walter Tusk"My business partners and I wish you a successful term as the leader of our nation."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:10:48 p15595 / c355
  • ·"Thank you, Mr. Tusk."
  • ·Nod.
  • ▶"Get to the point, Mr. Tusk. Why are you really here?"
Rayne's cautious nature and awareness of political intrigue prompt him to challenge Tusk's unexpected visit, suspecting ulterior motives given the volatile context.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Get to the point, Mr. Tusk. Why are you really here?"
Walter Tusk"I see that you're not very welcoming, President."
Walter Tusk"I merely congratulated you. I see no reason to necessitate rudeness."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:11:04 p15594 / c402
  • ▶"Forgive me, I didn't want to be rude. Long day."
  • ·"I don't believe I'm being rude. Time is money after all. Isn't that right?"
Acknowledging fatigue fosters diplomatic rapport while subtly conveying the weight of leadership responsibilities.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Forgive me, I didn't want to be rude. Long day."
Walter Tusk"No need to ask for forgiveness."
Walter Tusk"You are the President after all."
Walter Tusk"May I continue?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:11:24 p15544 / c593
  • ▶"Please."
  • ·Nod.
Anton needs to hear Tusk's business interests, given the context of the story and the need to address potential threats or opportunities.

Walter Tusk"I'd like to build a profitable relationship between us."
Walter Tusk"That being said, there are some concerns of mine and the people I represent..."
Walter Tusk"...which I would like to take this opportunity to clear up."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:11:40 p15528 / c355
  • ▶"I'm listening."
  • ·"What are these concerns?"
  • ·"The people you represent?"

Qwen3 (ablit.)"I'm listening."
Walter Tusk"The ruined state of the economy in Sordland has influenced everyone. The recession hit all of us hard."
NarratorWalter pulled out a box of cigars from his jacket.
Walter Tusk"I had these brought from Lespia. The best cigars in the whole continent. Would you like to try one?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:12:00 p15280 / c680
  • ·Smoke a cigar.
  • ▶"You go ahead, I don't want to smoke."
  • ·"I'd rather not have you smoke here."
Declining the cigar maintains professionalism and avoids entanglement in business interests, crucial for a leader navigating Sordland's instability.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"You go ahead. I don't want to smoke right now."
NarratorWalter grabbed himself a cigar and lit it. He looked out towards the city scenery as he puffed away.
Walter Tusk"You are having a good start to the term for sure. Besides the recent troubles, I mean."
Walter Tusk"I'm glad that you have decided to promote the free market, which is the only thing that can get us out of this mess."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:12:18 p15335 / c491
  • ·"We had nothing but troubles so far and economy is not the only concern."
  • ·"The ability of the free-market to evolve is hard to dispute."
  • ▶"It is good that we think alike on the matter."
  • ·"I took my economic team's advice."
A diplomatic acknowledgment of shared interests with Tusk to maintain business support while navigating internal party dynamics and economic pressures.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"It is good that we think alike on the matter."
Walter Tusk"To a closer relationship then!"
Walter Tusk"One that will bring Sordland out of crippling recession!"
NarratorHe grinned.
Walter Tusk"The high-speed rail investment was a great decision, the business logistics will improve and boost the economy once finished."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:12:39 p15246 / c777
  • ▶"The intention of the administration is to boost the economy."
  • ·"Our goal was to focus on the needs of the businesses."
  • ·"The Greater Holsord and Gelsord regions needed better links."
  • ·"I don't want to disclose our intentions to an outsider."
Aligns with the administration's economic stabilization goals while avoiding overly specific commitments that could alienate other factions.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"The intention of the administration is to boost the economy."
Walter Tusk"The right intention for sure."
Walter Tusk"We can be close allies. The Group can help you get Sordland out of the recession. Which will be the key problem to solve in your term and could even determine your re-election."
Walter Tusk"Let's start with a gesture of goodwill. Soon the investment project contract will be awarded to one of the three main contenders."
NarratorHe pulled a check out of his pocket and slid it towards me on the table.
NarratorMy personal bank details were listed on it, showing a deposit of 500,000 Sordish Ren ready to be transferred. This amount was equivalent to six months of the presidential budget.
Walter Tusk"If the contract were somehow awarded to Underhall Construction, they would not only work hard and deliver, but also do it on time."
Walter Tusk"What do you think, Mr. Rayne, wouldn't that be good?"
NarratorHe put his index finger on the check.
NarratorThere was a noise by the door. Livia Suno was standing there, holding a tray with a bottle and two glasses.
Livia Suno"I'm so sorry, Mr. Tusk, but all I could find was a 1942 Sevigny. I hope that's acceptable."
NarratorShe quickly put the tray on my desk and walked out. Tusk eyed her as she left.
Walter Tusk"Remind me to visit your office more often. Now, where were we? Do we have a deal?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:12:58 p15388 / c470
  • ·"Indeed, Mr. Tusk. I am looking forward to our future transactions. [Take Bribe]"
  • ·“Not yet. We both know you can do better than that amount."
  • ·“No. I will make my own decision.”
  • ▶“Did you just try to bribe the President of Sordland? I refuse!”
Refusing the bribe upholds integrity and aligns with the manifesto's emphasis on state-led stability over private interests, crucial for maintaining public trust during crises.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Did you just try to bribe the President of Sordland? I refuse!"
NarratorWalter sighed and pulled the check away, putting it in his pocket.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:13:19 p15349 / c730
  • ·“Did you think that I was just another corrupt politician?”
  • ·“If this is what you came here for then we are done here.”
  • ·“Thank you for the visit nonetheless.”
  • ▶“Be careful, Tusk, or you will have to make your deals in a prison cell.”
The President must assert authority against corruption, warning Tusk that further misconduct will have severe consequences.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Be careful, Tusk, or you will have to make your deals in a prison cell."
Walter Tusk"I think we started off on the wrong foot here."
Walter Tusk"My goal was to begin a mutually beneficial partnership. Nothing more, I assure you."
Walter Tusk"Well, I will excuse myself then. Thank you for your time."
Walter Tusk"Oh I almost forgot. Please say hello to Mr. Koronti for me."
NarratorHe smiled.
Walter Tusk"Have a good evening, President."
NarratorWalter stood up, grabbed his jacket and made his way towards the door.
NarratorHe closed the door behind him. I gathered my things and prepared to finally head home.
NarratorAs I walked out I noticed Livia still there, covering up her typewriter for the evening.
Livia Suno"If I may be so bold, Mr. President... did you accept his offer?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:13:40 p15345 / c684

Livia Suno"Of course. Sorry to intrude."

■ Railway ContractT02

BUDGET 4WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorWe arrived in Anrica by train. After being greeted by crowds of cheering citizens and the local government officials, we headed to the city hall.
NarratorWe were here for a significant meeting about the L-1 project. Several construction companies sent their representatives to the city hall.
NarratorEven though Lileas Graf couldn't make the meeting, I had Symon Holl and Gus Manger by my side.
Symon Holl"I came by train yesterday and it was a little uncomfortable. Besides starting our economic recovery, this project will make the journey here much more pleasant, Mr. President."
Gus Manger"That is a given. But we'll also see improvements in everything from logistics to growth. You made the right choice for sure."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:14:45 p15356 / c697

  • ·“I hope so, Gus.”

Symon Holl"I am glad to hear that. We should be able to slow down the recession if we finish this project successfully."
Gus Manger"I am sure we will. According to your document many citizens will be employed because of business expansions."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:15:03 p15359 / c487
  • ·“What will it take to stop the recession?”
  • ·“What kind of end results do we expect?”
  • ▶“Failure during this project is not an option.”

Emphasizing the critical importance of the L-1 project's success to stabilize the economy and quell political unrest aligns with Rayne's mandate to prioritize immediate stability over ideological debates.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Failure during this project is not an option."
Gus Manger"You are right. We need to work together and think things through."
Symon Holl"Our economic revitalization plan is set to be a success. Failure is indeed not an option."
NarratorSymon searched through the documents he was holding and found the one he was looking for.
Symon Holl"I have the overview of the companies here."
NarratorSymon handed the files over to me.
Look at the files.
Qwen3 (ablit.)I looked at the files. Three corporations were listed. The Sordish State Corporation, Underhall Construction and Taurus Holding.

Symon Holl"Which corporation should we talk about?"

Qwen3 (ablit.)"The Sordish State Corporation."
Symon Holl"The respected Sordish State Corporation, also known as the SSC, was founded in 1891."
Symon Holl"It is the main state corporation tasked with running the day-to-day construction services of our country."
Gus Manger"The SSC is the primary reason why our current infrastructure is outdated. We need more adaptive construction companies."
Gus Manger"The disadvantage would be going against your election promises of moving away from a state-dominant economy."
Gus Manger"The Sordish State Corporation lacks a modern way of thinking. They also under-perform with their slow construction methods."
NarratorGus stopped and laughed.
Gus Manger"Look at the abysmal regional railway system!"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:15:41 p15495 / c638

Qwen3 (ablit.)"They do have a wealth of knowledge in infrastructure."
Symon Holl"I have no great admiration for the SSC, but their track record clearly shows completed contracts with thousands of employees who have experience."
Symon Holl"According to the preliminary calculations of the SSC, they would need more resources to finish a project of this size."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:16:00 p15511 / c532

Qwen3 (ablit.)"I have settled on the SSC."
NarratorSymon signed a certain section of the dossier and fixed his glasses.
Symon Holl"Very well, Mr. President. The contract will be awarded to the Sordish State Corporation."
Gus Manger"No hard feelings, Mr. President. At the end of the day the success of the project is what matters."
Symon Holl"The company will be notified today, and construction work will begin immediately after."

Gus Manger"It will be a pleasure to celebrate together. For now, I'll return to my duties and visit some foreign partners in Rizia. See you soon, Mr. President."
“Thank you. This concludes the meeting.”
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Thank you. This concludes the meeting."

BUDGET 2WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorOur motorcade headed to the meeting location set by Marcel Koronti. I had only my most confidential and trustworthy team members with me.

Petr Vectern"I hope the journey was comfortable."

Marcel Koronti"Welcome. Your kind words have touched me, Mr. Vice President. He was indeed a great man."

Marcel Koronti"It's time to look forward. Let's talk about our bright future."

NarratorMarcel gently pointed at the seats. We sat down around a gigantic table. Before speaking he knocked on wood.
Marcel Koronti"The past months have been tumultuous to say the least, I am glad you and your family were unharmed at the shooting."
Marcel Koronti"The whole incident must have been a shock for the kids. They aren't used to what we lived through back in the late '20s. I still remember when my father took us out of the country when it became too dangerous in Conriat."

Qwen3 (ablit.)"You are playing a dangerous game."
Marcel Koronti"We are all dangerous men playing dangerous games."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:19:40 p15423 / c288
  • ·“Let's hear your offer.”
  • ▶“What do you want in return?”
  • ·“You aren’t just being nice, are you?”
Uncovering Koronti's demands is critical to assess his true motives and potential threats to the administration's stability.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"What do you want in return?"
Marcel Koronti"I want two things, Mr. President. Number one is a guarantee from you that the Rayne administration won't meddle in HOS affairs, no matter what happens."
Marcel Koronti"Number two is a simple gentleman's favor for the future. Let me prove my services to you first, and I'm sure you won't disapprove when the time comes."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:19:57 p15458 / c445

  • ▶“It is an interesting proposal.”
  • ·“How do I know that I can trust you?”
The proposal offers potential collaboration benefits while allowing caution, aligning with Rayne's need to balance risks and opportunities in a volatile political landscape.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"It is an interesting proposal."
Marcel Koronti"Not just interesting but necessary. You surely know that this is a win-win situation.
Marcel Koronti"The media will be on your side strengthening your position. It will also protect my business operations throughout the changes this country will see."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:20:13 p15474 / c406

  • ·“What do you think about this, Lucian?”
  • ·“You have been awfully silent, Petr.”

Rejecting the offer maintains presidential independence and avoids entanglement with HOS's media influence, aligning with Rayne's principles despite potential short-term risks.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Mr. Koronti, I have to reject."
Marcel Koronti"Such a pity, Mr. President. And here I thought we were going to be good friends."
Marcel Koronti"It's a shame. Really."

  • ▶“Remember who you are speaking to, Mr. Koronti.”
  • ·“I regret coming here to see you.”

Marcel Koronti"Oh, I know very well."

Lucian Galade"I was hoping that would end better."

Lucian Galade"The threats were unacceptable."

■ Decision FuneralT02

BUDGET 2WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
EventFuneral of Bernard Circas: The death of Bernard Circas has shocked the country and his funeral will be held in Deyr among a large crowd of sympathizers. Circas was not only a communist poet but also an elected MP that had a voice. A state funeral has been organized to honor him. The question is whether the President and the administration will attend the funeral or not.
EVENT Qwen3 (ablit.)19:21:10 p14888 / c521
  • ▶Attend the funeral
  • ·Do not attend
Attending the funeral demonstrates commitment to democratic principles and unity, signaling respect for all citizens' voices amid rising tensions.

■ Personal FuneralT02

BUDGET 2WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorDark clouds were looming over Deyr. They looked daunting as our convoy started approaching the century-old cemetery.
NarratorIt looked like it was going to rain. Certainly fitting weather for a funeral.
NarratorAs expected, Bernard Circas's passing maintained its media traction and all of Sordland was paying attention to the ceremony. Especially to what I would say in my speech today.
Serge Wolkner"Sir, we have arrived."
NarratorRain drops were running down the car window when Serge opened my door and held an umbrella over my head. As soon as I got out, flashes from the cameras blinded me.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:21:32 p15110 / c607
  • ▶“It's okay Serge. I will hold my own umbrella.”
  • ·“Thank you.”
Demonstrating personal resolve and connection with the people amid turmoil
Qwen3 (ablit.)"It's okay Serge. I will hold my own umbrella."
Serge Wolkner"As you wish sir."
NarratorHe handed me the umbrella and started waiting next to the car with his own.
NarratorPetr and Karl exited from their own cars in the convoy. They made their way over to me.
Petr Vectern"Anton."
Karl Greiser"Mr. President."
Karl Greiser"I just wanted to inform you that the perimeter is clear and secured completely. There is no one getting in or out without our knowledge."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:21:49 p15149 / c420
  • ▶“We don't want something similar happening again, do we?”
  • ·“You better be right for your sake.”
  • ·“Good work. Thank you Karl.”
Reinforces vigilance against past tragedies while subtly emphasizing the necessity of strict security measures.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"We don't want something similar happening again, do we?"
Karl Greiser"Absolutely not, Mr. President. The same mistakes will never happen again. I personally made sure of that."
Petr Vectern"Good. If anything happens to Anton, you will have to answer to the First Lady."
Petr Vectern"And trust me Karl. You don't want that."
NarratorPetr let out a laugh, which drew some attention from bereaved attendees.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:22:08 p15179 / c580
  • ·“Petr, you are being inconsiderate.”
  • ·“I don't think any of us want that.”
  • ·Gesture Petr to lower his voice.
  • ▶“You are the only one who can make me laugh at a funeral.”
The president uses sarcasm to gently mock Petr's inappropriate joke, maintaining authority while lightening the somber mood without direct confrontation.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"You are the only one that can make me laugh at a funeral."
Petr Vectern"Hah! Right? I mean, life is full of tragedy. We should try to take it easy if we can."
NarratorIt started to rain. Several unintelligible voices were heard from Karl's radio. He stepped out to reply and returned shortly.
Karl Greiser"We have a small problem, sir."
Karl Greiser"A few -- five to be exact -- Red Youth members are here to attend the funeral. They apparently said they wish to pay their respects to their fallen comrade."
Karl Greiser"Should we let them in?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:22:22 p15297 / c242
  • ·“Do you think they pose a security threat?”
  • ·“Let them in.”
  • ▶“Let them in but put a few guards on them.”
  • ·“Absolutely not.”
Balancing security concerns with diplomatic outreach to de-escalate tensions between political factions.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Let them in but put a few guards on them to be safe."
Karl Greiser"As you command."
Petr Vectern"Let's head over. They are about to start."
NarratorWe walked towards the crowd circled around the grave. Everyone in attendance was wearing black in mourning. The rain started pouring heavily.
NarratorBernard's widow and daughter were in the front row, staring at the coffin with a mixture of disbelief, confusion and above all, a cold, unforgiving sadness.
NarratorBoth of their eyes were very red and swollen from weeping.
NarratorBefore taking my place to observe the ceremony, I gave my condolences to Bernard's wife and his daughter who looked at me. She reminded me of Deana.
NarratorWhen the religious ceremony was done, I was invited by the priest to deliver my speech. I took the podium. I felt like I had a lump in my throat. I couldn't get the daughter's eyes out of my head.
“Today, we are mourning Bernard Circas, a father, a husband and a loyal servant of this country.”
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Today, we are mourning Bernard Circas, a father, a husband and a loyal servant of this country."
NarratorTowards the back of the crowd, I spotted the members of the Red Youth, wearing red shirts and black bands of mourning on their arms. They seemed ruly and were respectfully bowing their heads.
NarratorThere was complete silence except for the sound of raindrops.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:22:41 p15567 / c465
  • ·“This is a great loss for all of us.”
  • ·“I will confess that I disagreed with Bernard Circas almost entirely. But it doesn't mean that he deserved this. Our country can be a better place.”
  • ▶“Regardless of his ideology, this was a heinous act made towards a good man. We must end this cycle of violence.”
  • ·“He was a true comrade and he fought for what he believed in, his ideal vision of Sordland, in every step of his life.”
Condemning the violence while acknowledging the victim's humanity helps de-escalate tensions and emphasizes stability over ideological divides.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Regardless of his ideology, this was a heinous act made towards a good man. We must end this cycle of violence now."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:22:57 p15554 / c335
  • ▶“We will make sure that unfortunate events such as this will never happen again.”
  • ·“Circas had a dream of a unified Sordland. In these next months we need to fulfill his dream and band together.”
  • ·“I interpret this attack as an attack against Sordland and by extension, against me personally.”
  • ·“I promise you this. The blood of Bernard will not remain on the sacred ground of Sordland.”
Emphasizes preventing future violence to maintain stability, aligning with the administration's focus on avoiding ideological entanglements and ensuring national security.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"We will make sure that unfortunate events such as this will never happen ever again."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:23:12 p15572 / c319
  • ·“Let us all mourn the passing of Bernard Circas and remember him well.”
  • ▶“But, hatred begets hatred. There is only one way to overcome this vicious cycle. We must unite against the evil of this world.”
  • ·“The tears of the family he left behind have a price that needs to be paid. I will not stop until it is paid in full.”
Promoting unity against hatred aligns with de-escalating tensions while adhering to the manifesto's emphasis on stability over ideological confrontation.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"But, hatred begets hatred. There is only one way to overcome this vicious cycle. We must unite against the evil of this world."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:23:29 p15494 / c497
  • ·“Thank you.”
  • ▶“I would now like to recite one of the poems he left us.”
Reciting a poem honors Circas's legacy as a poet and emphasizes unity over division, aligning with the need to transcend ideological violence.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I would now like to recite a part from one of the poems he left us."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:23:46 p15511 / c459
  • ·“Our only strength is the will to survive, knowing that we will perish, knowing nothing more absolute.”
  • ·“We rise, toward the sun! Those who died, died fighting. They are now buried in the sun. No need to mourn, there is a raid to the sun."
  • ▶"They did not let us sing our songs. They are afraid, afraid of the dawn. They are afraid of hope, but our songs will be sung."
The poem emphasizes resistance against oppression and the enduring power of hope, aligning with the need to unite and address political tensions while honoring Circas's legacy as a poet.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"They did not let us sing our songs. They are afraid, afraid of the dawn. They are afraid of hope, but our songs will be sung."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:24:02 p15543 / c330
  • ·“Thank you.”
  • ▶“A moment of silence please.”
A moment of silence would honor Bernard Circas's memory and unify the attendees amid political tensions, reflecting the president's commitment to stability and respect for all citizens.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"A moment of silence please."
NarratorDuring the silence, all that was heard were the ravens.
NarratorSome attendees started clapping, some crying.
NarratorThe coffin was lowered into the grave by the gravekeepers. After a couple of minutes, Bernard Circas was with the soil, no more.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:24:17 p15468 / c315
  • ▶“As we bid farewell to Bernard, I want to close with the words every Sord lives by: A Morgna wes core!”
  • ·Step down from the podium.
The traditional slogan reinforces national unity and hope, providing closure to the funeral speech while aligning with Sordland's cultural identity.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"As we bid farewell to Bernard, I want to close with the words every Sord lives by: A Morgna wes core!"
NarratorEveryone replied: "Vectern sis da!". Victory is close.
NarratorWith applauses, I stepped down from the podium. It was time to leave.
NarratorThe widow thanked me with a soft and defeated voice before we dispersed.
NarratorOn my way back to the car, I still couldn't get the eyes of Bernard's daughter, nor the faltering voice of his wife, out of my head. Would Deana, Monica and Franc look the same way if something happened to me?
NarratorPetr put his hand on my shoulder.
Petr Vectern"That was a good speech."
NarratorI sensed only respect in his voice.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:24:35 p15555 / c430
  • ▶“It's time to go.”
  • ·“Thank you. I mean it.”
  • ·Nod.
The president needs to transition from the funeral's somber atmosphere to administrative duties, ensuring both personal composure and national responsibilities are prioritized.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"It's time to go."
Petr Vectern"Agreed."
Petr Vectern"We've got work to do."
NarratorHe was right. We had work to do. So much more to do.
NarratorI realized that the rain had stopped, and I looked up.
NarratorThe black clouds slowly dispersed and sunbeams enlightened our surroundings.
NarratorAs someone once said: 'Even the darkest night will end and the sun will rise.'
NarratorHope was still on the horizon.

■ Diplomacy OverviewT02

BUDGET 2WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorThe Blue Mansion in Lachaven hosted us for the important diplomacy gathering.
NarratorThe door opened, and Deivid Wisci -- once a lecturer to me, now the Minister of Foreign Affairs -- gently made his way towards my desk.
Deivid Wisci"May I sit down, Mr. President?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:25:09 p15216 / c506
  • ·“Leave the formalities aside, Deivid. Please take a seat.”
  • ▶“Of course. It's a pleasure to see you, Mr. Wisci.”
  • ·Gesture at the chair.
Maintaining diplomatic courtesy while acknowledging Deivid's new role ensures respect and professionalism in their interaction.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Of course! It is a pleasure to see you, Mr. Wisci."
Deivid Wisci"Likewise. We have so much ahead of us to accomplish."
NarratorHe slowly sat down, taking care not to aggravate the arthritis in his knees. He'd always had an air of gravitas about him, and old age had only served to deepen that.
NarratorAfter he settled himself, he regarded me with delight, his blue eyes sparkling.
Deivid Wisci"Look at you, Anton. The 4th President of Sordland. What a privilege for me."
Deivid Wisci"I still remember the first day you took my class at the Lachaven School of Economics."
Deivid Wisci"Somehow the inexplicable ebb and flow of life has led us here. To positions where we can change the course of history."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:25:28 p15247 / c521
  • ·“Everything happens for a reason, Deivid. It was meant to be.”
  • ·“We got a little lucky. That's all.”
  • ▶“This is our opportunity to shape the future and we will do precisely that.”
Anton's proactive nature and responsibility toward Sordland's future align with taking control of opportunities to shape the nation's direction.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"This is our opportunity to shape the future and we will do precisely that."
Deivid Wisci"I am looking forward to our new foreign policy."
Deivid Wisci"I am excited to present you the latest bill I am working on. The TCPA has turned out to be an initiative that may significantly boost our tourism and culture industry."
Deivid Wisci"This untapped value would help us promote our position internationally. I am convinced it can also help improve our economic condition after discussions with Ministers Holl and Manger."
Deivid Wisci"The GNA factions are supportive too. I really shouldn't have worked overtime hours to get it done, but I really care for the position and value of our cult-"
NarratorHe started coughing again. This time for longer.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:25:45 p15192 / c415
  • ·“Would you like some tea or water?”
  • ·“How is your health, Deivid?”
  • ▶“Let's start talking about our foreign policy.”
Prioritizing foreign policy discussion is critical given Sordland's geopolitical tensions and economic instability, requiring immediate strategic decision-making.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Let's start talking about our foreign policy."
NarratorThe door opened. Iosef and Lucian joined us for the briefing as the tall case clock hit the hour mark.
NarratorPetr, however, was missing. I wondered if he was late from all the drinking he did at the party in Lachaven.
Deivid Wisci"We'll talk more later, President Rayne. Welcome everyone."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:26:01 p15140 / c349
  • ·“How was your travel to Lachaven?”
  • ▶“Let's begin.”
The meeting is focused on diplomatic and foreign policy discussions, requiring immediate attention to pressing geopolitical issues rather than casual inquiries about travel.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Let's begin."
Deivid Wisci"Very well."
Deivid Wisci"I will start by providing a brief overview of our current status with our neighbours. What would you like to start with?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:26:19 p15098 / c549
  • ·“I'd like to hear about Agnolia.”
  • ▶“I want to know more about Valgsland.”
  • ·“What's the latest situation in Wehlen?”
Focusing on Valgsland aligns with the need for pragmatic foreign policy to stabilize Sordland, potentially securing regional alliances that support economic recovery without escalating tensions with major blocs.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I want to know more about Valgsland."
Deivid Wisci"Our cousins of old across the Markian Sea wish to have good relations with us. As we all know, Valgsland is a socialist republic aligned with United Contana. They are an important regional player."
Deivid Wisci"It's also the most significant sea power in almost all of Merkopa. It is the only country that can rival the superpowers with their navy. We had hostile relations with them after several wars in the 19th century, which we wouldn't want to experience again."
Deivid Wisci"Recently, the relationship has warmed up thanks to Emmerich Hegel. As a result, diplomatic talks can be conducted in the future."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:26:39 p15360 / c705
  • ·“It's not easy to forget about the Great Fire of Conriat. Thousands of lives were lost that day.”
  • ·“Breaking the ice with Valgsland could be good, if we are to rise diplomatically.”
  • ▶“We shouldn't take any sides between the regional powers.”
Maintaining neutrality aligns with the presidential manifesto's focus on avoiding entanglement in regional conflicts to prioritize domestic stability.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"We shouldn't take any sides between the regional powers."
Deivid Wisci"It could lead us to be squeezed between the two with no protection. It's a potential situation to be considered."
Iosef Lancea"They haven't been in good standing with us for the past two hundred years. Recently, they have also joined the Communist Contanan Security Pact, subjugating themselves to United Contana."
Iosef Lancea"On top of that, territorial issues have been flaring up between Agnolia and Valgsland because of the Heljiland Island. The Sordish Navy is very concerned with this volatile situation."
NarratorLucian looked slightly worried.
Lucian Galade"Unfortunately, billions of Sordish Ren worth of cargo is transported close to Valgslandian maritime borders on the Markian Sea and the Grey Strait."
Lucian Galade"This causes significant risks to our imports and exports in the case of a deterioration in relations."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:26:56 p15615 / c375
  • ▶“The strait is of high strategic value to us.”
  • ·“We can't expect the worst.”
The strait's strategic value underscores the need for proactive measures to protect Sordland's economy and security amid regional tensions.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"The strait is of high strategic value to us."
Lucian Galade"Iosef, the Navy is keeping an eye on the situation from a distance, right?"
Iosef Lancea"We are actively monitoring the sea routes and cargo ships. For now, there is no immediate threat. But we don't know what can happen in the future."

Qwen3 (ablit.)"Let's move on."

CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:27:32 p15629 / c433

Qwen3 (ablit.)"What is the situation with Rumburg?"
Deivid Wisci"Now to the most troublesome nation; Rumburg. A constitutional monarchy led by Queen Beatrice Livingston."
Iosef Lancea"I highly advise caution and calculated action against Rumburg. Our military must be prepared, the threat is real."
Deivid Wisci"They are strong and have the motivation, as well as the means, to damage us. We shouldn't give them any reasons to pursue aggression."

Qwen3 (ablit.)"I will take your advice in consideration."
Deivid Wisci"These types of nations will always try to make up a casus belli, a reason to declare war."
Iosef Lancea"While Thornbourgh openly denies such claims, their past and current actions make it clear that they want to destabilize us."
Iosef Lancea"The weapons caches found by our intelligence agency clearly shows what their goals are. Our military must be steadfast and our diplomacy valiant."

Lucian Galade"We could use their aggressive attitude against them by convincing the international community."
NarratorLucian grabbed the Lachaven Times newspaper and put his finger on the article about the latest diplomatic incident.
Lucian Galade"Their abrupt announcement about the closure of the Rumburg consulate in Lachaven is yet another step in their escalation."
“What about Lespia?”
Qwen3 (ablit.)"What about Lespia?"
Deivid Wisci"Our southern neighbour is aligning itself more and more with Arcasia. Lespia is currently led by Prime Minister Patricio Alvarez."
Lucian Galade"They are the jewel of the continent and the second largest economy in Eastern Merkopa. Though wealthy, Lespia suffers from excessive economic inequality."
Deivid Wisci"Our past relations have been great but during the last decade, their investments to Sordland dwindled due to our instability."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:28:28 p15801 / c728

  • ·“Lespia is a sleeping giant. We should not awake them.”

Qwen3 (ablit.)"Allying with the richest regional nation could be beneficial."
Deivid Wisci"It could be but it would also mean that we would draw closer to the Arcasia block and near the Arcasian Treaty Organisation."
Deivid Wisci"I am certain that the recession has a lot to do with their retraction of investments. Once they see that money isn't in the picture they vanish rather quickly because they are capitalists."
Iosef Lancea"They have shown a selfish attitude during the Wehlen Civil War by closing their borders and funnelling refugees to cross into Sordland."
NarratorLucian cleared his throat.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:28:45 p15833 / c416

Qwen3 (ablit.)"The Capitalism of Lespia is inspirational to me. Look at their economic power."
Deivid Wisci"Everything comes at a cost. In Lespia the cost is wealth inequality but business people are getting richer and richer."
Lucian Galade"There are hundreds of very privileged oligarchs who invest inside and outside of Lespia. Some made significant business investments in the cities of Benfi and Valgen."
Deivid Wisci"This covers the overview of the current situation. We will move on to our trade choice and the response to Rumburg's diplomatic escalation."
NarratorThe sun began to set over the hills surrounding Lachaven. The team was ready to give advice on the new trade initiative and the response to the latest diplomatic incident with Rumburg.
NarratorPetr arrived late. He looked a little rougher than usual. It must have been the drinking from last night.
Petr Vectern"Hey there, folks. Excuse my delay. I'm sure I didn't miss anything we didn't already know."
Iosef Lancea"You were still supposed to be a part of this meeting. An hour ago."
Lucian Galade"Punctuality is important. We are talking about serious subjects here."

Qwen3 (ablit.)"Petr was assigned a task from me, that is why he is late. So let's move on."
Petr Vectern"Ah, yes, yes. It went successfully, sir."
NarratorPetr's tone was more serious.
Petr Vectern"Expect a report on your desk tomorrow."
Deivid Wisci"The Vice President will be part of the trade delegation, so it will be great to have his input on that subject as well as on the Rumburg incident."
Deivid Wisci"Rumburg Consul Sir Bran Harrington announced the closure of the 120-year-old consulate. Now their diplomatic staff is leaving this city to go back to Thornbourgh."
Lucian Galade"He blamed the lack of security in Sordland due to the assassination and the subsequent protests. But it is obvious that they are making excuses."
Iosef Lancea"Bastards! First they close their consulate and then they blame our security!?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:29:19 p16119 / c396

Qwen3 (ablit.)"Their concerns could be genuine, but this reaction is unnecessary."
Petr Vectern"Agreed. Other diplomatic missions have worked with our security units to increase the protection of staff and buildings."
Deivid Wisci"The neglect of President Alphonso, and the chaos of the elections, delayed our diplomatic efforts."
Deivid Wisci"Now there are only two Rumburg diplomatic missions left in Sordland. One is the consulate in Deyr and the other is the embassy in Holsord."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:29:38 p16161 / c537
  • ▶“We are accustomed to the usual rhetoric but this is far more serious.”
  • ·“I must display strength to my people. We can't be pushed around.”
  • ·“They are bluffing. We are used to it by now.”
Acknowledging the severity of Rumburg's action while maintaining composure aligns with balancing domestic stability and avoiding unnecessary escalation, consistent with the manifesto's focus on neutrality and pragmatic governance.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"We are accustomed to the usual rhetoric but this is far more serious."
Petr Vectern"They are playing with fire. Look at what the Wehlen destabilization has done to the region. Sordland breaking apart would tear Merkopa apart."
Deivid Wisci"In my entire career I have never seen our Rumburg relations suffer this much. The previous low point was the War of Broken Wills."
NarratorIosef Lancea was furious. He barely kept himself from slamming the desk.
Iosef Lancea"There are worrying developments like the smuggled arms and the build-up near the border. Our military must be buffed up to stand against a great power like Rumburg."
Deivid Wisci"We either respond to this aggressive act with a diplomatic condemnation or we retaliate by closing our consulate in Dome."
Lucian Galade"I want to say that an escalation would be risky this early in our Presidency. We should focus on creating a negative image of Rumburg in the international scene."
Iosef Lancea"The people of Sordland must be protected, no matter the cost. I say we respond equally."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:29:58 p16348 / c528
  • ·“Retaliate by closing the Sordland Consulate in Dome! They think they can bully us. They were never so wrong.”
  • ▶“Write an official diplomatic response condemning this unreasonable act. Declare that Rumburg aims to destabilize Sordland and the region.”
A diplomatic condemnation aligns with the manifesto's neutral foreign policy, avoids escalating tensions with Rumburg, and prioritizes domestic stability over provocative retaliation.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Write an official diplomatic response condemning this unreasonable act. Declare that Rumburg aims to destabilize Sordland and the region."
Deivid Wisci"As you wish Mr. President. I shall begin work by immediately informing the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade."
Lucian Galade"Swaying the international stage is the key in long-term strategy."
Iosef Lancea"I wish we had reacted more strongly. If we don't confront them, they will keep escalating the situation."
Petr Vectern"I, on the other hand, am glad that we are taking the more cautious road."
Deivid Wisci"There are three trade-related topics to address today. Which topic should we tackle first?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:30:17 p16328 / c476
  • ▶“We should talk about the ongoing trade negotiations with our neighbors Wehlen and Agnolia.”
  • ·“I'm interested to hear the intentions of the Kingdom of Rizia.”
Focusing on trade negotiations with Wehlen and Agnolia aligns with maintaining regional stability and economic recovery while avoiding direct entanglement in larger bloc conflicts.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"We should talk about the ongoing trade negotiations with our neighbors Wehlen and Agnolia."
Deivid Wisci"Our primary goal will be to successfully sign at least one trade agreement with one of our smaller neighbors."
Deivid Wisci"We have two options with our trade initiative. Starting new negotiations with our old partner Agnolia to the north-east, and forming a new cooperation with Wehlen to the west."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:30:38 p16230 / c754
  • ▶“Tell me about the trade deal with Agnolia.”
  • ·“Tell me about the trade deal with Wehlen.”
Agnolia's historical ties and proximity make it a critical partner for stabilizing regional trade, while Wehlen's alignment with Contana introduces ideological risks that could complicate Sordland's neutral stance.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Tell me about the trade deal with Agnolia."
Deivid Wisci"First off, the reason behind this option is more diplomatic than economic, although of course we can't forgo the added economic value. Agnolia is an old ally and a valuable trade partner."
Deivid Wisci"The point we are at with the early negotiations is around the steel trade. They are demanding that we buy their steel for a higher price. "
Deivid Wisci"That is because they think that the deal is far too unbalanced as it is. "
Deivid Wisci"Their second main demand is a privileged access to our agricultural market."
Deivid Wisci"If we are to fulfil their demands, in return they are offering a full alliance with Sordland against Rumburg."
Iosef Lancea"Looking at the current advances of Rumburg near our borders, having a coalition against Rumburg would prove extremely useful."
Deivid Wisci"Obviously there will be consequences for entering a full alliance. Mainly the issue is around Heljiland. Valgsland has clear intentions on taking over the island one way or another and entering a military alliance with Agnolia will make them furious."
Iosef Lancea"I want to state my support for the decision to stick to our old ally, Agnolia. Even though I disagree with their demand to relax immigration, we have cooperated with them on many issues in the past."
Petr Vectern"There are obviously subjects that we would still need to tackle like our economies, the traded goods and what to do with immigration."
Deivid Wisci"My opinion is that this is a good deal and we should go for it. The new Agnolian Prime Minister is trying to improve the existing relationship."
Deivid Wisci"The downside is that we would need to make concessions. A reality of any trade deal."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:30:59 p16510 / c545
  • ▶“I would be open to creating a new agreement that would satisfy all sides.”
  • ·“I don't want to give any concessions to them.”
  • ·“We can't position ourselves against Valgsland.”
Balancing diplomatic flexibility and economic necessity by pursuing a negotiated agreement that addresses Agnolia's demands while mitigating risks to Sordland's stability and regional relationships.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I would be open to creating a new agreement that would satisfy all sides."
Deivid Wisci"That would be the best outcome."
Deivid Wisci"If you decide that Agnolia is a good option, another point is that they have a very democratic system. After the recent elections the PM has a mandate to arrange a fairer deal with us. Maartin Van Hoorten also is an avid supporter of relaxed immigration."
Deivid Wisci"Since we said we will tighten immigration, he might be reluctant."
Lucian Galade"Regardless, if we are going to increase our regional influence to previous levels, we might need to make amends with our neighbours."
Lucian Galade"Ultimately the decision will be up to you as always."
“Tell me about the trade deal with Wehlen.”
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Tell me about the trade deal with Wehlen."
Deivid Wisci"Dealing with a fractured country like Wehlen has its positives and negatives. Although it will be lucrative in the short term, there are risks associated with dealing with such a country."
Deivid Wisci"They are proposing co-investment projects and a no-tariff agreement. They are also offering oil in return for attracting foreign capital interests from Sordland."
Deivid Wisci"However, it is possible that there will be international ramifications of getting closer with a country like Wehlen."
Deivid Wisci"All in all, this is a very lucrative deal for us. However, we need to think about the consequences."
Iosef Lancea"I certainly don't support the decision to become friendlier with Wehlen. We carried their burdens for decades. Their migrants flooded our country and now they are expecting a deal from us."
Petr Vectern"Maintaining influence with Wehlen could prove useful. Yes, it is risky, but think about our influence on a underdeveloped region like that. We could also sell more products and enter their market easily."
Deivid Wisci"I am of the opinion that it is simply too risky. Yes, we would gain resources and immediate capital, but I'm afraid of the consequences."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:31:17 p16613 / c339
  • ·“I think the return is too lucrative to pass on regardless of the risk.”
  • ▶“You may be right. It's risky.”
The risks associated with Wehlen's instability and potential international backlash outweigh the short-term economic gains, aligning with the administration's focus on stability and caution.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"You may be right. It's risky."
Lucian Galade"I tend to agree."
Deivid Wisci"If we wish to go forward with the deal when the time comes, there is the topic of immigration to consider."
Deivid Wisci"Wiktor Smolak is a known isolationist. Our promise to tighten our immigration policy will increase the likelihood of the deal. He believes our borders need better security."
Lucian Galade"I personally don't like the idea of getting too close to Wehlen."
Deivid Wisci"Me too."
Iosef Lancea"Agreed."
Petr Vectern"I still think we should give it a try."
Deivid Wisci"Hmm."
Deivid Wisci"Our campaign promise to steer Sordland towards the west and Arcasia will have an effect on the negotiations."
Deivid Wisci"Especially with Wehlen. As you know the current affairs of Wehlen are partly a result of United Contana's support of Wiktor Smolak. This will be a problem but definitely worth trying."
Deivid Wisci"As for Agnolia, this promise has made them cautious, which might make it harder to get a good deal."
Deivid Wisci"We also need to consider the effects of our economic doctrine plan."
Deivid Wisci"The fact that we are headed towards a market economy will have an influence."
Petr Vectern"Good negotiators optimize the interests which makes the differences become trivial, allowing both sides to reach a deal."
Deivid Wisci"Very true, Mr. Vectern. We have a lot of time to prepare before the scheduled visits to Wehlen and Agnolia."
Deivid Wisci"I believe this gives a brief overview of our current options."
Deivid Wisci"Our trade delegation will begin work towards the end of the year. We expect final negotiations and the signings of the trade agreements to happen next year."
Petr Vectern"I'm very much looking forward to visiting Staalport and Wraclawitz with party animals like Deivid and Symon! Haha!"
NarratorPetr laughed. Iosef narrowed his eyes and stared at him.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:31:41 p16554 / c541
  • ·“I'd like to know more about Rizia.”
  • ▶“I don't need any further information about Rizia.”
  • ·“Let's discuss the deal potential and what's on the table.”

Qwen3 (ablit.)"I don't need any further information about Rizia."
Deivid Wisci"Of course, Mr. President."
“Let's discuss the deal potential and what's on the table.”
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Let's discuss the deal potential and what's on the table."
Deivid Wisci"From what we have gathered, there is no clear offer yet. The interest clearly seems to be more control over the gas produced and sold in Sordland. I suspect this must be related to the recent tensions on the coast of Pales."
Deivid Wisci"Mr. Manger is working out the details to get more concrete headline terms soon. Of particular interest are the gas fields in the Narbel area of the Nargis region."
Petr Vectern"That must be related to Gasom operations. Gus has ties with the people around that corporation. Even though there is another CEO, Ewald Alphonso is still chairman."
Lucian Galade"Gasom is a private corporation, a fact that limits our control over any foreign investment. Tarquin Soll's Energy Protection Act of 1932 specifically limits the purchases of Sordish energy company shares by foreign nations to ten percent."
Lucian Galade"Ewald Alphonso adjusted this law in 1948 and increased it to up to 20 percent. Anything above that requires us to change the Energy Protection Act. Ewald holds most of Gasom's cards in his hands still."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:31:59 p16600 / c316
  • ·“Things didn't end amicably between us. I mean, we aggressively destroyed his campaign team one by one.”
  • ▶“I see a strong case for Tarquin Soll's regulation. Energy security is important for our nation.”
  • ·“Gasom is private, but it still sells most of the gas it produces to Sordland, right?”
Supporting Tarquin Soll's regulation aligns with prioritizing energy security and state control over critical resources, which is crucial for stabilizing Sordland's economy and resisting foreign influence.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I see a strong case for Tarquin Soll's regulation. Energy security is important for our nation."
Petr Vectern"We don't want to find ourselves in the energy crisis situation of the early 1930s, where nations like Rumburg and Lespia leeched off our weakened position and charged us triple the cost per cubic ton."
Lucian Galade"They smelled weakness, and thanks to initiatives of the Soll government, and the technocrats, the nation survived those hardships."
Iosef Lancea"No matter how much people criticize Soll these days, the Colonel is the reason why Sordland can stand mostly independent in this age."
Deivid Wisci"The energy topic is gaining importance again with the tensions in the Antacean Sea. I'm keeping a close watch on the situation."
NarratorLucian pulled out a pen and took a few notes.
NarratorDeivid coughed a bit and then continued talking.
Deivid Wisci"From our diplomatic channels, we can tell that Lespia is still very interested in Sordish petrol, whereas Rumburg wants more gas resources."
Iosef Lancea"As a sovereign nation, we must protect our resources. Our energy independence is part of our national security concern here; Gasom handles most of our gas production."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:32:17 p16765 / c419
  • ·“Allowing more market economics to determine the value and output of our energy is aligned with our economic plan.”
  • ▶“We should protect our energy independence, but we don’t know the offer's specifics yet.”
  • ·“As long as we can improve our economy and resource extraction, we shouldn’t care much.”
Protecting energy independence aligns with historical regulations and national security concerns while allowing careful evaluation of offers before committing to market-driven changes.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"We should protect our energy independence, but we don’t know the offer's specifics yet."
Deivid Wisci"I agree, but Gasom is a private corporation. Even though it mostly supplies the government, we have little control over it."
Iosef Lancea"If Rizia wants to buy more than 20 percent of shares allowed by the protectionism laws, then the government can step in to make changes to the EPA."
Lucian Galade"Precisely, but we don’t know if this will be the case. There is a scenario in which they might invest within the EPA limitations; we just have to hear more before weighing the options."
Petr Vectern"They never revealed much about their involvement to me, but I always had the feeling that Alphonso's crew was more linked to Gasom than they seemed."
Lucian Galade"I have information that the CEO that replaced Alphonso is a loyalist. Alphonso still is the chairman of Gasom and the de facto key figure. Additionally, National Business Council Chairman Mikail Aven and Minister Gus Manger have shares in the company and preside over the board."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)19:32:36 p16700 / c431

  • ·“Affluent Sords don’t stray far from each other.”

Qwen3 (ablit.)"The shareholder links raise questions regarding Alphonso and Manger's motivations."
Lucian Galade"I suspect that the corporation isn’t doing too well, and they are seeking investments. Any investment into Gasom will enrich the owners but might also lead to more jobs and growth."
Deivid Wisci"Any further comments, esteemed cabinet members?"
NarratorThe room went silent and no one stepped forward. It was noticeable how much people respected Wisci. He carried the soul of Sordland as a symbol of the past.

Petr Vectern"If there's one thing Gus is good at, it’s making deals."
Deivid Wisci"I’m curious how it will develop. It’s mostly out of the hands of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs for now."
Lucian Galade"I will assist in analyzing the political implications, Mr. Wisci."
Deivid Wisci"Thank you. Our support staff and I will be working towards the next steps soon after receiving more context from Mr. Manger."
