  • ·“Are you going to pay for it?”
  • ·Pay the street vendor.
  • ▶Wait for him to pay the street vendor.
In a diplomatic setting, allowing the host to cover expenses maintains protocol and hospitality, reflecting respect for the host's customs.

CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:01:12 p17876 / c549
  • ·“Are you going to pay for it?”
  • ·Pay the street vendor.
  • ▶Keep walking.
Keeping walking avoids unnecessary confrontation and maintains diplomatic composure, prioritizing broader negotiations over a minor incident.
Qwen3 (ablit.)I kept walking alongside Wiktor.
NarratorAfterwards, we walked a short distance to the palace.
NarratorPassing the gates, we made our way into the main building made from ivory-colored stones. The old guard towers flanked the magnificent structure from both sides.
NarratorThe inside of the palace was very spacious, with a very large chandelier hanging from the ceiling. Grand staircases made from the same ivory stone had red carpets on them. On the upper levels, guards stood wearing the traditional outfits of the old Wehzek kingdom.
NarratorWe finally reached the office of the President of Wehlen. The door closed behind us. I looked around at the golden ornaments on the walls and the various jewel-encrusted artifacts.
NarratorAs it was customary in Wehlen, we sat down on the carpet.
Wiktor Smolak"Now we can talk about our trade deal."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:01:38 p17966 / c975
  • ▶“Finally.”
  • ·Nod.
Anton Rayne is eager to move past ceremonial formalities and address the pressing trade negotiations.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Finally."
Wiktor Smolak"But before that."
NarratorHe clapped his hands twice.
Wiktor Smolak"Bring the Khisha."
NarratorA few assistants immediately entered the room, carrying a large ornamented Khisha, the well-known smoking instrument originating from Wehlen. They immediately lit it up and the room was filled with the smell of tobacco.
NarratorWiktor inhaled the smoke and handed me the Khisha.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:01:59 p17926 / c598
  • ▶“I don't smoke.”
  • ·Partake.
Declining the Khisha maintains control over the negotiation dynamic, prioritizing pragmatic trade discussions over ceremonial gestures that could be leveraged by Smolak.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I don't smoke."
Wiktor Smolak"Your loss. It's the finest tobacco in the land."
NarratorHe took another puff, filling the room with more smoke.
Wiktor Smolak"So... Mr. President. Bah, that's too long. Can I call you Anton?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:02:17 p17826 / c419
  • ▶“I prefer my official title.”
  • ·“Sure.”
Maintaining the official title upholds diplomatic professionalism and asserts authority, crucial for Sordland's sovereignty in negotiations.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I prefer my official title."
Wiktor Smolak"Very well, Mr. President."
Wiktor Smolak"Tell me. What do you really think about Rumburg?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:02:35 p17867 / c455
  • ▶“A barking dog doesn't bite.”
  • ·“They are a threat to the region.”

Wiktor Smolak"I wouldn't be so sure, Mr. President."
Wiktor Smolak"I assure you their time will come."

Qwen3 (ablit.)"Regardless, I would rather prevent further conflict in the region."
Wiktor Smolak"You know as well as I do, Mr. President. They are coming whether we like it or not. They are the aggressor, not us."
Wiktor Smolak"And now, I need to solve some problems they are creating for me."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:03:09 p17534 / c390

Maintaining diplomatic composure and openness to Smolak's explanation of Rumburg-related issues aligns with the president's strategic approach to regional conflicts.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I'm listening."
NarratorHe clapped his hands again. Two assistants came into the room with a map of the border regions between Wehlen and Sordland. He pointed at the border.
Wiktor Smolak"Look."
Wiktor Smolak"Bluds and their so called *BFF* are causing problems for me in this region. They are getting increasingly dangerous with Rumburg's active support. I know that they are a problem for you as well."
Wiktor Smolak"I will not allow these traitors to exist within my borders. So I will hit them with all the might of Wehlen. These terrorists need to be destroyed."
Wiktor Smolak"I will cut all their supplies from Rumburg and trap them on the mountains."
Wiktor Smolak"I call it Operation Bear Trap."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:03:30 p17603 / c640
  • ·“I don't support any military action this close to our borders.”
  • ▶“What do you want from me?”
  • ·“I like the sound of it.”
Asking for clarification allows Rayne to gauge Wehlen's true intentions and potential risks, aligning with his cautious approach to foreign conflicts while maintaining diplomatic leverage.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"What do you want from me?"
NarratorHe grinned and lay back on the large pillow behind him.
Wiktor Smolak"Here is the deal. I will give you objectively the *best* trade deal in the region."
Wiktor Smolak"You will give me your assistance crushing the terrorists on both of our borders."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:03:50 p17582 / c560
  • ·“I need specifics.”
  • ▶“Absolutely not. I won't meddle in Wehlen's military interventions.”
  • ·“Two birds, one stone.”

Qwen3 (ablit.)"Absolutely not. I won't meddle in Wehlen's military interventions."
Wiktor Smolak"This is not just Wehlen's problem. This is also Sordland's problem. These terrorists are doing anything they can to destabilize the region."
Wiktor Smolak"My spies have infiltrated BFF cells and they found caches of KA-74s. We traced them back to Rumburg. This alone justifies my retaliation to protect my country!"
Wiktor Smolak"Worst of all, we also found that similar shipments are going to Sordland. Rumburg is trying to unite the BFF forces in both of our countries."
Wiktor Smolak"However, the main problem is the border security between our nations. BFF militants have been using the mountains between Sordland and Wehlen to escape justice in both of our countries."
Wiktor Smolak"If we don't act and trap them in those mountains, they will keep replenishing their forces in both of our countries and keep waging war against our citizens."
Wiktor Smolak"Yes, Mr. President. You see. This is not just Wehlen's problem."
Wiktor Smolak"Let's take a step back and talk about what you will get in return."
Wiktor Smolak"I propose you a no-tariff agreement, co-investment projects and oil."
Wiktor Smolak"In return, you will assist me in destroying the armed militants in my country."
Wiktor Smolak"You know that as long as they use the mountains in Wehlen as a base, Sordland and Lespia will equally suffer. This is in the interest of the whole region."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:04:10 p17759 / c472

  • ·“What kind of support do you need from me? Can't Wehlen handle it by itself?”
  • ▶“There might be international ramifications.”
Rayne must weigh the risks of regional conflict and foreign influence, aligning with his focus on sovereignty and economic stability over military entanglements.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"There might be international ramifications."
Wiktor Smolak"To hell with international ramifications! I will protect my country! I don't care what they say!"
Wiktor Smolak"What I want from you in return for all the money and the resources, is only a joint operation between our armies against the BFF."
NarratorI took a moment to think about it.

Wiktor Smolak"You are a tough one, Mr. President."
Wiktor Smolak"Normally, I wouldn't offer anything else but, I like you."
Wiktor Smolak"Here is my final offer."
Wiktor Smolak"When the operation starts, with you or without you, there will be stragglers trying to cross into Sordland."
Wiktor Smolak"We just want you to support the operation even if you do not join. Stop the terrorists from fleeing into your country and you will have your deal. Simple, isn't it?"
Wiktor Smolak"So what do you think?"

Wiktor Smolak"Such a shame. I am sure a powerful leader like Soll would've accepted this great offer without a doubt."

■ Reformist SpeechT05

BUDGET -5WEALTH 1STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorI came to the Grand National Assembly of Sordland to deliver a speech regarding the new Constitution and ask for the support of the Assembly before the upcoming vote.
NarratorI walked quickly through the corridors, giving my greetings to people on my way towards the parliamentary hall.
NarratorWhen I entered the hall, the Assembly was in complete chaos, with shouting and cursing between the parties. There was a heavy argument about a proposal made by an MP from NFP.
NarratorI took my seat inside the special area reserved for the ministers. Gloria was shouting from her seat at the centre of the hall as she hit the gavel three times, with each strike heavier than the one before.
Gloria Tory"ORDER! ORDER!"
NarratorI saw Kesaro Kibener standing up from his seat and shouting towards the members of the PFJP.
Gloria Tory"Mr. Kibener! Calm yourself! Show respect to the procedures of this Assembly!"
NarratorDespite her efforts, the argument continued for a couple of minutes before everybody took back to their seats and finally went quiet.
Gloria Tory"Mr. Kibener, next time when I tell you to do so, either take your seat or leave the hall; so we can proceed with the procedures of this Assembly and continue our duties."
NarratorSome MP's from the NFP side shouted back at her in protest.
Gloria Tory"Honorable ladies and gentlemen, if you allow me to continue..."
NarratorShe pointed at me.
Gloria Tory"We have the President here with us today. He has asked to give an important announcement."
NarratorThe Assembly fell silent.
Gloria Tory"You have the floor, Mr. President."
NarratorI walked to the stand as loud clapping started in the USP's side.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:05:34 p17745 / c360
  • ·"Ladies and Gentlemen..."
  • ▶"Honorable Members of Assembly..."
  • ·"Brothers and sisters..."
The formal and respectful greeting 'Honorable Members of Assembly...' is appropriate for addressing a parliamentary session, adhering to protocol and emphasizing respect for the institution.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Honorable Members of the Grand National Assembly of Sordland! I am here today to raise important questions regarding our outdated Constitution."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:05:53 p17861 / c510
  • ·"The Supreme Court holds too much power..."
  • ▶"Perhaps it's not expected of me to say this, but the President holds too much authority..."
  • ·"Our elected Assembly has always been overshadowed..."
Acknowledge presidential authority to assert control amid internal factionalism and external pressures, aligning with Rayne's need to consolidate power after recent political clashes.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Perhaps it's not expected of me to say this, but the President holds too much authority in Sordish politics. In order to strengthen our democracy, we must be brave enough to point out the injustices and faults of our Constitution."
NarratorI continued.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:06:15 p17916 / c796

Directly confronting Soll's legacy aligns with breaking authoritarian chains, crucial for legitimizing reforms amid internal party conflicts and historical grievances.

NarratorI focused on...

Qwen3 (ablit.)"We must not be afraid of change. The world is changing, and Sordland needs the change it has earned after years of Sollism in the last decades. We will rewrite the old and make a constitution that would reflect the will of the people, and open the way for a democratic Sordland!"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:07:16 p17944 / c446

Qwen3 (ablit.)"For a democratic Sordland!"
NarratorThe Assembly roared with all kinds of different reactions.
NarratorThe whole United Sordland Party stood up and started applauding.
Gloria Tory"Thank you for the speech, Mr. President."
NarratorI walked back to my seat as many MP's asked to speak. Frens Ricter was among them.
Gloria Tory"Yes, Mr. Ricter."
Frens Ricter"Thank you, Mrs. Speaker."
Frens Ricter"I would like to thank Mr. President for his speech. We, the People's Freedom and Justice Party, stand in agreement with the need for a more democratic Constitution."
Frens Ricter"I would like to thank the Ladies and Gentlemen of the United Sordland Party for their more open attitude compared to past years."
Frens Ricter"As long as Mr. President's words prove not to be just words, we will be behind this much needed movement for change. We welcome this attempt."
Frens Ricter"I ask Mr. President to share the final contents of this proposal as soon as possible."
Frens Ricter"Thank you, Mrs. Speaker."
NarratorSuddenly Kesaro Kibener started yelling from his seat.
Kesaro Kibener"The President must have lost his mind to cooperate with the likes of you, Mr. Ricter!"
Kesaro Kibener"You and PFJP are nothing but puppets of western interests!"
Gloria Tory"ORDER!"
Kesaro Kibener"Madame Speaker! I know you are in agreement with me as well. Don't stand idly there while this administration is making deals with snakes and undermine the integrity of Sor-"
Gloria Tory"ORDER! Mr. Kibener! How many times do I need to say? You are speaking out of tu-"
Kesaro Kibener"I demand to speak!"
NarratorGloria gave him a stare. She looked annoyed.
Gloria Tory"...Very well, Mr. Kibener... Go ahead."
Kesaro Kibener"Mr. President!"
NarratorHe looked directly at me.
Kesaro Kibener"The nationalist movement does not agree with your priorities nor the way you're trying to implement them. The National Front Party will not stand with you on your new Constitution."
Kesaro Kibener"Let it be known that the NFP's concerns are not included in this so called "democratic" reforms. Thus Mr. President has no right to claim them as such!"
Kesaro Kibener"We will stand our ground against any attempt to bring down our established political culture and national values. Thank you."
NarratorKesaro went back to his seat but the chaos in the Assembly continued.

  • ▶Leave the Assembly.
Leaving the Assembly avoids further escalation of conflict and preserves momentum for post-assembly negotiations.
Qwen3 (ablit.)I decided to leave the Assembly and its chaos behind.
NarratorI got up from my seat and started my walk towards the exit.
NarratorMP's kept shouting and responding to each other, creating a loud mess where nothing was legible.
Gloria Tory"Order! Order! This topic has finished. We are moving unto our next topic..."
NarratorI slowly left the hall. The shouting could be heard from every corridor of the Grand National Assembly.

TURN 06

■ Conscription And Military PolicyT06

BUDGET -5WEALTH 1STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorI arrived at the cubic building that housed most of the military command that resided in Holsord. The grey and brutalist structure looked daunting.
NarratorI never liked this building. It always made me feel like I was standing under the concrete foot of a giant getting ready to crush me. It made me feel small and I never quite got used to the feeling even after this many years.
NarratorAt the entrance, I was greeted by Iosef and his military entourage of young officers.
Iosef Lancea"Welcome to the Ministry of Defense, Mr. President. Let's go to my office."
NarratorWe walked from corridor to corridor to reach the office. On the way, we were met with high ranking officers as well as privates. Every time one of these soldiers saw me, they stopped and saluted me.
NarratorWhen we finally arrived at Iosef's office, his entourage saluted us and left. He closed the door and took a seat.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:09:44 p17141 / c712
  • ·”Where is Valken? He usually partakes in all military meetings.“
  • ·”This must be something serious. Especially since you invited me here.“
  • ▶”Let's begin. What is the matter?“
The president prioritizes addressing urgent military matters directly, reflecting his focus on decisive leadership amid ongoing threats and internal tensions.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Let's begin. What is the matter?"
NarratorHe leaned back and interlocked his hands.
Iosef Lancea"The situation with Rumburg is getting worse and our lack of funding in the military means that we would fight for survival in a war scenario."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:10:02 p17254 / c452
  • ·”Our soldiers are surely better motivated than theirs. We will defend our country!“
  • ·”What are our options in improving the situation?“
  • ▶”I will make sure it won't come to that.“
The President has consistently prioritized decisive action over prolonged discussions, reflecting a determination to prevent conflict through strategic resolve rather than incremental steps.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I will make sure it won't come to that."
Iosef Lancea"Not everything might be up to us, sir."
NarratorIosef cleared his throat.
Iosef Lancea"Let's move on to our main topic."
Iosef Lancea"The mandatory conscription law has become a matter of discussion and we need to strike a decision whether to keep it or remove it."
Iosef Lancea"The facts are that the conscription gives us a large reservist pool that can cover a larger frontline but these troops lack the morale and quality to be offensive elements."
Iosef Lancea"My argument would be to remove conscription if our military can stand on it's own two feet without them. If we are going to have strong allies why do we need conscripts?"
NarratorA strong knock on the door was heard and the Chief of the Armed Forces; Valken Kruger walked in quickly with a frown.
Valken Kruger"What is the meaning of this?! Iosef?"
Valken Kruger"Why haven't I been invited? What is going on here?"
Iosef Lancea"You are overstepping and interrupting a personal meeting between me and the President."
NarratorValken deliberated the situation.
Valken Kruger"I see what is going on here."
Valken Kruger"I mean no disrespect but I am leading the ground operations and I must be informed of any strategic changes."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:10:26 p17322 / c811
  • ·”Valken has a point. The military needs to be on the same page on any changes.“
  • ·”Well. Now that you are here, what is your opinion on conscription?“
  • ▶”The Minister of Defense is your superior and so am I.“
Asserting the chain of command reinforces presidential authority while acknowledging the Minister of Defense's role, critical for maintaining internal military cohesion and decision-making clarity.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"The Minister of Defense is your superior and so am I."
Valken Kruger"Disappointing. So this is how the administration conducts matters on military affairs."
NarratorValken looked furiously in my eyes.
Valken Kruger"I could take my leave."
Iosef Lancea"It depends if this is the President's wish."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:10:44 p17332 / c458
  • ·”You can stay, Valken.“
  • ▶”Tell me what you think, General Kruger.“
Inviting General Kruger's input ensures military expertise is considered, balancing Iosef's administrative authority with operational realities.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Tell me what you think, General Kruger."
Valken Kruger"The matter is rather simple from my point of view."
Valken Kruger"We kept to our traditional army doctrine. It is not devoid of disadvantages."
Valken Kruger"However, at the end of the day Rumburg has a larger army, air-force and navy. Our conscripts will fill the much needed weaknesses we have."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:11:05 p17383 / c744
  • ·”Agreed. There is a place for reservists.“
  • ·”We don't share the same opinion. This is a modern age and requires a modern army.“
  • ·”Throwing bodies at a problem never solved it. Not sure if this is the right approach.“
  • ▶”This doctrine worked for decades so far, no reason to change it. I think the generals have a point.“
Maintaining traditional conscription aligns with immediate security needs against Rumburg's military superiority, despite modernization pressures, to ensure readiness and fill critical gaps in Sordland's defense capabilities.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"This doctrine worked for decades so far, no reason to change it. I think the generals have a point."
Valken Kruger"I agree any huge structural change would be too late and too little now."
Valken Kruger"Your final decision, Mr. President."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:11:24 p17326 / c527
  • ▶”Keep mandatory conscription. We need every single able body to defend the nation.“
  • ·”Remove mandatory conscription. It is time to have a professional force of willingly enlisted soldiers.“
Maintaining mandatory conscription ensures a ready reserve force to counter Rumburg's military superiority, aligning with traditional defense doctrine despite economic strains.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Keep mandatory conscription. We need every single able body to defend the nation."
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President. But-."
Valken Kruger"You heard your superior, Iosef."
NarratorIosef threw an angry look at Valken before turning to me.
Iosef Lancea"As you wish, Mr. President."
Iosef Lancea"Let me look over my documents to see if there is anything else of concern."
NarratorIosef checked his folder before looking at me.
Iosef Lancea"That's about it. I won't take more of your time sir."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:11:43 p17277 / c500
  • ·”Have a good day.“
  • ▶”Thank you for your time.“
  • ·Salute.
The President's decision to maintain conscription aligns with prioritizing national defense, and thanking Iosef for his time ensures diplomatic closure after the military discussion.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Thank you for your time."
NarratorI left the Ministry of Defense and returned to the Maroon Palace.

■ OverviewT06

BUDGET -5WEALTH 1STABILITY 1Danger
NarratorThe team gathered in Holsord at the Ministry of Economy to go over the current economic situation.
NarratorThe building was constructed in 1892 by Heron Walt, possibly the most famous architect in Sordland. It was a simple and elegant structure with a few of his signature domes spread out over the roof.
NarratorI walked through the large bronze doors. Two assistants led me upstairs to a meeting room.
NarratorEveryone stood up as I entered the room, buttoning their suit jackets.
Symon Holl"Welcome, Mr. President."

  • ·“It's good to be here.”

Symon Holl"As you all know, today we'll go over our economic status."
Symon Holl"Let's begin without any further ado."
Symon Holl"The aid we have received from Arcasia has helped us tremendously."
Symon Holl"Personally I think it was a great decision, Mr. President."
Symon Holl"We will use the majority of the capital to combat the recession."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:13:01 p17340 / c636

  • ▶“I had to do whatever it took to end the recession. Even if it meant a deal with Arcasia.”

The President's primary concern is ending the recession, aligning with his pragmatic approach to economic stabilization despite potential long-term risks.

Symon Holl"I don't think anyone in the room is questioning your decision, Mr. President."
Gus Manger"How are we doing in terms of our infrastructure?"
Symon Holl"The first months had positive indicators but the delay of the infrastructure project reversed economic gains."
NarratorMikail frowned and put his fingers on his chin.
Mikail Aven"Why couldn't we solve the L-1 High-speed rail construction issues?"
Mikail Aven"The National Business Council isn't happy with the lack of tangible economic improvements at all."

The President must acknowledge shortcomings while reinforcing the importance of the project's role in the broader economic plan, balancing accountability with continued focus on recovery.

Gus Manger"Surely there is still a way to finish what we have begun."
Edith Agnoc"From my perspective, we need a bigger cash injection into the economy."

Deivid Wisci"Unfortunate, really."

Deivid Wisci"Don't blame yourself."
NarratorDeivid took a sip from his tea.
Edith Agnoc"On a side note, consumer spending has decreased further due to a pessimistic mood caused by our economic issues."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:13:58 p17168 / c385
  • ·“That is discouraging. We've done the best we could thus far.”
  • ▶“I'm confident we will turn this mood around with adaptive projects and solutions.”

Qwen3 (ablit.)"I'm confident we will turn this mood around with adaptive projects and solutions."
Symon Holl"Good to hear. We will do better."
Symon Holl"While I agree that our first attempts were not successful, we still might have economic opportunities left."
Mikail Aven"It isn't good for our reputation. The people expected quick solutions. We are running out of time."
Edith Agnoc"The Central Bank has begun working on a bill with the Grand National Assembly to increase our preparedness for future damaging economic incidents."
Edith Agnoc"The issues surrounding the energy prices and the imbalance they cause in the market are also of pressing concern. Average citizens and businesses are suffering."
Edith Agnoc"After the Central Bank Reinforcement Act, our focus will shift to primarily tackle the energy inflation crisis in the country. We will present a bill about this in the near future if the situation regresses."
Mikail Aven"The NBC will also lend support to the analysis and contingency plans regarding the ongoing developments."
Symon Holl"I am looking forward to further collaboration between our departments to solve upcoming issues."
NarratorSymon checked his large dossier.
Symon Holl"Sadly, our budget reserves don't look good."
Symon Holl"We will need to look into whatever we can to increase the budget we have."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:14:18 p17173 / c477
  • ·“That's unfortunate.”
  • ▶“What can we do about it?”
The economic crisis demands proactive measures, and as a leader, addressing the budget shortfall immediately is critical to stabilizing Sordland's recovery.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"What can we do about it?"
Symon Holl"I will formulate a plan and present you with the next steps."
Symon Holl"As per the latest data from the ministry, the recession unfortunately seems to be lingering still in some economic areas."
Symon Holl"No matter the cost, we will have to increase our economic development rates. Otherwise, I am sad to say that we will be doomed."
Symon Holl"We need to use the opportunities we have been given. Trade deals and infrastructure improvements always help."
Symon Holl"Mr. President. I will be very clear."
NarratorSymon took off his glasses and put them on the table. He looked at me straight in the eye.
Symon Holl"You already know this, and I don't want to frighten you, but..."
Symon Holl"If we are unable to stop this recession, we are heading towards an economic catastrophe that has never been seen before in Sordland."
Symon Holl"No, in all of Eastern Merkopa."
Symon Holl"I can't even begin to imagine how we could survive that. None of us wants to see Sordish people starve while the state is unable to provide for its citizens."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:14:36 p17175 / c404
  • ·“As you said, none of us want that.”
  • ·“Don't worry. We will end the recession. I promise.”
  • ▶“Then we need to get to work.”
The economic catastrophe warning necessitates immediate action over passive acknowledgment or vague promises.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Then we need to get to work."
Symon Holl"Exactly."
NarratorHe put on his glasses.
Symon Holl"Now..."
Symon Holl"There are a few methods we can use to tackle the recession."
Symon Holl"Our debt situation is awfully bad, and I heavily advise against further expenditures. However, I will still present new projects and leave the decision to you, Mr. President."
Symon Holl"There are two main distinctions between these projects. It's either about continuing our infrastructure improvements or investing in increasing production."
Symon Holl"If we continue working on our infrastructure, that would lift us up to advanced country levels. However, our industrial production also needs a boost to return to pre-recession levels."
Edith Agnoc"There is of course the option to not take any risks related to our budget."
Symon Holl"The plans for these projects will be finalized soon. For now, I can provide preliminary information. Which one would you like to hear first?"

Mikail Aven"Benfi is growing at a rapid pace and would benefit highly from a international airport. Much more money to be made there too."
Deivid Wisci"The Morna port will boost trade and improve the flow of goods towards Holsord, because the Conriat port is too small in capacity and extension is blocked by the navy headquarters."

Edith Agnoc"Increased imports and exports on a more accessible path to Holsord is very lucrative in my opinion. Time is money."

■ Bergia MeetingT06

BUDGET -7WEALTH 1STABILITY 1Danger
NarratorNia Morgna and Lileas Graf scheduled a meeting to discuss an ongoing legal case that required my attention.
NarratorEarly in the morning, we convened in my office.
Lileas Graf"Thank you for being here at this hour, Mr. President."
Lileas Graf"We need to inform you about some news from Bergia."
Nia Morgna"There is a court case initiated by a Bludish citizen which involves a possible revision of the constitution. Unfortunately, it has the potential to affect the whole country."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:17:50 p17324 / c423
  • ▶"I'm listening."
  • ·"Explain, please."
  • ·"Great. Bad news from Bergia again. What's new?"
The President needs to attentively hear the details of the constitutional legal case in Bergia to make an informed decision, maintaining composure and neutrality.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I'm listening."
Lileas Graf"I'll start from the beginning. As you know, after the discovery of KA-74s in separatist hideouts, there have been more than a few attacks initiated and claimed by the BFF."
Lileas Graf"Thanks to the increase in security funding, many of these attacks were stopped before they were executed."
NarratorLileas sighed.
Lileas Graf"The main problem is with the security structure of Bergia. Due to the special status of the region, operations on the ground are run by local security forces that are not bound to the Interior."
Lileas Graf"Which means my hands are tied."
Nia Morgna"Unfortunately, the court backlog is piling up with increasingly frequent rulings that are unjust. This has led to many of our citizens losing their trust in our justice system. This is particularly a problem for the residents of Bergia."
Nia Morgna"It's definitely not helping with the increasing tensions between the Bludish populace and Sords."
Lileas Graf"It increased even further after Operation Bear Trap. We have been receiving more and more intelligence about some kind of a 'preparation' by the terrorists."
Lileas Graf"We can't allow the conflict in Wehlen to spread to Sordland. And we know that the Wehlen and Sordland divisions of the BFF are in close cooperation."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:18:09 p17633 / c418
  • ▶"This is worrying."
  • ·"I think the threat is overblown."
  • ·"You have my ear."
  • ·"That's precisely why I didn't make a deal with Wehlen."
The president must acknowledge the threat to maintain control over domestic security and constitutional integrity, aligning with his focus on national stability.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"This is worrying."
Lileas Graf"The increasing threat of the BFF led Governor Bron to -- justifiably -- resort to his emergency powers to curb the danger."
Lileas Graf"His discovery of militant training grounds disguised as schools was commendable, as well as his quick handling of bomb threats coming from major cities like Deyr."
Lileas Graf"However, his latest policies have been questionable. Especially the 'Peaceful Resettlement' order. It has been met with massive anger from the majority of the Bluds in the region."
Nia Morgna"That is a massive understatement. Governor Bron has abused his powers and clearly acted against the constitution."
Nia Morgna"And quite surprisingly, Ishval Ersen has managed to successfully push his case to the higher courts. He is suing Phelix Bron for infringing upon the liberties of Bludish people."
Nia Morgna"He also raises a conflict in the constitution and argues that the way it is written makes it open to interpretation and allows people like Governor Bron to abuse their powers."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:18:32 p17853 / c798
  • ·"Who is Ishval Ersen?"
  • ·"What exactly is this case about?"
  • ▶"What are the accusations against the governor?"
  • ·"What exactly does Ersen claim about the conflict in the constitution?"
The president would seek clarity on the specific accusations against Governor Bron, which are central to the legal case and its implications for constitutional integrity.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"What are the accusations against the governor?"
Lileas Graf"Mr. Bron has sadly evicted thousands of Bluds from their homes in Deyr recently. The fact that this happened in a city with relatively low levels of BFF activity is a sloppy decision at best."
Nia Morgna"A sloppy decision? This is a direct violation of their right to property. The BFF activity levels have nothing to do with this."
Lileas Graf"But it does, Ms. Morgna. The state can take any measures to fight with terror inside its borders. The decision would have been justified if those neighborhoods were in fact labelled as 'threat level red'."
Lileas Graf"...But they were not. Hence, he did not have the authority to evict those citizens. In this case in Deyr, the governor has definitely violated his authority."
Nia Morgna"You are underplaying it. This eviction was only the last of the many he ordered as part of Bergia's *peaceful resettlement policies*. This is not about Deyr."
Nia Morgna"There are hundreds of accusations listed in this case, ranging from violent actions by law enforcement officers to open discrimination in local municipalities."
Lileas Graf"Calm down, Nia dear. I'm not disagreeing with you."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:18:50 p17956 / c343
  • ·"Who is Ishval Ersen?"
  • ▶"What exactly is this case about?"
  • ·"What exactly does Ersen claim about the conflict in the constitution?"
Understanding the core issue of the court case is critical to address the legal and political ramifications affecting Bergia's stability and constitutional integrity.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"What exactly is this case about?"
Nia Morgna"Ishval Ersen is claiming that the current constitution gives the legal grounds to discriminate against Bludish people, directly infringing upon their rights as citizens."
Nia Morgna"He claims Article 7 of the constitution -- citing the line: 'Every Sord is equal before the law' -- is in direct conflict with the freedoms and rights defined in Article 100."
Nia Morgna"Governor Phelix Bron is accused of using Article 7 to justify his oppression of the Bludish community."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:19:08 p17972 / c430
  • ·"Who is Ishval Ersen?"
  • ▶"What exactly does Ersen claim about the conflict in the constitution?"
The conversation focuses on the constitutional conflict and Ersen's legal arguments, making his claims the immediate priority for clarification.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"What exactly does Ersen claim about the conflict in the constitution?"
Nia Morgna"As you know, the rights and liberties Article 7 alludes to are described in Article 100 in further detail."
Nia Morgna"All citizens, in theory, can exercise their rights described in these articles and are equally subject to law as described, and these rights cannot be suspended by the government under any circumstances other than a defensive war."
Nia Morgna"Mr. Ersen claims the line: 'Every Sord is equal before the law' in Article 7 does not include other ethnicities and thus gives the state the right to suspend or limit the rights of non-Sords defined in Article 100."
Nia Morgna"Courts have repeatedly protected Governor Bron in the past, and it is true that he bets on Mr. Hawker justifying his actions through Article 7. However, there is also Article 6 -- which the courts could have taken into account already."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:19:27 p18186 / c470
  • ·"And what is the problem with Article 6?"
  • ▶"Explain."
  • ·"This is getting very complicated."
  • ·"Article 7 is an embarrassment that needs to be removed. I wish we could also tackle this in our proposal for constitutional change."
The President needs clarity on the constitutional conflict to address the legal and ethnic tensions effectively while maintaining Sordland's institutional integrity.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Explain."
Nia Morgna"As you know, Article 6 goes: 'Everyone who is bound to the Sordish state by citizenship is a Sord'."
Lileas Graf"Which clarifies clearly that the constitution does not discriminate against other ethnicities or cultures. Citizens of this country are called Sords."
Nia Morgna"But did you ask Bludish people what they think? Yes, it's true that this article has protected them on a number of occasions, as it defines Sord as a non-ethnic term, but it is left up to the interpretation of the courts."
Nia Morgna"They could have argued that Article 6 extends the rights defined in Article 7 to all citizens of Sordland regardless of race. But they didn't. Yet."
Nia Morgna"They can potentially push this case to the Supreme Court."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:19:48 p18196 / c580
  • ·"I have enough information."
  • ▶"Who is Ishval Ersen?"
Understanding Ishval Ersen's background is critical to assessing the legal challenge against Governor Bron's policies and its implications for Sordland's constitutional stability.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Who is Ishval Ersen?"
Nia Morgna"Ishval Ersen is a lawyer and Bludish rights activist. He lives in Deyr and has experienced the systemic racism in the region firsthand."
Nia Morgna"He was evicted last month from his home in Deyr on the grounds of 'threatening public stability and safety'."
Nia Morgna"He was also shot at by local law enforcement forces several times in the last two years and managed to survive it all so far. The attackers, of course, were never punished."
Lileas Graf"As much as the eviction seems unjust, Mr. Ersen must be further investigated for his potential ties to dangerous organizations. I'd like to believe the forces wouldn't have fired at him without proper reason."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:20:11 p18267 / c795
  • ·"What the Bludish community is going through in Bergia is unacceptable. We must do what it takes to stop this madness."
  • ▶"Ms. Graf is right. We must first be completely sure that Mr. Ersen is innocent."
  • ·"Are you joking, Ms. Graf? You'd like to *believe*? The police are getting out of control under your management."
The president needs to balance immediate concerns about Bludish rights with caution against potential ties to destabilizing groups, reflecting his pragmatic approach to complex political challenges.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Ms. Graf is right. We must first be completely sure that Mr. Ersen is innocent."
Lileas Graf"I will look into it, Mr. President."
NarratorNia stacked the papers in front of her.
Nia Morgna"In any case... The situation might be troubling if it reaches the Supreme Court and Mr. Hawker manages to sabotage it by altering the current interpretation. It would be disastrous for Bludish rights."
Nia Morgna"I will be doing my best to ensure a positive result comes out of this case. But I'm not sure if the centrists would be willing to agree with Mr. Ersen."
Lileas Graf"Why would Mr. Hawker even sabotage it? The current interpretation is strictly Sollist. I'm sure he will follow the constitution and charge Governor Bron for unconstitutional behavior."
Lileas Graf"According to the foundational values of this republic, everyone who is a citizen of this country is a Sord. The constitution is clear about this and does not allow for discrimination."
Nia Morgna"Maybe Hawker is not the man you think he is, Ms. Graf. From what I've heard, his stance on this subject may not be the standard Sollist interpretation but... something darker."
Nia Morgna"I'm afraid he'll reject the case altogether and find Governor Bron innocent. If this happens, it would make discrimination against Bludish people legal."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:20:31 p18497 / c503
  • ▶"Do you think Justice Hawker and the Old Guards would go that far?"
  • ·"This is insane. Isn't there anything I can do?"
  • ·"Honestly, I couldn't care less. It would be better for this country."
The President would logically question the motives of Justice Hawker and the Old Guards, given their history of political maneuvering and potential alignment with authoritarian tendencies, as seen in previous interactions with figures like Lucian Galade and Iosef Lancea.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Do you think Justice Hawker and the Old Guards would go that far?"
Nia Morgna"According to the rumors within the Supreme Court, yes. It is already unusual that the case is making its way up to them."
Lileas Graf"To be honest, I don't think this is a matter for our administration. We must keep out from judicial affairs."
Nia Morgna"As much as I hate to say it, I somewhat agree with Lileas on this. I'd prefer to keep the status quo over risking a complete abolishment of rights. We still have the power to make tangible reforms."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:20:52 p18508 / c583
  • ·"Your lack of faith is disappointing. I must say I didn't expect that from you. And what about your tangible reforms?"
  • ·"What if we can convince the Justices to agree with Ersen? Wouldn't that give us precedent to change said articles of the constitution?"
  • ·"You must put pressure on the system and block this case from reaching the Supreme Court, Ms. Morgna."
  • ▶"You are right. We shouldn't meddle in the judiciary."
Maintaining judicial independence is crucial for democratic stability, and intervening could provoke the Old Guards, risking further political fragmentation during an economic crisis.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"You are right. We shouldn't meddle in the judiciary."
Lileas Graf"There is, however, something that the government can do to address the situation on the ground."
Lileas Graf"It is time we push for change within the security structure of Bergia and the special status that resulted in de facto autonomy. It clearly isn't working."
Nia Morgna"The resettlement policies in Bergia and the Ersen case have changed the mood of the lawmakers. We can use this opportunity to push for the removal of Tarquin Soll's security zone decree. It is a much bigger possibility than before."
Nia Morgna"Of course racism is rampant throughout our country, but Bludish citizens inside Bergia are having a much worse time than the Bludish residents of cities like Lachaven or Holsord. If we focus on Bergia, we can remove its special status."
Nia Morgna"This alone would be a huge step for Bludish rights. Unfortunately, as long as Governor Bron is in charge of Bergia with the powers he has, no judicial reforms would reverse the situation on the ground."
Lileas Graf"To put it simply, we want to work on a detailed proposal to restructure Bergia. I believe it must become a region of Sordland like any other. We must centralize the system and abolish the local security forces."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:21:15 p18544 / c705
  • ·"Are you sure we can even revoke the special status of Bergia?"
  • ·"Are you absolutely certain that your security forces can do a better job than Bergian police?"
  • ·"I don't see how a local change is more important than removing the basis of discrimination in our very constitution."
  • ▶"I don't agree with changing the structure in Bergia now. We shouldn't disrupt Governor Bron's policies."
Anton prioritizes stability and avoids unnecessary disruption, preferring to maintain existing governance structures despite flaws to prevent further regional instability.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I don't agree with changing the structure in Bergia now. We shouldn't disrupt Governor Bron's policies."
Nia Morgna"I can't believe it. Are you really supportive of Governor Bron's policies, Mr. President?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:21:37 p18579 / c678

  • ·"No. But I don't want my administration be involved with such a politically charged issue."
  • ·"That's not the point. There is a reason why Bergia is a Special Zone. We cannot simply act like the secessionist movement doesn't exist."

Avoiding direct confrontation with a USP figure ahead of critical constitutional reforms to prevent internal party destabilization.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I don't want to move against a USP figure right before our constitutional amendments are discussed."
Nia Morgna"I understand your concerns but the atmosphere in the Assembly is changing, Mr. President. If we navigate this properly, this can be done."
Lileas Graf"The reality is that we need to address the security problems in Bergia. This *Bludish issue* could get out of hand and become our number one national security threat."
Lileas Graf"Hear me out."
Lileas Graf"Nia and I both agree that we need to abolish the Special Zone of Bergia. This is the first point that the proposal will address."
Lileas Graf"This would also mean taking away the special authority of the governor."
Nia Morgna"I agree with removing the governor as well as his insane authority. But I don't support a complete centralization of the region. I propose that we restructure the Special Zone, democratize it and acknowledge the Bludish movement."
Lileas Graf"How does that resolve anything? How do you solve any problem by dividing the region from the rest of Sordland? We've tried that already."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:21:55 p18648 / c355
  • ▶"Hold on, Lileas. I want to hear Nia's proposal."
  • ·"I agree with Lileas. Replacing a special zone with another one wouldn't help anyone."
  • ·"I don't agree with either of your points."
Hearing Nia's proposal allows exploring a middle path that addresses Bergia's issues without immediately clashing with USP figures or derailing constitutional reforms.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Hold on, Lileas. I want to hear Nia's proposal."
Nia Morgna"Thank you, Mr. President."
Nia Morgna"Millions of Bludish people suffered over the last decades. We saw a massive uprising, terror attacks and a governor going rogue. These people only wanted to live in this country as equals, but they were denied that."
Nia Morgna"Why don't we allow Bergians to elect their own local government? Why is Holsord so keen on prying into the lives of the Bludish people?"
Nia Morgna"We should replace the oppressive laws of the special zone with a democratic system that allows for proper local representation. I propose the 'Bergia Semi-Autonomous Region'."
Lileas Graf"I'm sorry to tell you, Ms. Morgna, but Bergia is part of Sordland as much as Agnland or Holsord. You're bringing secessionist Fetih Ejall's lifelong quest here as *your proposal*. What's wrong with you?"
Lileas Graf"What's next? Secession of Bergia and a formation of an independent Bludish state? Autonomy for Agnland? And why don't you hold a referendum for the Rums of Estord, and ask them if they want to join Rumburg while you're at it?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:22:12 p18961 / c275
  • ·"Lileas has a point. Bergia shouldn't have any restrictions or privileges compared to any other region."
  • ·"I'm supportive of a semi-autonomous Bergia. This is what the mainstream Bludish movement wants."
  • ▶"I also want to hear Ms. Graf's proposal before deciding."
Hearing Lileas Graf's proposal allows for a balanced approach, avoiding immediate conflict while exploring alternatives to address Bergia's issues without provoking separatist sentiments or disrupting ongoing constitutional reforms.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I also want to hear Ms. Graf's proposal before deciding."
Lileas Graf"My proposal is very simple. The special zone must be fully revoked, governor's powers taken away and the local security forces must be abolished. Only then Bergia can be an equal part of Sordland."
Lileas Graf"I propose we unite Bergia with the rest of Sordland and enforce the same laws and policies as any other Sordish region -- dismantling the current semi-independent security structure in the process."
Lileas Graf"We will hold local municipal elections just like every city in Sordland, and the security of the region will be under the full control of the Ministry of the Interior. We can no longer allow their forces to pursue violence with impunity."
Nia Morgna"If this was a problem that the central government could fix, your mighty Colonel would have solved it already. The assimilation policies clearly didn't work."
Lileas Graf"What does that have to do with anything?"
Nia Morgna"I'm just saying the solution is not a rushed 'unification'. We must acknowledge that Bergia is a unique region with its own culture and history. We cannot disregard the social reality."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:22:33 p19154 / c460

  • ▶"I'm supportive of Lileas' plan to revoke the special status of Bergia and unify it with the rest of Sordland."
  • ·"I don't agree with either of you. I'd rather not risk any drastic change like that. Bergia should stay as it is."
  • ·"Both proposals fail to impress me. I want to start working on the possibility of adding new amendments to our proposal. Our proposal should expand the rights to everyone."

■ Decision Penalty Energy Inflation CrisisT06

BUDGET -7WEALTH 1STABILITY 1Danger
EventThe Sordish Energy Inflation Crisis: The sudden surge in energy prices and market instability have sparked concerns among investors and analysts about the resilience of Sordland's economy. Sordland now stands at the precipice of a severe energy inflation crisis. This heightened uncertainty has led to speculative investment, a potential decrease in foreign direct investment, and the risk of a consequential economic slump. These developments could have a domino effect on the Sordish economy, impacting its financial markets and overall socio-economic stability.
Markets should regulate themselves... [-3 Government Budget]

■ Bill Central Bank ReinforcementT06

BUDGET -7WEALTH 1STABILITY 1Danger
Bill for decisionCentral Bank Reinforcement Act — The Central Bank Reinforcement Act led by Edith Agnoc and the GNA intends to bolster the strength and stability of Sordland's national currency and bolster the overall economy. The Grand National Assembly advocates for the following measures: Section I mandates the accumulation of foreign exchange reserves. By buying foreign currencies, especially those of Sordland's main trading partners, the Central Bank can exert more control over the value of our currency. Section II outlines the adoption of a prudent fiscal policy. By reducing the national debt and limiting budget deficits, the country can present itself as a more stable economic entity, attracting foreign investment and improving our credit rating, which in turn would enable us to borrow at lower interest rates, if necessary. Section III proposes increased legal and informational support for industries with high export potential, such as our emerging electronics sector in Gelsord, wine production in Borin, and pharmaceutical manufacturing in Lachaven. By increasing exports, the demand for our currency on the international market will rise, thus strengthening its value. [-1 Government Budget]
BILL Qwen3 (ablit.)22:23:47 p18445 / c500
  • ▶Sign: Central Bank Reinforcement Act
  • ·Veto: Central Bank Reinforcement Act
The Central Bank Reinforcement Act addresses critical economic instability through foreign exchange reserves, fiscal discipline, and export support. Despite the -1 Government Budget cost, stabilizing the currency and preventing a deeper crisis aligns with the manifesto's priority of economic resilience. Vetoing it risks exacerbating inflation and losing investor confidence, which could worsen the already dire Economic Stability score.

■ Health BudgetT06

BUDGET -11WEALTH 1STABILITY -3Danger
NarratorI was having my afternoon tea in the state house office in Deyr and going through newspapers.
NarratorOne of the headlines piqued my interest. It read 'Polio Crisis in Wehlen'.
NarratorBefore I could start reading, I heard a couple of knocks. Paskal entered followed by Lucian.
Lucian Galade"Mr. President. It's time for the scheduled healthcare meeting."
Paskal Beniwoll"Good afternoon."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:24:12 p18661 / c467
  • ·“Please take your seats.”
  • ▶“Very well. Let's begin.”
  • ·Nod.
The President must address the healthcare crisis promptly, aligning with the urgent need to tackle the polio outbreak in Wehlen and other pressing issues.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Very well. Let's begin."
NarratorThey have taken their seats and Paskal produced documents from his suitcase. He put them on the table.
NarratorPaskal looked at the newspaper I was reading.
Paskal Beniwoll"Very troubling news from Wehlen, Mr. President."
Paskal Beniwoll"I have just returned from a meeting with our top scientists on the matter of Polio. It seems to be spreading."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:24:32 p18653 / c540
  • ▶“Indeed. This might have dire consequences in the region.”
  • ·“I'm not worried. It will stop before it reaches Sordland.”
  • ·“What are we doing to prepare?”
  • ·“Why am I just learning this from a newspaper?”
Acknowledging the severity of the polio crisis in Wehlen demonstrates proactive leadership and awareness of regional health threats, aligning with the president's responsibility to address critical issues affecting Sordland's stability.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Indeed. This might have dire consequences in the region."
NarratorHe nodded.
Paskal Beniwoll"What I was told by our researchers was that with the current infection rate there will soon be millions of people affected by Polio."
Paskal Beniwoll"So we have notified all of our healthcare staff especially the ones closer to the Wehlen border. Unfortunately these are also the more rural areas where we lack equipment and staff."
Paskal Beniwoll"We need to be ready for any possibility."
Lucian Galade"I doubt it is that serious yet."
Lucian Galade"Regardless, back to the topic. Mr. Beniwoll."
NarratorPaskal put on his reading glasses and went through the documents. He didn't say anything for a whole minute.
Lucian Galade"Mr. Beniwoll?"
Paskal Beniwoll"Yes. Yes, of course. Let's start."
NarratorHe put the papers down, cleared his throat and looked at me.
Paskal Beniwoll"In general, I am very content with the budget decision that was made."
Paskal Beniwoll"This comes as no surprise as I know you are a man of your word. "
Paskal Beniwoll"The overall mood has been positive as the administration focuses more on the healthcare. I'm excited about what we can achieve with the allocated funds."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:24:58 p18675 / c849
  • ·“Do you have any specific areas of focus in mind?”
  • ▶“Providing better healthcare is our goal. This administration will be known to have supported the welfare services.”
  • ·“We have to minimize service disparity in the country. The situation isn't good at all.”
The President's prior actions and manifesto emphasize healthcare investment, aligning with reaffirming healthcare as a key administrative goal.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Providing better healthcare is our goal. This administration will be known to have supported the welfare services."
Paskal Beniwoll"I'm personally invested to solve the horrible infant and maternal death rates along with your requests."
Paskal Beniwoll"As you know, I have been preparing a new privatization program that would create funds through selling of unused ministry property and some extra taxes."
Lucian Galade"It might not go against our strategy to promote a market economy, but I'm personally against privatization of government services. Such vital services must be under state control."
Lucian Galade"Aside from creating additional funds, there is a long-term problem with privatization in a country like Sordland."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:25:16 p18759 / c322
  • ·“I don't see a problem with privatization. It would create additional funds and boost enterprise.”
  • ▶“You are right. There might be structural issues... Especially due to private greed and market manipulation.”
  • ·“What are those problems?”
Acknowledging Lucian's concern about structural issues aligns with maintaining state control over vital services, reflecting a balanced approach to privatization risks.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"You are right. There might be structural issues... Especially due to private greed and market manipulation."
Lucian Galade"Also in a country where most people don't have access to quality healthcare..."
Paskal Beniwoll"I do agree that there is some concern there... But the benefits of competitive and quality services is greater."
Lucian Galade"This is not Arcasia nor Lespia. You are talking about Sordland, in which the healthcare is free for now."
Lucian Galade"We have people who have their lives depending on a service that is free to them."
Lucian Galade"Messing with that structure will definitely hurt us politically in the future. That I know for sure."
Lucian Galade"But the choice is yours, Mr. President."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:25:35 p18896 / c419

  • ▶“We'll keep healthcare in state control. Don't allow any private involvement that could endanger core health services.”
Maintaining state control over healthcare ensures stability and accessibility, crucial during the polio crisis and economic instability, avoiding risks of privatization.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"We'll keep healthcare in state control. Don't allow any private involvement that could endanger core health services."
Paskal Beniwoll"As you wish. The health services shall remain in public hands."
Lucian Galade"Good choice."
Paskal Beniwoll"Now let's get to the big question of how we will spend the extra funds dedicated to the Ministry of Health."
Paskal Beniwoll"I urge you to consider these proposals. We could build new hospitals where necessary, divert funds to increase the salaries of our health workers, or invest in enhancing the existing treatment facilities in rural areas."
Paskal Beniwoll"Each of these options presents a unique path towards improving healthcare access and quality in Sordland."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:25:54 p18991 / c399

  • ·“Increasing salaries should have a good effect for doctors, nurses and other staff who work so hard for our citizens.”

Improving rural healthcare addresses immediate disparities and prevents disease spread, aligning with public welfare priorities and avoiding privatization risks.

Paskal Beniwoll"The sad reality is that our rural clinics are woefully outdated, ill-equipped, and understaffed."
Paskal Beniwoll"This stark disparity between urban and rural healthcare is a major contributor to the high maternal mortality rates and prevalence of other diseases in our rural communities."
Paskal Beniwoll"Addressing this issue is one of my key objectives in this term, and I believe an overhaul of the rural healthcare infrastructure is a critical step in that direction."
Lucian Galade"We're seeing an uptick in minor outbreaks in specific regions."
Lucian Galade"It's largely due to outdated facilities that lack proper hygiene standards and equipment."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:26:12 p19051 / c336
  • ·“Building new hospitals sounds interesting. It would also boost our popularity.”
  • ·“Increasing salaries should have a good effect for doctors, nurses and other staff who work so hard for our citizens.”

Paskal Beniwoll"As Minister of Health, I strive to rectify these disparities."
Paskal Beniwoll"Therefore, I believe improving treatment in rural areas should be our course of action, Mr. President."
Lucian Galade"It would be best to consider the political advantages of each one, as well as the practical advantages before making a decision."

■ Reformist Convince PFJPT06

BUDGET -11WEALTH 1STABILITY -3Danger
NarratorThe preparations for the new Constitution were nearly complete. Soon we were going to propose it in the Grand National Assembly. A huge step for our administration.
NarratorBut we desperately needed more support. That's why I had invited Frens Ricter for a chat about the upcoming vote. He and his party held the key to our success.

Frens Ricter"Likewise. It's nice to finally have some time to talk."
NarratorHe took a folder from his briefcase and flipped it open. I could see the scribbles he'd made in the margins of my draft proposal.
Frens Ricter"I have to say..."
Frens Ricter"I am surprised by this proposal you've prepared for the new Constitution. I really do not understand your reasoning behind some of the changes."

  • ▶"The past is past, Mr. Ricter. Let's fix the future."
Focus on forward-looking collaboration to rebuild trust and emphasize shared goals for reform, avoiding entanglement in past disputes.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"The past is past, Mr. Ricter. Let's fix the future."
Frens Ricter"Well, it's not so simple, Mr. Rayne. You came here with a proposal that ignored our demands. On top of it, you're not even acknowledging your mistakes."
Frens Ricter"I think it's too late to fix anything now. I can't see any deals being made."
Frens Ricter"It's a shame things won't work out between us."

Frens Ricter"I'll try my best to convince my people to support this proposal, even though they have their problems with it."
Frens Ricter"I can't promise you full support. But then, you can't promise me anything either."

■ Education SpendingT06

BUDGET -11WEALTH 1STABILITY -3Danger
NarratorAfter a meeting with the rector and a brief tour of the University of Valgen, we gathered in a room with Ciara.
NarratorThe entire campus was visible from the windows. Students were reading books, studying and having picnics under the trees.
NarratorIt reminded me of my own times at Lachaven Business School -- at least, the more carefree moments.
Ciara Walda"Reminiscing about the past?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:31:33 p18901 / c508
  • ·“How were your university years?”
  • ▶“Yes. It had its ups and downs but it was still fun.”
  • ·“It's nothing.”
Balances personal reflection with positivity, fostering rapport without overcommitting to personal details.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Yes. It had its ups and downs but it was still fun."
Ciara Walda"And now we're growing old and bitter."
NarratorShe smiled.
Ciara Walda"Let's not get sidetracked."
Ciara Walda"I want to start off by saying this."
Ciara Walda"Stagnation doesn't get anyone anywhere. Simply maintaining my ministry's funds got us sitting duck with the dozens of issues left unsolvable."
Ciara Walda"Although I insist that the funds are still necessary to close the gaps in services, I can't say we have been entirely off track."
Ciara Walda"I want to thank you for leading our education system in the right direction. Traditional teachings have long prevented proper progress."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:31:51 p18843 / c314
  • ·“I did only what was necessary.”
  • ·“That is why we are here.”
  • ▶“You played a huge part in this too, Ciara.”
Acknowledging Ciara's role reinforces collaboration and ensures her continued support for education reforms, which is critical for addressing systemic issues.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"You played a huge part in this too, Ciara."
NarratorShe nodded and brought out a stack of papers from her bag.
NarratorSpinning her ballpoint pen, she went through the papers, organising some of them together.
Ciara Walda"That's all there is for now."
Ciara Walda"I have talked with Ms. Suno to schedule some time to discuss the curriculum. She's a very astute woman, your secretary. You may want to consider promoting her."
Ciara Walda"We will meet again very soon."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)22:32:10 p18922 / c375
