Qwen3 (ablit.)I handed the sword back to the Archpriest. He took it and passed it to a disciple.
NarratorI got up and proceeded to my designated spot next to Lucian. He leaned over and whispered in my ear.
Lucian Galade"Unfortunately that was not quite correct, sir."
NarratorMy eyes met the Archpriest's and he very slightly shook his head.
NarratorThe ceremony went on for another hour and a half. Finally, the noon bells chimed, marking the end of the cathedral service.
NarratorWe left the cathedral to greet the waiting crowd.
NarratorAs I walked outside, I heard scattered boos. A man in a Worker's Party of Bludia shirt shouted that I was only here for show, that I couldn't even bother to get the ceremony right. Others yelled in agreement.
Lucian Galade"We should go before things get heated."
NarratorWe entered our cars and drove back to our suites in Deyr.

■ Personal University ChoiceT07

BUDGET -15WEALTH 0STABILITY 0Danger
NarratorI arrived home from work, hung my jacket up, and put my briefcase in the hall.
NarratorI paused, awaiting my usual greeting from my wife and daughter. Deana was out at one of her after-school art classes, I remembered. But where was Monica?
NarratorFrom the living room, I heard a muffled sob.
NarratorI entered to find Monica sitting on the sofa, a letter in her hands and a look of despair on her face.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:19:04 p16625 / c279
  • ▶"Darling, what's wrong?"
  • ·"Who is it from?"
  • ·Sit down next to Monica.
The president should first inquire about Monica's distress to address her immediate emotional state and understand the situation before delving into specifics.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Darling, what's wrong?"
NarratorMonica briefly rested her head on my shoulder, then handed me the letter.
Monica"It's from the Department of Education. Franc's university exam results are in."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:19:20 p16698 / c301
  • ·"He must have made us proud."
  • ·"How did he do?"
  • ·"Why are you so worried, then?"
  • ▶"He failed, didn't he?"
Monica's despair and the context of the letter suggest Franc's poor performance, aligning with the dire emotional state described.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"He failed, didn't he?"
Monica"Just read the letter."
NarratorI looked at the page. Below Franc's name and address, his score was written: 435 points, out of a total of 1000.
NarratorAt the bottom of the letter, the word "Failed" was stamped in red.
Monica"All those years of effort, all those private lessons... all down the drain."
Monica"You know what this means -- he's going to have to join the military. He could die, Anton!"

Petr Vectern"...And here's our president!"
NarratorHe held out my chair for me to sit down and smiled.
Petr Vectern"Please."
NarratorI sat down.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:23:35 p16344 / c403
  • ·Let Petr talk.
  • ▶"What's on our agenda?"
Asserting control over the meeting's direction to address critical national issues amid political tensions and ensure actionable outcomes.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"What's on our agenda?"
Petr Vectern"We'll be discussing our current status and planning the remainder of our term."
NarratorPetr walked to his seat but didn't sit down. He took a look at the ministers.
Petr Vectern"Now that Mr. President is here, we can begin the meeting. I know that some of you have had your concerns about our style of governance so far. There will be a time to speak up about these in the meeting today."
Petr Vectern"But I must also thank you all for your hard work so far. We are very lucky to have a team like this. All of you have been invaluable to this administration."
NarratorNia and Ciara grimaced at his remarks.
Petr Vectern"Having said that... Now we have reached a critical point of our term. We are nearing the end."
Petr Vectern"We have worked long and hard but we couldn't reform the Sordish Constitution..."
Petr Vectern"Our organization was flawed. Our party was not united. The very parliament we control has denied us the reforms."
Petr Vectern"Lucian and I have begun investigating who let us down at the vote when we needed them the most."
Petr Vectern"Kesaro Kibener and the National Front MP's didn't like the proposal and voted against it."
Petr Vectern"The Lotherberg group led a massive lobbying effort against us in the Assembly which caused us to lose many votes."
NarratorHe paused and looked around the room.
Petr Vectern"But the past is past. We must now look towards our future."
Petr Vectern"We are investigating and analyzing our faults. We will be taking measures accordingly."
NarratorHe glanced at both Lucian and me.
Petr Vectern"Our party still remains divided. In order to look at the future, we may need to make some changes."
Petr Vectern"On the other hand, the opposition is exploiting our failure to reform. The latest polls show a steep increase in the opposition's support."
Petr Vectern"So... Let's start the meeting with a brief look at some reports."
NarratorHe fumbled with the stack of papers in front of him.
Petr Vectern"With the latest developments, we have seen an enormous decrease in our popularity. The public opinion about our party is at an all-time low."
NarratorHe took out a piece of paper from the stack and inspected it.
Petr Vectern"A major concern of the people is the state of our economy. We are slowly slipping into a depression."
Petr Vectern"This will have dire consequences for our administration."
Petr Vectern"We mustn't lose hope and give it our best. Sordland needs us more than ever."
NarratorPetr turned to me.
Petr Vectern"I'll give the word to the President now."
NarratorHe sat down.

NarratorPetr gestured at Symon and he immediately stood up.
Symon Holl"Mr. President. Dear colleagues."
Symon Holl"Mr. Vice President was pretty on point with his summary of the current economic status."
Symon Holl"He also used the word 'depression.' He couldn't have been more correct. Our GDP has shrank by 15 percent last year."
Symon Holl"This downward trend has broken our economy over the last years. If we cannot contain this in time, we will end our term -- in the truest of sense of the word -- in a depression."
Symon Holl"However, this has not yet reflected well with our debt situation. Our total national debt has increased about 85 billion SR this year. If we maintain such a deficit over the next years we will face a massive debt crisis which could crash the economy."
Symon Holl"The assembly is working on introducing new tax bills which will generate more revenue for the government. These efforts will depend on the government deficit situation."
Symon Holl"On the other hand, unemployment has seen no positive changes. There has been an increase in joblessness. It is now at 26 percent. We have lost control of the situation."
Symon Holl"We caused millions of people to lose their jobs."

  • ▶"Together, as a country, there is no end to what we can achieve."
Emphasizes national unity and collective resilience, fostering public solidarity amid political setbacks.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Together, as a country, there is no end to what we can achieve."
NarratorI took a deep breath. Lucian shooed a fly away from my forehead.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:31:21 p17022 / c376
  • ▶"We will start by focusing on the economy."
  • ·"With reform now off the table, I shall keep following through on my promise to improve Sordland's healthcare system."
  • ·"We will start by focusing on eliminating internal and external threats."
The economy is in critical danger, with a budget deficit and high unemployment, requiring immediate focus to stabilize Sordland and prevent further crisis.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"We will start by focusing on the economy."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:31:42 p17115 / c788
  • ·"I thank you for standing by me, your president. Both in good times and bad."
  • ·"I'd like to thank my wife and children for standing by me."
  • ▶"I'd like to thank my Vice President, Petr Vectern, for standing by me."
  • ·"I'd like to thank my chief strategist Lucian Galade for standing by me."
Acknowledging Petr Vectern reinforces political solidarity and highlights his role as a key ally during challenges, aligning with the need to project unity and leadership.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I'd like to thank my Vice President, Petr Vectern, for standing by me."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:31:58 p17141 / c361
  • ·"A'Morgna wes core! Vectern sis da!"
  • ·"God bless Sordland."
  • ▶"That is all. Thank you."
  • ·"I salute you all with respect."
The speech emphasizes resilience and moving forward, requiring a formal yet concise closing to acknowledge the audience and conclude the address professionally.

NarratorThe director gave the cut signal, and the cameraman stopped filming. My speech was finished.

TURN 08

■ Personal Franc LeavesT08

BUDGET -15WEALTH 0STABILITY 0Danger
NarratorIt was a cloudy, rainy day in Holsord.
NarratorThe gloomy weather echoed my own feelings. My constitutional changes had been defeated. I was back to square one.
NarratorI couldn't spend the day moping, however. Franc was finally about to leave home.
NarratorMy son piled the last of his bags next to the door. Serge and the assistant driver were waiting outside next to the car.
Monica"Did you pack everything?"
Monica"Don't forget to give me a call *immediately* after you arrive."
Franc"I will. I think I'm all packed."
NarratorMonica helped him put his backpack on while Serge started carrying the luggage to the car.
Monica"I made you a sack lunch! I'll get it from the kitchen."
NarratorShe dashed down the hall. Franc and I turned to face each other in the doorway.
Franc"So, I guess this is it."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:32:32 p17168 / c412
  • ·"I guess it is."
  • ·"I know studying abroad wasn't your first choice. But I hope you can live with it."
  • ▶"Don't forget where you came from, son."
The president would emphasize maintaining familial and national roots despite the son's departure, reflecting his character's focus on heritage and responsibility.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Don't forget where you came from, son."
Franc"How could I? I'm the President's son!"
NarratorFranc shifted his weight nervously.
Franc"You still never told me about your university years, but I know I've had it easy compared to you. I hope moving abroad gives me a chance to prove myself the same way you did."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:32:49 p17240 / c429
  • ·"For your sake, I hope that chance never comes."
  • ·"Just don't do anything stupid."
  • ▶"Don't model your life on mine. You're your own man now, Franc."
  • ·"You don't have to prove yourself to anyone. Especially not me."
Encourages Franc's autonomy and independence, reflecting Anton's belief in personal responsibility and growth beyond his own shadow.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Don't model your life on mine. You're your own man now, Franc."
NarratorFranc smiled.
Franc"All right, Dad."
NarratorAt that moment, Deana rushed to the door.
Deana"Franc! Franc!"
Deana"I'm gonna miss you."
Franc"I'll miss you too. Don't forget, you've got to give Mom and Dad an extra hard time for both of us now."
NarratorDeana nodded.
Deana"Okay."
NarratorFranc picked up his sister and gave her a big hug. Monica appeared in the doorway, holding a paper bag.
Monica"I cut the crusts off your sandwich, just the way you used to like."
Franc"I'm not a kid anymore, Mom."
Monica"Can't a mother pretend? Oh, Franc!"
NarratorShe embraced him with tears in her eyes. Franc looked as if he was about to cry as well.
NarratorI caught her glaring at me over his shoulder. I hoped someday she would understand why I'd decided to send him away.
NarratorSerge approached.
Serge Wolkner"Sir, Franc needs to catch the next ship to United Contana which leaves from Conriat Port in four hours."
Serge Wolkner"We will have to leave now to make it."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:33:13 p17281 / c623
  • ·"Thank you, Serge."
  • ▶"Time to go, Franc."
The urgency of departing to catch the ship necessitates a direct command to move forward, aligning with the practical demands of the situation.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Time to go, Franc."
NarratorFranc turned around and took one last look at me.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:33:29 p17213 / c361
  • ·Say nothing.
  • ▶"Good luck."
  • ·"See you soon."
  • ·"Make me proud."
A balanced expression of support and acknowledgment of Franc's new challenges, aligning with Anton's authoritative yet caring demeanor.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Good luck."
NarratorFranc nodded, before heading to the car.
NarratorSerge looked at his watch.
Serge Wolkner"Time to go."
NarratorFranc waved his hand as he entered the car.
NarratorThe engine started and they were on their way.
NarratorMonica stood next to me as they drove away. Her eyes were teary.
Monica"There he goes."
Monica"It already feels different."
NarratorShe put her head on my shoulder.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:33:50 p17174 / c595
  • ·"I know."
  • ·"He will be fine."
  • ▶"We still have each other."
  • ·Stroke her hair.
Reaffirms their familial bond and provides emotional support during a difficult moment.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"We still have each other."
NarratorShe turned to me, kissed me on my cheek and put her head on my shoulder again.
Monica"With the defeat of your reforms, I hope we will be alright."
NarratorWe watched for a few more minutes, even after the car was no longer in sight.
NarratorI sighed and closed the door.

■ Late Railway OpeningT08

BUDGET -15WEALTH 1STABILITY 0Danger
NarratorIt was finally happening. Tomorrow would be the grand opening ceremony of the L-1 High-speed rail.
NarratorThough the construction delay had taken its toll on our morale, this was still a proud moment for all of us.
NarratorSymon and Gus, who had heavily assisted in the project, had come to discuss the ceremony plans with Lucian and myself.
NarratorLucian stood up.
Lucian Galade"First of all..."
Lucian Galade"This day might have arrived later than expected, but it doesn't change the fact that we are here now."
Lucian Galade"This is still a big success. Congratulations, everyone."
Symon Holl"To many more projects like this."
Gus Manger"Well done, everyone."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:34:13 p16721 / c366
  • ▶“Good work.”
  • ·“We couldn't have done this without our excellent team.”
  • ·Nod.
The President would acknowledge the achievement directly, emphasizing personal responsibility and leadership over collective credit.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Good work."
Lucian Galade"Indeed."
Lucian Galade"Let's move on to our plans for the opening tomorrow."
Lucian Galade"It's been a long time since the last successful infrastructure project. From a public relations point of view, we can certainly capitalize on the occasion."
Lucian Galade"I have a plan on how to do that."
Lucian Galade"If the President accepts, driving the first high-speed train to Lachaven would be great for our image nationwide."
Lucian Galade"What are your thoughts?"
Symon Holl"I don't support anything beyond the opening ceremony. The railway's completion speaks for itself."
Gus Manger"I see your point. But involving the President further could be beneficial for the administration."
Lucian Galade"I think so too. Mr. President?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:34:40 p16777 / c373
  • ▶“I agree. Get me on that train."
  • ·“I'd prefer we just do the opening ceremony. No need for senseless spectacle.”
Participating in driving the train boosts public morale and highlights infrastructure progress, crucial after recent setbacks.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I agree. Get me on that train."
Lucian Galade"Very well."
Gus Manger"Sounds fantastic. The President arriving to Lachaven in the driver's seat would make the headlines for sure."
Lucian Galade"Additionally, a large crowd has been invited to the ceremony and will be watching the opening speech."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:34:59 p16740 / c502
  • ·“Nothing like a cheerful crowd. Looking forward to it.”
  • ▶“It will be a good opportunity for exposure.”
  • ·“Like the good old campaign days.”
  • ·“I guess we can't avoid that.”
The response acknowledges the strategic value of public exposure for the infrastructure project, aligning with the President's pragmatic approach to governance and the emphasis on PR benefits mentioned by Lucian.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"It will be a good opportunity for exposure."
Lucian Galade"Agreed. Every single press outlet has of course been notified and they will be there."
Symon Holl"On another note. It goes without mentioning that this was a team effort."
Symon Holl"Beginning with the President who put his trust in me, I want to thank all who were involved in L-1."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:35:16 p16706 / c470
  • ·“Symon, you deserve the credit. After all, it was you who came up with the project.”
  • ·“Thank you for your kind words. It truly means a good deal.”
  • ▶“Thank you.”
The president's response should maintain diplomatic neutrality while acknowledging the team effort without overcommitting to individual credit, ensuring broader coalition support.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Thank you."
Gus Manger"We had our disagreements, but the important thing was the outcome. The Sordish people have been waiting a long time for this."
Gus Manger"We will show our best face tomorrow. Meanwhile, I'll make sure that our message about improving the countryside gets across."
Symon Holl"Alright then. We have a lot of work to do. See you tomorrow morning."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:35:33 p16805 / c394
  • ·“See you at the ceremony.”
  • ·“Goodbye for now.”
  • ▶“Once again, good work everyone.”
The president needs to acknowledge the team's efforts and maintain morale ahead of the critical infrastructure ceremony.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Once again, good work everyone."
NarratorWe all dispersed in order to prepare for the big day.
NarratorThe next morning, a stage had been erected inside the central station.
NarratorIt was a sunny day in our usually cloudy capital city.
NarratorThe crowd looked quite large. I spotted many party members.
NarratorWe took our places onstage.
Signal for Lucian to begin the ceremony.
Qwen3 (ablit.)I gave the green light to Lucian, and the opening address began.
Lucian Galade"Brothers and sisters of Sordland!"
Lucian Galade"I welcome you Holsordians today as a proud servant of this country."
NarratorLucian's voice echoed in the central station. Thousands of Sordland and USP flags began to wave as the loud cheers intensified.
Lucian Galade"Without further ado, I invite the President of Sordland to the stage."
Walk to the podium.
Qwen3 (ablit.)I walked up to the podium to address the crowd. The press was taking dozens of pictures.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:35:51 p16936 / c404
  • ·“Holsordians! Welcome to the opening ceremony of the L-1 High-speed railway.”
  • ·“Citizens! Welcome to the opening ceremony of the L-1 High-speed railway!”
  • ▶“People of Sordland! Welcome to the opening ceremony of the L-1 High-speed railway.”
The salutation 'People of Sordland' emphasizes national unity and inclusivity, aligning with the significance of a nationwide infrastructure project like the L-1 High-speed rail.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"People of Sordland! Welcome to the opening ceremony of the L-1 High-speed railway."
NarratorThe chanting and excitement from the crowd intensified. A large group began singing the national anthem.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:36:08 p16932 / c424
  • ·“At the beginning of this journey, we promised jobs and opportunity. We kept our promise.”
  • ▶“Trust me, our work is far from over. This project is only the beginning.”
Acknowledges ongoing challenges while positioning the project as part of a broader effort, aligning with the economic crisis and need for sustained reform.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Trust me, our work is far from over. This project is only the beginning."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:36:24 p16899 / c399
  • ▶“We built this railway to create economic growth throughout the Greater Holsord and Gelsland regions."
  • ·“Our intention with the L-1 Railway was to allow citizens to easily commute between the major cities of Holsord and Lachaven.”
Emphasizing economic growth aligns with the administration's focus on fiscal recovery and infrastructure as a catalyst for regional development, addressing immediate economic challenges.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"We built this railway to create economic growth throughout the Greater Holsord and Gelsland regions."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:36:40 p17121 / c425
  • ·"Thanks to this railway, travel between Holsord and Lachaven is now twice as fast and endlessly more comfortable."
  • ▶“Thanks to this railway, thousands of new jobs have been and will continue to be created."
Emphasizing job creation aligns with the administration's focus on economic recovery and addressing high unemployment, directly tackling the nation's immediate needs.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Thanks to this railway, thousands of new jobs have been and will continue to be created."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:36:58 p17105 / c502
  • ·“None of this would be possible without the sound advice of my cabinet member Gus Manger. Please come over here.”
  • ·“Ensuring the success of this project wasn't just one of my presidential goals -- it was my personal priority."
  • ▶“I want to acknowledge the brilliant mind behind this project, the esteemed Minister of Economy and Tax, Symon Holl. Please come over here.”
Symon Holl's role as Minister of Economy and his direct involvement in economic policies align with acknowledging his contribution to the infrastructure project's success.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I want to acknowledge the brilliant mind behind this project, the esteemed Minister of Economy and Tax, Symon Holl. Please come over here."
NarratorSymon walked up beside me.
Symon Holl"Thanks, Mr. President."
Symon Holl"Our aim with this project wasobvious: to improve the speed and efficiency of transportation."
Symon Holl"Once we cut that ribbon and the citizens of Holsord get on the train, I believe you'll agree we have succeeded."
NarratorSymon went on to explain how this railway was a crucial stepping stone on the way to a full Sordish economic recovery.
NarratorLucian took Symon's place after he was done with his speech.
Lucian Galade"Citizens of Holsord! There you have it!"
Lucian Galade"As promised, Anton Rayne, the 4th President of Sordland, has delivered!"
NarratorWe walked towards the line where the first electric train was waiting.
NarratorA red ribbon was hanging by the door of the train.
NarratorSymon handed me an oversized pair of scissors.
Cut the ribbon.
Qwen3 (ablit.)I cut the ribbon, triggering cheers from the crowd.
Symon Holl"Congratulations to all. Hopefully this success will lead to many more."
Gus Manger"It's a good moment indeed. We have helped so many in the countryside."
Lucian Galade"The train is prepared, sir."
Lucian Galade"The journey to Lachaven should take about four hours. The rest of us will be on the train as well."
Get in the train.
Qwen3 (ablit.)I entered the cab and took a seat with the assistant beside me. The crowd was trying to catch a glimpse of me through the window.
Lucian Galade"Who do you want to travel with in the cab, Mr. President?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:37:23 p17219 / c609
  • ·“Lucian, I want you to ride with me.”
  • ▶“I want to have Symon in the cab.”
  • ·“I'd like Gus with me.”
  • ·“I will drive the train on my own.”
Symon Holl's role as the architect of the economic recovery and his direct involvement in the L-1 project make him the most logical choice to emphasize the administration's focus on economic growth and infrastructure success.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I want to have Symon in the cab."
Symon Holl"This isn't necessary, but I won't refuse. We can talk about future economic projects."
NarratorThe locomotive assistant flipped a few switches, and the train began pulling out of the central station. Symon smiled awkwardly at the press photographers as I "drove".
NarratorThe new train was in pristine condition. Its electric engine provided the smoothest ride I'd ever had.
NarratorAfter an uneventful four hours on board, we were welcomed by a throng of citizens at Lachaven station.
NarratorThe railway officially opened to the public right after we left, and thousands began using it.
NarratorIt was a great victory for my administration.

■ Presidential PowersT08

BUDGET -15WEALTH 1STABILITY 0Danger
NarratorLucian and I sat in my office, waiting for Petr. I was at my desk and he was on the sofa, legs crossed.
Lucian Galade"Sir. I have some news for you."
Lucian Galade"Mr. Holl and I have been working on several possible plans to boost the economy, as is our focus."
Lucian Galade"He is currently compiling a very detailed report with suggested projects."
Lucian Galade"I'll make sure you have a chance to see it soon."
NarratorHe looked at his watch again.
Lucian Galade"Looks like Mr. Vice President is late yet another time."
NarratorHe lowered his voice.
Lucian Galade"I am slightly concerned about Petr."
Lucian Galade"He seems to have his mind occupied. He's been late to almost every meeting we've had recently."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:37:55 p16805 / c534
  • ▶"I noticed it too. I need to talk to him in private sometime."
  • ·"I know Petr well. There is nothing to be concerned about."
  • ·"I don't know what's wrong, but I also do not appreciate him being late all the time."
The President would prioritize addressing Petr's erratic behavior given past manipulative tendencies, opting for private intervention to maintain control over alliances and prevent further factional threats.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I noticed it too. I need to talk to him in private sometime."
NarratorSuddenly there was a knock at the door.
NarratorIt was Petr. He walked inside with files in both hands.
Petr Vectern"Gentlemen!"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:38:12 p16026 / c420
  • ▶"Welcome Petr. Have a seat."
  • ·"Finally. Let's begin the meeting."
  • ·"Why are you so late?"
Maintaining diplomatic composure by welcoming Petr warmly ensures continuity of authority while addressing potential underlying issues privately later.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Welcome. Petr. Have a seat."
Petr Vectern"Thank you."
NarratorHe placed the files on my table, took out several documents and organized them in front of me.
Petr Vectern"Here is everything, just as you asked. Our plans for our next reforms."
Petr Vectern"You can use your presidential decrees to enact reforms now. We have enough political capital. I also brought a list of suggested reforms from our party."
Petr Vectern"What are your thoughts, Anton?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:38:28 p16012 / c373
  • ·"I have a few ideas myself."
  • ▶"Let's go over the documents."
Reviewing the documents allows for a structured approach to address Sordland's crises while navigating internal party dynamics and potential reforms.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Let's go over the documents."
NarratorI took a look at the documents.
One-time decree panel11 decree(s) available, 3 slot(s) remaining
DECREE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:38:48 p16617 / c644
  • ·Amnesty for All Soll-Era Political Prisoners — The political persecutions of the post civil war era left thousands of prisoners waiting for their life sentences in prisons like Antel Rock. A presidential pardon decree for all political prisoners would see arbitrarily persecuted activists and thought-leaders regain their freedom.
  • ▶Abolish the Special Zone of Bergia — Under the "Bergia Standardization and Unity Measure", the special status of Bergia is proposed to be abolished. BSUM endorses transitioning from a governor's rule to city-based mayoral elections, while revoking Bergia's special zone status. The implementation of standard national laws and policies will be promoted to facilitate Bergia's full integration into national governance.
  • ·De-Sollinisation — The influence of President Soll is in every facet of society with his portraits, statues and name dotting every possible spot. The administration can begin a de-Sollinisation process with a decree to remove the cult personality that has been established around the second President.
  • ·Enact Value Added Tax — A new decree is set to enact a 20 percent Value Added Tax. This tax addition targeting all sectors is a strategy to generate fresh tax revenues amidst increasing financial pressures. Businesses will collect and remit the tax to the Ministry of Economy, marking a significant readjustment.
  • ·Gender Equality In Education — Sordland's education system has different classes and curriculum for genders. Through a new decree we can provide gender equality in the education system by reverting the heterogeneous studies that were imposed by a traditional education doctrine in the mid 30's.
  • ·Allow Private Prisons — All prisons in Sordland are run and funded by the Ministry of Interior. A decree could be passed to allow private companies to construct and operate prisons for profit while still adhering to the rules and guidelines of the Interior.
  • ·Rural Education Institutes — Sordland lacks technical and theoretical institutes for rural regions causing people to migrate to cities for opportunities. A new directive would see the construction of hundreds of education institutes that would teach the rural population in technical and theoretical skills.
  • ·Fair Trade and Competition Commission — Trade and commerce in Sordland has few regulatory measures resulting in unfair competition at times. A new Fair Trade Commission can be established via decree to push for fair market competition by protecting local industries through new regulations.
  • ·Free Prescription Medicine — Prescription medicine in Sordland is partially paid by the Ministry of Health according to the importance of the medication. A new decree would allow all citizens to get their medication completely for free.
  • ·Lower Retirement Age — The retirement age of Sordland had been increased to 65 in the mid 1940's due to economic stagnation. The older voting blocs are requesting a decrease. Our administration can pass a decree that would lower the retirement age to 60.
  • ·Supreme Council of Sordish Radio and Television — Freedom of speech is protected in Sordland but with the rise of new media private organisations gained a massive influence on society. With a new decree we could create a government agency; the SRSC that could protect the independence of the media or control it.
  • ·Done — enact no more (used 0/3 slots)
Abolishing the Bergia Special Zone addresses both security threats from Bludish separatism and economic inefficiencies. Integrating Bergia into national governance could stabilize the region, reduce rebellion risks, and allow centralized resource allocation, aligning with urgent domestic stability needs.
One-time decree panel10 decree(s) available, 2 slot(s) remaining
DECREE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:39:11 p16528 / c884

  • ·De-Sollinisation — The influence of President Soll is in every facet of society with his portraits, statues and name dotting every possible spot.   • ·Gender Equality In Education — Sordland's education system has different classes and curriculum for genders. Through a new decree we can provide gender equality in the education system by reverting the heterogeneous studies that were imposed by a traditional education doctrine in the mid 30's.
  • ·Allow Private Prisons — All prisons in Sordland are run and funded by the Ministry of Interior. A decree could be passed to allow private companies to construct and operate prisons for profit while still adhering to the rules and guidelines of the Interior.

Enacting a Value Added Tax provides immediate revenue to address the dire economic situation, aligning with the manifesto's focus on fiscal recovery. While risky, it directly tackles the budget deficit, which is critical given the economy's danger level.

The Fair Trade and Competition Commission (option 5) addresses immediate economic instability by regulating markets and protecting local industries. This aligns with the manifesto's focus on fiscal recovery and public-private partnerships, fostering fair competition to stabilize the economy without large upfront costs.
NarratorFollowing a brief discussion, I took notes about my decisions.
Petr Vectern"Approving the abolishment of the Special Zone could be the most major decision you've made today."
Petr Vectern"No more governor of Bergia, just cities with mayors like everywhere else. Let's hope that makes the locals feel more like they're part of this country."
Petr Vectern"Nobody but you could have done something like this, Anton."
Lucian Galade"While the moderates of the Bludish movement and regular citizens will likely be supportive of this..."
Lucian Galade"The Bludish nationalists and the mainstream democratic freedom movement will be furious."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:39:54 p16220 / c491
  • ·"They will have to respect the new structure."
  • ·"You disapprove?"
  • ▶"I was aware of that when I made my decision, Lucian."
Acknowledging potential backlash demonstrates awareness of political risks while reinforcing commitment to reform despite opposition.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I was aware of that when I made my decision, Lucian."
Lucian Galade"Of course, sir."
NarratorPetr took a close look at the papers I signed.
Lucian Galade"I agree that raising new tax revenues is important but we have to understand the public reaction to a new tax levy."
Lucian Galade"A loss in public opinion is guaranteed as the consumers and the economy adjusts to this change."
Petr Vectern"There is a high chance this might influence the average citizen more negatively."
Petr Vectern"We better put the newly generated money to good use."
NarratorPetr placed his finger on my notes about the Fair Trade Commission.
Petr Vectern"That's a nice one. The commission will be essential in dealing with the monopolies."
Petr Vectern"It was time we established a body that regulates economic competition."
Petr Vectern"Well, we both respect your decisions, Anton."
Petr Vectern"But now that everything's settled, I should get to work. We'll catch up soon."
NarratorLucian looked at his watch.
Lucian Galade"We actually have six more minutes."
Lucian Galade"But very well. Let's call it here."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:40:16 p16175 / c712
  • ▶"Thank you both."
  • ·"Thank you, Lucian."
  • ·"Thank you, Petr."
The President acknowledges both Lucian and Petr's contributions to the meeting's outcomes, reflecting mutual respect and collaboration.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Thank you both."

■ Valgsland VisitT08

BUDGET -14WEALTH 1STABILITY 1Danger
NarratorMidnight was approaching as our delegation boarded SRS Holsord.
NarratorThe large ship was once the royal yacht of King Egmund himself. By the order of Soll, it was repurposed to serve the leaders of Sordland for state visits.
NarratorWe departed from the port of Lachaven, along with a few other smaller escort boats.
NarratorI entered the luxurious compartments reserved for heads of state. I lay down on the bed to rest my eyes before we arrived at Wilbern, a major city in Valgsland.
NarratorThe Markian Sea was known for its treacherous waters, but tonight's trip promised to be rather calm and quiet. Still, I couldn't sleep.
NarratorI went out to the upper deck to have some fresh air. A light breeze was blowing, and I relished its briny smell. There was nobody here except Deivid.
NarratorMy foreign minister was leaning over a railing, gazing out at the inky waves.
Deivid Wisci"Mr. President. I am surprised you are still up this late."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:40:42 p16146 / c471
  • ·“I can't sleep.”
  • ·“I like taking night walks.”
  • ▶“I could say the same thing about you.”
The President acknowledges Deivid's presence with a subtle remark, maintaining diplomatic balance while observing his foreign minister's late-night activity.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I could say the same thing about you."
Deivid Wisci"Good point."
Deivid Wisci"I used to feel that sleep was wasted time. Why spend one-third of one's life in the dark?"
Deivid Wisci"I rather appreciate it now. But there's still something special about being awake while the rest of the world has its eyes closed."
Deivid Wisci"Do you mind if I ask why you can't sleep?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:40:59 p16158 / c378
  • ·“I do mind.”
  • ·“It's nothing. Don't worry about it.”
  • ▶“Are you also worried about the future all the time?”
Shifting the focus to Deivid's potential concerns fosters mutual understanding and highlights shared pressures, crucial for maintaining trust in a high-stakes political environment.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Are you also worried about the future all the time?"
Deivid Wisci"I'm an old man, Mr. President. I have been alive for over seventy years."
Deivid Wisci"I spent most of those years worrying about various things. Endlessly."
Deivid Wisci"What I realized in time was that worrying is simply a part of life."
Deivid Wisci"As mankind, we are forever gullible. Always reaching out for what we don't have."
Deivid Wisci"That includes the choices we *could've* made, and their results which will never come to pass."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:41:16 p16189 / c429
  • ·“Thank you for your wisdom, Deivid.”
  • ·“That doesn't help me at all.”
  • ▶Silently watch the horizon.
Anton seeks silent reflection to process the weight of his responsibilities amid ongoing crises, aligning with his need for introspection amidst chaos.
Qwen3 (ablit.)I silently watched the horizon.
Deivid Wisci"I must go and try to sleep, Mr. President."
Deivid Wisci"I recommend you do the same."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:41:35 p16118 / c618
  • ·“Good idea.”
  • ▶“I will stay a little longer.”
The President needs additional time to process the day's challenges and strategize for upcoming negotiations, balancing vigilance with the weight of leadership responsibilities.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I will stay a little longer."
Deivid Wisci"Very well. Good night."
NarratorI watched the sea for some more time before going back to my compartment to sleep.
NarratorThe dawn arrived and our ship moored at the busy port of Wilbern.
NarratorAmid the usual camera flashes, our convoy made its way to the train station.
NarratorAfter a short, uneventful ride, we arrived at the central train station of Haelm, the capital city of Valgsland. A huge crowd was already there, awaiting my arrival. They swarmed around me and took photos as I stepped out of the train.
NarratorOur delegation met with various members of the Valgslandian cabinet before taking our cars to the Royal Palace. The flags of Valgsland and the CSP were waving next to the entrance.
NarratorAssistants took my belongings and led me to the museum wing. It was there that I saw Chancellor Hegel. He was inspecting a very large painting on the wall.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:41:50 p16304 / c299
  • ▶“Chancellor Hegel.”
  • ·Clear your throat.
The president would formally address the chancellor upon meeting, maintaining diplomatic protocol.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Chancellor Hegel."
NarratorHe turned around. He was a man of small stature with a bald spot and a striking visage. His famous pipe was in his mouth.
NarratorHe briefly scanned me from head to toe.
NarratorWe shook hands as he stared me down.
Emmerich Hegel"Mr. Rayne. Welcome to Valgsland."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:42:12 p16292 / c810
  • ▶“It's good to be here.”
  • ·“Thank you.”
  • ·“So. Where do we begin?”
A polite acknowledgment of the invitation sets a respectful tone for diplomatic discussions while maintaining composure.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"It's good to be here."
NarratorHe gestured towards the painting.
Emmerich Hegel"This was made in 1776 by the famous Valgslandian painter, Otto Kiebern."
NarratorI looked at the painting. It must've been over five meters tall. It was a battle scene. On the left side there were banners carried by Valgs; on the right, Sords.
NarratorCountless spears formed a triangle at the center. The sky was red from the blood.
Emmerich Hegel"What do you think about it?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:42:28 p16271 / c336
  • ▶“I think that we have come far. After all, we are standing here side by side instead of facing each other on a battlefield.”

Acknowledge progress in diplomacy to emphasize current cooperation over past conflicts, aligning with the need for stable alliances during Sordland's economic crisis.

Emmerich Hegel"We can do so much more through unity. However, not everyone wants that."
NarratorWe started walking.
Emmerich Hegel"I must admit I have been watching your rule with worry."
Emmerich Hegel"You accepted Arcasian aid and sold your sovereignty by letting them build bases."
Emmerich Hegel"You banned completely legal organizations such as the Red Youth."
Emmerich Hegel"Your actions are clear as day."

Emmerich Hegel"I sincerely hope you understand that the world doesn't work that way anymore, Mr. Rayne."
Emmerich Hegel"Perhaps in the past, but not anymore."
Emmerich Hegel"Listen, Mr. Rayne."
Emmerich Hegel"You have come here for a trade deal."
Emmerich Hegel"Then let's talk about your deal and we will call it a day."

Emmerich Hegel"I have no qualms about what has been negotiated so far."
Emmerich Hegel"Sordish tungsten in return for Valgsland's hard-earned money."
Emmerich Hegel"It's a good deal, really."

■ Lespia VisitT08

BUDGET -14WEALTH 1STABILITY 1Danger
NarratorThe Maroon Train slowly started to move.
NarratorThis train had been in service for many years. It had nine locomotives with special quarters and an office. As the name implied, it was painted bright maroon.
NarratorAmong camera flashes and cheers, we departed from the Holsord central train station. Our destination was Perla, the capital of Lespia. The historic route we were using was also called The Southern Express.
NarratorWe passed through the Lespian mountain range to the lowlands, with a short stop at the beautiful Sarea Nueva where Lespian security forces joined us.
NarratorLooking outside the window, the difference in wealth between Valgen and Sarea Nueva was extraordinary.
NarratorI heard a knock on the door to my office on the train and Symon entered.
Symon Holl"Mr. President."

NarratorA few minutes later, with a long whistle, our train came to a stop. I waved to the crowd from the window.
NarratorThe crowds of reporters that flocked over were pushed away by the Lespian security guards. We made our way to our cars and the convoy began its short trip to the Yellow House.
NarratorThe Yellow House was situated at the very centre of Perla in the middle of a circular park that was off limits to regular citizens. The park was full of roses on small patches.
NarratorThe convoy came to a stop and I made my way to the entrance. The assistants directed me to a room to wait for Prime Minister Alvarez.
Wait.

■ Personal Petr TalkT08

BUDGET -14WEALTH 1STABILITY 1Danger
NarratorIt was a dark, chilly evening when Petr called and asked if we could meet. He gave no details -- all he said was that it was important.
NarratorI drove alone through Holsord's suburbs until I reached the meeting point he named: 147 Wruhec Avenue, number 26. Our old campaign headquarters, untouched since the night of the election. This was where everything began.
NarratorI made my way up the dusty staircase of the office building. The light in the second-floor hallway was out, so I used a cigarette lighter to find my way to the wooden door marked 26. I pulled on the handle, but it wouldn't budge. The heavy door had always been a pain to open.
Petr Vectern"Coming."
NarratorPetr pushed on the door with all his weight. It made a screeching sound as it opened.
Petr Vectern"Without further ado, I bid you welcome to this grand nostalgia trip, hosted by yours truly, Petr Vectern!"
NarratorHe made an exaggerated bow. The dust from the opened door was still settling down.
Petr Vectern"Feeling sentimental yet?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:47:54 p15816 / c390
  • ·“Indeed. I haven't been to this place in ages!”
  • ▶“Not really. It's just an office.”
  • ·“I remember it as being much cleaner.”
Anton maintains a pragmatic distance, avoiding sentimentality to focus on the meeting's purpose amid underlying tensions.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Not really. It's just an office."
Petr Vectern"Well, I for one am overflowing with nostalgia."
Petr Vectern"Come over here. Remember this?"
NarratorHe walked over to his old desk and pointed to the gramophone that had annoyed our neighbors and kept us company for so many late nights. Petr filpped the switch, and it filled the room with swing music from the '20s.
Petr Vectern"I'll be damned. It still works."

  • ·"I like her, too. That doesn't mean I'd break my vows and risk a scandal for a bit of horizontal polo!"
  • ·"Don't tell me you're going to run off with her!"
The revelation of Petr's affair with the president's secretary is a profound personal betrayal, warranting an expression of hurt and disappointment to address the fractured trust.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"How could you do this to me?"
Petr Vectern"I know, I know. But we're ending things. I told her it's too unprofessional. And I want Evelyn back."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:51:25 p16164 / c510
  • ·"Well, maybe I can speak to Evelyn and plead your case."
  • ▶"It's too late. There's still a chance this could get out. I need to talk to Lucian."
  • ·"I still can't believe this. I ought to have you both fired."
  • ·"Who else knows besides me and the three of you?"
Involving Lucian ensures strategic damage control, leveraging his media and intelligence expertise to contain the scandal before it destabilizes the administration.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"It's too late. There's still a chance this could get out. I need to talk to Lucian."
Petr Vectern"Anton, please! I'm begging you."
NarratorThere was an edge of desperation in Petr's voice. He took another drink.
Petr Vectern"How many years have we known each other? How many scrapes have we gotten ourselves out of? I can handle this myself. Just trust me and don't bring anyone else into this."
NarratorI turned to face my friend and Vice President. The habits that had gotten him dubbed the "bad boy of Sordish politics" when we were young were no longer so charming coming from a middle-aged man. Yet he was still a capable politician, and I couldn't ignore our long history together.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:51:40 p16204 / c308
  • ·“All right. It's just an affair, and it's over at that. We can weather this."
  • ▶“You've done enough damage. I will handle it myself.”
  • ·“I will be blunt. You will fix this or I will remove you from office."
The affair threatens administrative integrity; immediate personal control is needed to manage fallout and prevent further scandals.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"You've done enough damage. I will handle it myself."
NarratorWithout waiting for his reaction, I left the rooftop alone and made my way downstairs.
NarratorOn my way out, I grabbed my belongings and took one last look back at the office. It had been home to some happy memories for sure, but this visit had permanently cured me of any nostalgia.
NarratorAs soon as I arrived home, I headed up to my study, sat down and stared at the telephone.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:51:58 p16205 / c543
  • ·Call Evelyn.
  • ▶Call Lucian and tell him what happened.
  • ·Trust Petr and call no one.
Involving Lucian ensures the scandal is managed strategically, leveraging his media expertise to control the narrative and prevent it from destabilizing the administration, despite his manipulative tendencies.
Qwen3 (ablit.)I picked up the phone and dialed Lucian's number.
NarratorHe picked up very quickly.
Lucian Galade"Yes, Mr. President."
NarratorI told Lucian everything Petr had told me about himself, Evelyn and Livia.
Lucian Galade"Thank you for calling me, Mr. President. This serves to confirm some suspicions of my own. We have to meet."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:52:15 p16128 / c412
  • ·"Tomorrow?"
  • ▶"Right now?"
  • ·"What suspicions?"
Immediate action is critical to prevent the scandal from escalating and to coordinate with Lucian's strategic insights before public exposure.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Right now?"
Lucian Galade"Tomorrow morning in your office. Early, before either of the two arrive."
Lucian Galade"Don't bring this up with anyone else. I fear we may have more than a mere dalliance on our hands."
NarratorThe line dropped.
NarratorI stared at the receiver, wondering what his last statement meant. What was going on that I didn't know about?
NarratorI went to bed, but couldn't sleep at all.

■ Affair MeetingT08

BUDGET -14WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorIt was early in the morning after my talk with Petr. The sun was just beginning to peek over the horizon.
NarratorThe Maroon Palace was empty save for a few cleaning staff. I entered my office to find Lucian already waiting for me.
Lucian Galade"Mr. President. Thank you for meeting with me at this hour."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:52:37 p15835 / c321
  • ·"I couldn't sleep anyway."
  • ▶"Cut to the chase."
The President needs to address the urgent matter directly, avoiding unnecessary pleasantries given the gravity of Petr's confession and Lucian's implied deeper concerns.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Cut to the chase."
Lucian Galade"Very well. I'm pleased that you chose to call me after your conversation with Mr. Vectern last night -- although I was already aware of his illicit relationship with Miss Suno."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:52:55 p15876 / c485
  • ·"I figured as much. You do know everything that goes on in the Palace, after all."
  • ▶"How did you find out?"
Anton would logically inquire about Lucian's source of knowledge given his suspected manipulation and control over information.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"How did you find out?"
Lucian Galade"I'll get to that."
Lucian Galade"On its own, given Mr. Vectern's established reputation as something of a playboy, the affair is unlikely to cause much damage. However, a more troubling possibility has been brought to light."
Lucian Galade"A recent conversation with Mr. Hailstone, our whistleblower from Rumburg, revealed the presence of a spy within the Maroon Palace."
Lucian Galade"I believe Miss Suno is our mole. She has been using her relationship with Mr. Vectern to gather information."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:53:09 p16033 / c238
  • ·Laugh incredulously.
  • ▶"Tell me more."
  • ·"You're being paranoid, Lucian."
  • ·"That's preposterous."
Gathering more information is critical to assess the severity of the espionage and protect national security.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Tell me more."
Lucian Galade"I can do better than that -- I'll show you."
NarratorLucian reached into his coat pocket, pulled out an audio tape and fed it into the reel-to-reel machine on my desk.
Lucian Galade"This was recorded in the Palace at 18:09 on the second day of your trade visit to Lespia."
NarratorHe pressed play. I heard Petr and Livia's voices.
Livia Suno"You're sure everyone's gone for the day?"
Petr Vectern"Relax, Liv. Nobody's working that hard with Anton off in Lespia."
NarratorLivia giggled. I heard the sound of kissing, then of drinks being poured.
Livia Suno"Mmm. I'm glad you stayed behind."
Livia Suno"Did he tell you how it's going over there? Do you think there's a chance they'll form an alliance?"
Petr Vectern"No, doesn't seem like it. We'll have to find other alternatives if we're going to keep Rumburg at bay."
Petr Vectern"It seems like we've still got a shot with the superpowers, at least."
Petr Vectern"We've opened two new lines of communication with Ventrie City and Kyow. Just exploratory talks at this point, but they might lead to something deeper."
Petr Vectern"And speaking of something deeper, Miss Suno..."
NarratorLucian grimaced and switched the tape off.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:53:29 p16074 / c621
  • ·"That's it? That's your damning evidence?"
  • ·"This is indeed cause for concern."
  • ▶"Curiosity about foreign relations doesn't make one a spy."
  • ·"And then what happened?"
The conversation details foreign policy discussions, not overt espionage, suggesting caution before labeling Livia a spy.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Curiosity about foreign relations doesn't make one a spy."
Lucian Galade"Perhaps, but why so much curiosity about these particular alliances, if not because they could strengthen us against Rumburg?"
Lucian Galade"And that's not all. I found her resume on file and there's a year-long gap between her finishing secretarial school and beginning her career."
Lucian Galade"Normally, this would have been looked into. But Miss Suno was hired before a background check could be completed. By one Petr Vectern."
NarratorJust then, there was a knock on the door. Livia opened it cautiously.
Livia Suno"Good morning, Mr. President, Mr. Galade. I didn't realize you'd be in so early. Can I get you some coffee?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:53:47 p16181 / c516
  • ·Wait for Lucian to say something.
  • ·"Coffee would be great, actually."
  • ▶"Close the door. Have a seat."
Confronting Livia directly asserts control over the situation, forcing her to respond under scrutiny while addressing the espionage threat decisively.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Close the door. Have a seat."
NarratorLivia sat. She looked puzzled.
Livia Suno"Is something the matter?"
Lucian Galade"Enough pretending. Your secret is out."
Livia Suno"I... I'm not sure what you're talking about."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:54:06 p16142 / c595
  • ·"Come on, Livia. Petr already told me everything."
  • ·"You know perfectly well."
  • ▶"Livia. Lucian has evidence."
Lucian has already obtained the audio evidence, making this the most direct and strategically sound response to pressure Livia into confessing or revealing further information.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Livia. Lucian has evidence."
NarratorLivia buried her face in her hands.
Livia Suno"I'm so sorry, Mr. President. I was foolish, and Petr was so nice to me... But it's over now. Nobody ever needs to know--"
Lucian Galade"That's not what the President means, Miss Suno. We're referring to you being an intelligence agent for Rumburg."
NarratorLivia sat upright. She looked shocked.
Livia Suno"Pardon me, Mr. Galade, but are you feeling all right?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:54:25 p16161 / c627
  • ·"No, he isn't. You'll have to excuse him."
  • ·"I know. It sounds ridiculous to me as well."
  • ·"Don't play dumb, Livia. If that is your real name."
  • ▶"He's fine. What do you have to say for yourself?"
The President confronts Livia directly, leveraging Lucian's evidence while maintaining control over the interrogation process.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"He's fine. What do you have to say for yourself?"
Livia Suno"Mr. President, I understand if you don't want me to keep my job, but why smear me with outlandish accusations?"
Lucian Galade"Miss Suno, indulge me for a moment. What were you doing in the year 1951?"
NarratorLivia's face flushed.
Livia Suno"If you must know, I was working as a cigarette girl at Porto's. It's a Lachaven saloon. I was saving up so I could afford my own place in Holsord."
Livia Suno"Here's the proprietor's number if you need to double check."
NarratorShe scribbled a telephone number on a piece of paper. Lucian snatched it up.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:54:42 p16215 / c434
  • ·"But why have you been asking me so many questions about what I do?"
  • ▶"But why were you asking Petr about my alliances?"
  • ·"But why were you so eager to help Deivid back in Benfi?"
The question directly addresses Livia's espionage activities regarding foreign alliances, which is the core of the evidence presented by Lucian.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"But why were you asking Petr about my alliances?"
Livia Suno"To tell the truth, Mr. Rayne... I was hoping to run for office myself someday. I want to make a real difference in the world, like Ms. Morgna or Ms. Walda."
Livia Suno"But I guess I messed that up by getting involved with Petr. Now they're all just going to say I slept my way to the top."
Livia Suno"Wait. How did you know about that conversation?"
NarratorThere was a loud pounding on the door. Petr barged in without waiting for an answer. He was unshaven, and wore the same clothes as the night before.
Petr Vectern"I came as soon as I heard the three of you were in here. Anton, what the hell are you doing? I told you I had this under control."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:54:59 p16471 / c376

  • ·"Sorry. Lucian already found out."

Asserting authority to de-escalate the situation and prevent Petr from disrupting the confrontation with Livia.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Sit down and shut up."
NarratorPetr took a seat on the far end of the sofa. He looked over at Livia and noticed her troubled expression.
Petr Vectern"God, why involve her? This is my mess. She did nothing wrong."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:55:14 p16499 / c364
  • ·"Well... there's a small chance she could be a spy."
  • ·"Lucian has the preposterous idea that she's a spy."
  • ▶"She's a spy, Petr. You messed up big-time."
Directly confronts Petr's betrayal and acknowledges Livia's potential espionage, asserting authority over the crisis.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"She's a spy, Petr. You messed up big-time."
Petr Vectern"I-- what?!"
Lucian Galade"I have reason to believe she's been relaying information to Rumburg. Information that you leaked to her in your carelessness."
NarratorLucian rewound his tape and played it again. As Petr listened, his eyes widened in disbelief.
Petr Vectern"Lucian, you rat bastard. You've been bugging my office!"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:55:37 p16362 / c861
  • ▶"Obviously for a good reason."
  • ·"Lucian, is this true? How could you?"
  • ·"I'm sure there's an explanation for this."
Anton would prioritize maintaining control and trust in Lucian's strategies, implying the surveillance had a justifiable purpose to uphold his authority.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Obviously for a good reason."
NarratorBoth Petr and Livia were glaring at Lucian now. He looked unrepentant.
Lucian Galade"I had noticed the Vice President's behaviour becoming increasingly erratic and unprofessional over the past months. I wanted to get to the root of the cause."
Petr Vectern"So you wiretapped your own goddamn colleague? And for what? Have you been getting off on this, you pervert?"
Lucian Galade"Certainly not. I believe that along with the Rumburg whistleblower's admission that there is a spy in the Palace and certain gaps in Miss Suno's resume, this tape makes a compelling case to investigate the secretary for espionage."
Lucian Galade"Whether or not the investigation yields positive results, Mr. Vectern ought to be held accountable for his willingness to divulge such sensitive information. He ought to stand trial or, at the very least, resign."
NarratorPetr's jaw dropped. Livia stood up.
Livia Suno"If I may, Mr. President. I've been paying attention to what goes on in the Palace. Not because I'm some sort of agent, but out of genuine interest. Hard as that may be for you to believe, Mr. Galade."
NarratorShe scowled at Lucian, then turned back to me.
Livia Suno"And from what I've seen, this man has been attempting to undermine your vice president at every turn. Perhaps with the goal of replacing Petr himself."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:55:55 p16551 / c483
  • ·"Of course you'd say that. Petr's your lover."
  • ·"That's ridiculous. I would never make Lucian vice president."
  • ·"Nonsense. Lucian's been nothing but loyal."
  • ▶"That actually makes a lot of sense."
Lucian's calculated actions and history of manipulation align with undermining Petr to consolidate power, making Livia's accusation politically plausible.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"That actually makes a lot of sense."
Livia Suno"Thank you. I'm happy someone at the Palace still takes me seriously."
NarratorLucian frowned.
Lucian Galade"My personal misgivings about Mr. Vectern aside, I would never do anything to harm this administration."
Lucian Galade"Mr. Vectern, on the other hand, has at best committed adultery, and at worst, delivered state secrets straight into enemy hands."
NarratorWho could I believe?

Lucian Galade"What are you going to do, Mr. President?"

Lucian Galade"Then you're playing straight into Rumburg's."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:56:53 p16519 / c556

Lucian Galade"Sir, this is a mistake."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:57:10 p16542 / c473

The President must assert his autonomy and accept responsibility for his decisions, regardless of internal conflicts or external pressures, to maintain authority and integrity.

NarratorLucian clenched his jaw. I'd never seen him this unhinged in all my years working with him.
Lucian Galade"Very well, sir."

NarratorI was right to defend my secretary and my best friend. But I hoped I hadn't just made an enemy.

■ Politics Bills BriefingT08

BUDGET -14WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorThe morning sunlight streamed through the windows as Lucian and I entered the White Room.
NarratorI sat down. Lucian remained standing.
Lucian Galade"I'm afraid I won't be able to join for your meetings today, sir."
Lucian Galade"The Livia Suno affair has left me with much to catch up on."
Lucian Galade"But I did want to give you a quick word of warning."
Lucian Galade"I have noticed that the USP faction in the Assembly has adopted a confrontational stance of late."
Lucian Galade"Their displeasure with you has led them to engage in obstructionist tactics, slowing down policy implementation and creating legislative gridlock."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:57:34 p16193 / c342
  • ·"Damn. I really have turned into the next Alphonso, haven't I?"
  • ·"So... what does that actually mean?"
  • ▶"I'll show them what happens when you get in the way of Anton Rayne."
  • ·"Blocking progress for the country because of their petty disagreements with me. I hope they're happy with themselves."
Asserts determination to counter opposition, reflecting resolve against internal sabotage and maintaining presidential authority.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I'll show them what happens when you get in the way of Anton Rayne."
Lucian Galade"It won't be easy to get past this, I'm afraid.
NarratorHe dropped a thick folder in front of me.
Lucian Galade"I was hoping to go over the day's agenda with you before your cabinet members began arriving. But duty calls."
NarratorHe walked out. Almost immediately, as if she'd been waiting for him to leave, Livia entered with a carafe of coffee,
NarratorShe poured me a cup with a smile, then took a look down at the folder in front of me.
Livia Suno"It looks like you have quite the packed agenda for today, Mr. President."
Livia Suno"I'm sorry again about the disturbance my indiscretion caused. I hope it didn't create too much extra work for you."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:57:53 p16291 / c493
  • ·"Lucian's madcap accusations created the disturbance, not you."
  • ·"It wasn't your fault. It was my lecher of a vice president."

  • ·"My work is none of your business."
Anton needs to reassert authority over Livia while maintaining operational control, making a professional conduct warning both pragmatic and symbolic.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I do expect more professional behavior from you going forward, Ms. Suno."
Livia Suno"And you will get it, Mr. President. Enjoy your coffee."
NarratorShe walked out. I enjoyed a split second of solitude before the morning's first meeting.
NarratorInternal affairs was first on the list of items. Lileas and Nia, along with their close staff, joined me around the table.
Lileas Graf"Shall we begin?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:58:02 p16234 / c436
  • ·"Yes, please do."
  • ·"I hope you've got only good news for me."
  • ▶"Tell me what's been going on in Bergia."
Directly addressing Bergia's situation is crucial given recent administrative changes and potential unrest, ensuring immediate oversight of regional challenges.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Tell me what's been going on in Bergia."
Lileas Graf"Mr. President. I want to congratulate you again on your historic accomplishment for Bluds and Sordland. Thank you for approving my plans for the revocation of the Special Zone."
NarratorNia averted her gaze.
Lileas Graf"The internal security structure of Bergia has already been dismantled. Part of the local forces will be integrated into the Sordish Police Force."
Lileas Graf"The governor position has been abolished. In the next election, each town will elect its own mayor as in the rest of Sordland."
Nia Morgna"And now you, Ms. Graf, have complete control over the region's security..."
Lileas Graf"As I should, Ms. Morgna. I am the Minister of the Interior, after all."
Nia Morgna"In any case, now that the governor is gone, this will hopefully prove to be a huge step for Bludish rights in the region."
Nia Morgna"Of course, the Unified Language Act is creating a lot of resentment within the Bludish communities, who want to choose their own language in education."
Nia Morgna"This is certainly a point of contention."
NarratorShe sighed.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:58:20 p16622 / c427

Qwen3 (ablit.)"I hear your concerns."
NarratorNia nodded.
NarratorLileas stood up.
Lileas Graf"I have important updates about the increasing tensions on the streets."
Lileas Graf"We are getting reports of more and more demonstrations against the government, with some having the potential to become violent."
Lileas Graf"Karl and I are watching the situation closely."
Lileas Graf"Rest assured, our police forces will not let the situation get out of control."
NarratorShe placed a stack of papers in front of me.
Lileas Graf"The security committee of the Assembly is working on a draft law that would help strengthen our internal security in times of crisis. Such as now."
Lileas Graf"I've been following their work and I truly believe they've done an exemplary job with this. Mr. Holstron -- who has been working on this for quite some time -- has made some great arguments for the bill."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:58:40 p16821 / c580

Qwen3 (ablit.)"If it's Mr. Holstron's work, I'm sure I'll be vetoing it."
Lileas Graf"You should be taking this more seriously, Mr. President."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:58:58 p16781 / c491
  • ▶"Tell me about this bill."
  • ·"I'll think about that when -- and if -- the Assembly approves it."

Qwen3 (ablit.)"Tell me about this bill."
Lileas Graf"Firstly, it aims to grant the necessary authority to the police forces on the ground to enable quick and decisive responses in situations where public security is at risk."
Lileas Graf"It will also remove several legal limits that were prescribed for the forces -- allowing them to function much more efficiently."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:59:15 p16783 / c469

  • ·"I'm sorry. I don't think I'll be supporting this bill."

Qwen3 (ablit.)"This sounds all too vague. What legal limits are you talking about here?"
Lileas Graf"It removes the prerequisites of warrants and substantial evidence, allowing for suspicion-based arrests and confiscations if the forces believe the targets to be potential threats to public safety."
Lileas Graf"It also adds a clause which allows security forces to exercise their authority beyond their legal limits *only if* it is deemed essential for maintaining state security."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)23:59:33 p16858 / c495
  • ·"I like the sound of it."
  • ·"I'm sorry. I don't think I'll be supporting this bill."
  • ▶"This is insane, Lileas. I'm not going to turn Sordland into a police state."

Qwen3 (ablit.)"This is insane, Lileas. I'm not going to turn Sordland into a police state."
Lileas Graf"You are clearly not seeing the threats."
Nia Morgna"You are creating them yourself, Lileas."
NarratorLileas sighed.
Lileas Graf"In any case..."
Lileas Graf"That's all from me about the security bill."

Lileas Graf"We'll keep it brief. I think that will be all for our internal affairs update."
NarratorThey gathered their documents.
Nia Morgna"See you later, Mr. President."

NarratorSymon came in shortly afterwards.
Symon Holl"Mr. President. Where's the Vice President today?"

Qwen3 (ablit.)"That's confidential, Mr. Holl."
Symon Holl"Understood."
NarratorSymon's expression remained unchanged as he sat down to face me.
Symon Holl"I just wanted to provide you with a few updates on the economy."
Symon Holl"You may have noticed that the government budget is in need of a boost."
Symon Holl"My ministry is therefore working on a bill that will increase the tax on tobacco products from 10 to 30 percent."
Symon Holl"A similar bill is being drafted to introduce a 20 percent tax on alcohol."
Symon Holl"In addition to bringing in needed revenue, the new laws would curb excessive drinking and smoking in Sordland."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:00:09 p16936 / c458
  • ·"Very good. Those are nasty vices anyway."
  • ·"We can't tax people's booze and cigarettes. There'd be riots in the streets."
  • ▶"Interesting initiatives. But would the income we'd receive be worth the harm to Sordish businesses?"
Balancing fiscal needs with economic stability by questioning the impact on businesses aligns with prioritizing recovery over immediate austerity, avoiding potential backlash that could destabilize the administration.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Interesting initiatives. But would the income we'd receive be worth the harm to Sordish businesses?"
Symon Holl"Good question. The tax wouldn't apply to products designated for export, which would mitigate the damage somewhat."
Symon Holl"Ordinarily, I'd advise against this kind of thing. But with our budget dipping into the red, any measure that would reverse our deficit is worth considering."
Symon Holl"Especially since our income from tourism is on the decline."
Symon Holl"Our investment in cultural preservation failed to produce the intended effect, it seems."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:00:27 p17033 / c484
  • ▶"That's disappointing to hear."
  • ·"Who wouldn't want to visit Sordland? It's the best country in the world!"
The president acknowledges the failure of the Tourism and Cultural Preservation Act, aligning with the need to address economic shortcomings rather than dismissing them.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"That's disappointing to hear."
Symon Holl"To be fair, I never understood why anyone would want to vacation in Sordland when Lespia is right there."
NarratorThere was a knock on the door. Gus Manger strolled in and grabbed a seat.
Gus Manger"Mr. President. Symon."
Symon Holl"Thanks for joining us, Gus. I believe you wanted to update the President on the latest developments in the Gruni region?"
NarratorGus nodded vigorously.
Gus Manger"Yes, you both know how revitalizing the region has always been my pet project of sorts."
Gus Manger"Well. I'm saddened to say that despite my ministry's best efforts, we're going to have to close the books on this project."
Symon Holl"'Close the books', Mr. Manger? What does that mean?"
NarratorGus sighed.
Gus Manger"It means that without a massive injection of funding, the economic growth I had hoped to see in the region is simply not going to happen during this term."
Gus Manger"What could have been a beacon of success for Sordland is turning into a cesspool of crime, unemployment and homelessness."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:00:47 p17090 / c556
  • ▶"Perhaps this wouldn't be happening if you'd focused as much attention on Gruni as you have on your side ventures."
  • ·"You can't just give up. I know how much Gruni means to you."
  • ·"I knew it from the beginning. It made no sense to try to turn Gruni into something it wasn't."
The President's pragmatic nature and history of holding officials accountable for failures align with criticizing Gus's lack of focus on Gruni.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Perhaps this wouldn't be happening if you'd focused as much attention on Gruni as you have on your side ventures."
NarratorGus gritted his teeth.
Gus Manger"If Gruni is failing, it is not for my lack of trying. You have no idea how much blood, sweat and tears I put into my development plan."
Symon Holl"Try to calm down, Gus. The President makes decisions on behalf of the entire country, not only your home region."
NarratorThe door to the White Room opened slowly, and Deivid made his way in.
Deivid Wisci"Oh, hello, gentlemen. I'm early again, aren't I?"
Symon Holl"Not to worry, Mr. Wisci. We've just about wrapped things up."
NarratorSymon and Gus rose from their seats and left the room.
NarratorAfter the door closed, Deivid regarded me for a second. His blue eyes seemed to see right through me.
Deivid Wisci"How are you, Anton? You look as if you haven't slept in ages."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:01:09 p17217 / c666
  • ·"It's been a rough few days, Deivid."
  • ▶"Sleeplessness comes part and parcel with this job. You know that."
  • ·"You're better off not knowing."
  • ·"Can I ask you a personal question? Were you ever unfaithful to Cathrin?"
Maintains professional composure while deflecting personal inquiries, aligning with Anton's need to project stability amid political turmoil.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Sleeplessness comes part and parcel with this job. You know that."
Deivid Wisci"I'll be very brief. Perhaps we can even carve out some time for you to rest your eyes."
Deivid Wisci"Regretfully, tensions are continuing to mount between Sordland and Rumburg."
Deivid Wisci"As you might imagine, our decision to grant asylum to their whistleblower Mr. Hailstone did not sit well with the Kingdom."
Deivid Wisci"We must exercise caution. The slightest provocation on either side could cost us our chance at reconciliation."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:01:28 p17203 / c505
  • ·"Bringing Rumburg to justice for what it's done is far more important than reconciliation."
  • ▶"You're right as usual. The last thing we want is a war on our hands."
  • ·"I don't even know if peace is possible after so many years of animosity."
Prioritizing peace avoids escalating tensions with Rumburg, ensuring stability amid internal challenges and foreign espionage risks.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"You're right as usual. The last thing we want is a war on our hands."
Deivid Wisci"A compromise in the name of the greater good may spare countless lives."
Deivid Wisci"I'll leave you to it, Anton. I know you've got a lot on your plate."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:01:44 p17180 / c360
  • ·"It's always a pleasure, Deivid."
  • ·"Thanks for your brevity. Have a good day."
  • ▶"We'll talk soon."

Qwen3 (ablit.)"We'll talk soon."
NarratorDeivid left the White Room, gently closing the door behind him.

BUDGET -14WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorSeveral days had passed since the confrontation with Petr, Livia and Lucian in my office.
NarratorLucian was still acting frosty towards me for rejecting his advice. On the other hand, both Petr and Livia were unnaturally nice to me, though they avoided speaking to each other.
NarratorI was just settling in behind my desk when Lucian knocked on my door.
Lucian Galade"Sir, a moment of your time?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:02:14 p16554 / c441
  • ▶"Of course."
  • ·Motion for him to come in.
  • ·"Not now."
The President must address Lucian's concerns promptly to maintain authority and resolve the internal conflict, despite prior tensions.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Of course."
NarratorLucian entered my office and closed the door behind him.
Lucian Galade"I still believe you were wrong not to pursue an investigation into Miss Suno. However, I acknowledge my lack of concrete evidence and unorthodox information gathering method may have given you pause."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:02:31 p16665 / c356
