  • ·"You're not going to change my mind about this."
  • ▶"Unorthodox? You surveilled Petr behind my back!"
The president would confront Lucian's betrayal of surveilling Petr without his knowledge, highlighting the violation of trust and unauthorized tactics.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Unorthodox? You surveilled Petr behind my back!"
Lucian Galade"I have already apologized to Mr. Vectern for the intrusion. It won't happen again."
NarratorLucian looked me in the eye.
Lucian Galade"I feel I would be failing in my duties as a chief strategist not to give you one last piece of advice on this."
Lucian Galade"Please, consider dismissing Miss Suno from her position at the palace. Just in case I am right after all."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:02:49 p16779 / c492
  • ·"Well, she has been a distraction to Petr..."
  • ▶"I can't. She's so good at her job!"
  • ·"Are you crazy? She'll go public about the affair."
The President prioritizes practical governance and recognizes Livia's competence, making dismissal unnecessary despite suspicions.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I can't. She's so good at her job!"
Lucian Galade"Her looks may be unmatched, but her skills most certainly are not."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:03:06 p16802 / c441
  • ·"All right. If it'll get you off my case, I'll fire her."
  • ▶"No. I won't continue to indulge your paranoid fantasies. Livia stays."
The President values loyalty and competent leadership, and refuses to let Lucian's paranoia dictate decisions without concrete evidence.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"No. I won't continue to indulge your paranoid fantasies. Livia stays."
NarratorLucian looked as if he was about to object one last time, but then smiled thinly.
Lucian Galade"As you wish. I won't bring this up any further."
NarratorHe walked out and closed the door behind him.

■ Decision Penalty USPObstructionistT08

BUDGET -14WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
EventUSP Apparatus Obstructs: The United Sordland Party has recently adopted a confrontational stance, engaging in obstructionist tactics that are raising political tensions. The party's actions, primarily aimed at hindering the President's initiatives, have led to a significant slow down in policy implementation. This disruption has resulted in legislative gridlock, creating a challenging environment for the execution of administrative plans.
Don't toe the party line...

■ Womens Rights MeetingT08

BUDGET -14WEALTH 1STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorI was sitting in my office. I looked at the clock.
Narrator2:26 PM. It was almost time for my appointment with Ciara on women's rights.
NarratorThere was a knock at the door. Ciara entered the room with folders in her hand. The door did not close behind her.
NarratorMonica entered the room and stood next to Ciara.
Ciara Walda"Mr. President."
Monica"Anton, before we start..."
Monica"I just wanted to thank you for agreeing to this meeting."
Monica"I've been dreaming about this ever since I volunteered with the Sordish League of Women. I can't believe the moment's finally here."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:03:32 p16868 / c465
  • ·“Anything to make you happy."
  • ·“I think both of you together could finally bring change to Sordland.”
  • ▶“I hope you thought this through. Real change requires carefully considered plans.”
The President cautiously acknowledges the need for structured planning, reflecting his awareness of political challenges and desire to avoid hasty decisions amid internal conflicts.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I hope you thought this through. Real change requires carefully considered plans."
Monica"We did."
NarratorCiara sat down on a chair and laid down the folder she was holding.
Ciara Walda"We still have a long way to go before Sordish women are able to enjoy the same rights and privileges as men."
Monica"Or even as their counterparts in Arcasia and United Contana. We've been lagging behind on this for decades, Anton."
Ciara Walda"And this isn't only a moral issue. I have seen enough revolutions in this world to understand the main driver of any change."
Ciara Walda"Money."
NarratorShe flipped a folder open and started pulling out documents.
Ciara Walda"In United Contana, law dictates that women earn the same wages as men in their respective fields. My research shows that this led to a 15.5% increase in productivity. You could say this had a direct influence on their superpower status."
Ciara Walda"Arcasia passed laws ensuring maternity leave rights, child day care centers and tax deductions for working families. In turn, they experienced a 12.6% productivity boost and a flourishing economy."
Ciara Walda"Both countries also have significantly lower infant mortality rates, a statistic I believe is directly linked to the improved status of women."
Ciara Walda"Our administration has thus far failed to pull Sordland out of its economic recession."
NarratorShe leaned back in her chair.
Ciara Walda"So I am asking: Why not try passing similar laws? At worst, even if they fail to improve the economy, it'll still be a significant step forward for our society."
Ciara Walda"It's only logical."
NarratorMonica handed me one of the documents. It had a list of bullet points on it.
Monica"The two of us talked it over, and these are the measures we believe are most urgently needed."
NarratorI let my eyes wander down the list.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:03:56 p17098 / c669
  • ·"Criminalizing domestic violence."
  • ▶"Support for working mothers."
  • ·"Equal treatment in the workforce."
  • ·"Gender equality in public education."
Support for working mothers aligns with expanding vocational training and healthcare access while addressing immediate workforce productivity, balancing social progress with fiscal pragmatism amid economic instability.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Support for working mothers."
NarratorCiara nodded.
Ciara Walda"It simply isn't fair that women in the workforce are punished for having children."
Monica"Remember what I told you about Janice? My friend who basically got fired for becoming pregnant? That would never happen to a man."
Ciara Walda"Paid maternity leave and state-subsidized daycare centers could be key to keeping more mothers employed."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:04:15 p17133 / c499
  • ·"I don't want to reward women for choosing work over raising their children."
  • ·"It's true -- having children never hindered my career prospects. It shouldn't hinder a woman either."
  • ·"I'll have to think about this."
  • ▶"Monica, you always said you never regretted giving up a career to raise Franc and Deana."
Challenging Monica's past statements aligns with the tension in their relationship and leverages her own words to question her current advocacy, reflecting strategic manipulation amid political pressures.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Monica, you always said you never regretted giving up a career to raise Franc and Deana."
Monica"Anton, I never had a choice."
Ciara Walda"Moving on."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:04:34 p17151 / c579
  • ·"Criminalizing domestic violence."
  • ▶"Equal treatment in the workforce."
  • ·"Gender equality in public education."
Prioritizing equal treatment in the workforce aligns with economic recovery goals by addressing productivity-enhancing measures like maternity leave and wage parity, as highlighted by Ciara's data on Contana and Arcasia.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Equal treatment in the workforce."
Monica"That's an easy one."
Ciara Walda"Right now, Sordland has one of the continent's largest pay gaps between men and women. We need legislation ensuring that a woman doing the same job as a man is paid the same wage."
Monica"We would also introduce quotas to get more women into positions of power -- in the Assembly, for instance, or on the boards of corporations."
Ciara Walda"Eventually. Narrowing the pay gap is a far more pressing issue, as it would bring financial independence to countless lower-income women."
Monica"Ciara, we never talked about this. But I always thought that with more women in power, higher status for all women would naturally follow."
Ciara Walda"Women are starving because they don't make a living wage. That's a damn sight more important than your friend Janice being passed over for a promotion."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:04:53 p17265 / c511
  • ▶"I don't see a problem with quotas, but tackling the pay gap is much more urgent."
  • ·"Quotas seem far easier to implement than equal pay laws. We should start there."
  • ·"Quotas are a terrible idea. People should be hired on merit, not simply because of their sex."
Tackling the pay gap is a direct economic imperative that aligns with fiscal recovery goals, while quotas risk being perceived as superficial solutions without immediate tangible benefits.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I don't see a problem with quotas, but tackling the pay gap is much more urgent."
NarratorMonica gritted her teeth.
Ciara Walda"Thank you, Mr. President."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:05:14 p17195 / c743
  • ▶"Criminalizing domestic violence."
  • ·"Gender equality in public education."
Criminalizing domestic violence aligns with addressing immediate systemic issues affecting women's rights, complementing previous discussions on workplace equality and societal reform.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Criminalizing domestic violence."
Ciara Walda"I shouldn't have to tell you how crucial this is. It is utterly unbelievable that, in this day and age, Sordish men are legally allowed to beat their wives."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:05:32 p17280 / c517
  • ▶"I agree. It's a travesty."
  • ·"What families do in their own homes is none of our concern."
Aligns with progressive reforms and public welfare, reinforcing the administration's commitment to social stability and addressing critical gender equity issues.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I agree. It's a travesty."
Monica"I'm happy we're on the same page."
Ciara Walda"As incidences are especially high within Bludish communities, I would also advocate for setting up a special domestic violence task force in collaboration with Bludish women's rights organizations."
NarratorMonica looked at her strangely.
Monica"Are you sure that's something we ought to focus on? The Bluds are... different. They have their own rules."
Ciara Walda"Monica, I'm surprised at you! We can't let women suffer under the patriarchy, no matter their ethnicity."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:05:52 p17340 / c546
  • ·"Interfering in Bludish matters has landed us in enough trouble already."
  • ▶"The Bluds must conform to Sordish law."
  • ·"I'm still not sure criminalizing domestic violence is a good idea. For the Bluds or anyone else."
Asserting Sordish law over Bludish matters maintains national unity and addresses domestic violence as a universal issue, balancing integration with reform.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"The Bluds must conform to Sordish law."
Ciara Walda"We can discuss the particulars once the law has passed."
"Gender equality in public education."
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Gender equality in public education."
NarratorCiara grinned.
Ciara Walda"You know how much this means to me. And you've witnessed the dangers firsthand."
Ciara Walda"The way girls are shut out of a real education unless their parents have the means to get them out of the public school system -- so many bright minds are being lost."
Monica"I wouldn't entirely demean the old system, Ciara. Deana still loves her handicrafts classes."
Ciara Walda"I'm not saying we'll take those away. Maybe boys should learn handicrafts too."
NarratorMonica burst out laughing.
Monica"Imagine Franc in an apron!"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:06:14 p17257 / c702
  • ·"We've had this conversation, Ciara. I'm entirely on your side."
  • ▶"We can't force schools what to teach to whom."
The President prioritizes pragmatic governance over ideological mandates, recognizing the challenges of enforcing educational reforms against entrenched resistance.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"We can't force schools what to teach to whom."
Ciara Walda"We can if not doing so puts us on the wrong side of history. Have you forgotten what we decided about evolution?"
Ciara Walda"Let's move on for now."
Ciara Walda"That covers all subjects."
Ciara Walda"What are your thoughts so far?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:06:32 p17277 / c442
  • ·“You've made a good case for all of these changes.”
  • ·“I'm not sure I can risk passing laws like these so close to the election.”
  • ▶“It feels too much like you're comparing Sordland to other nations. We must choose our own path.”
Emphasizing Sordland's unique path allows Anton to navigate internal party resistance and maintain control amid political tensions, aligning with his cautious governance style.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"It feels too much like you're comparing Sordland to other nations. We must choose our own path."
Ciara Walda"I agree completely. We must aim to be better than them. Better than all the superpowers."
Ciara Walda"None of them have yet managed to achieve true parity among the sexes. If we can do that, we'll make history."
Ciara Walda"Let's talk about how to proceed."
Ciara Walda"I want to establish a commission for women's rights in Sordland with me as its head. Our goal shall be passing and implementing a set of laws to solve the problems we just discussed."
Monica"As the commission's spokeswoman, my responsibility will be to make speeches and organize fundraisers. I will be the face of this movement while Ciara handles the groundwork."
Monica"If I can show that the First Lady is behind this movement, many women will feel encouraged."
NarratorCiara gestured down at her outfit.
Ciara Walda"Especially those who aren't keen on pantsuits."
Ciara Walda"The organization's name will be 'Commission on the Status of Women'."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:06:51 p17284 / c532
  • ▶“It's going to be damn hard getting your laws past the Assembly."
  • ·"What do you need a commission for? I'll run your laws past the Assembly."
Acknowledging the difficulty aligns with the observed USP obstructionism and ensures pragmatic realism amid legislative gridlock.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"It's going to be damn hard getting your laws past the Assembly."
Ciara Walda"I know. But even a watered-down version would be better than nothing."
Ciara Walda"Mr. President, I know we've had friction in the past. But this has nothing to do with the failed education reform."
Ciara Walda"This is a completely separate topic and it should be handled as such."
NarratorMonica lowered her voice.
Monica"Anton, I'm not completely naive. I know what we're proposing would have its downsides."
Monica"But every time I look at our daughter, I think about how I'd do anything to grant her a better future."
Monica"I know you would too. Just like you told me all those years ago."
Monica"I hope you'll make the right decision. We both do."
NarratorCiara was looking studiously at a scratch on my desk. She blinked and turned back to us.
Ciara Walda"That's right, Mr. President."
NarratorThey rose from their seats expectantly.
NarratorI took a few minutes to think.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:07:11 p17306 / c507
  • ▶“I will approve the creation of the commission and support your bill in the Assembly.”
  • ·"Sorry, but the answer is no. You can try and run your laws by the Assembly, but they'll get no support from me."
Approving the commission aligns with addressing gender equality despite political challenges, reflecting commitment to progressive reforms and societal progress.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I will approve the creation of the commission and support your bill in the Assembly."
NarratorMonica turned to her.
Monica"That's all the excitement you can muster? You did it! We did it!"
NarratorMonica hugged Ciara. She smiled uncomfortably.
Ciara Walda"It's not over yet, Monica. The true battle is in the Assembly. We'll celebrate once that's over with."
Ciara Walda"I'll start gathering names for the commission tonight."
NarratorCiara started collecting her documents.
NarratorShe and Monica headed towards the door. Monica paused before leaving.
Monica"I love you, Anton."
Monica"I'll see you at home."
NarratorI could hear Monica and Ciara talking excitedly to each other as the door closed behind them.
NarratorI looked out my window over the city of Holsord. It was a clear day outside.

■ Gendarmerie DisputeT08

BUDGET -14WEALTH 0STABILITY 3Unstable
NarratorI arrived in the city of Vesord with Petr after receiving an emergency meeting request from Iosef and Lileas.
NarratorI had seen the reports of a recent issue that rose between the Sordish Police Forces and the Gendarmerie units at the city.
NarratorThe report said that an operation against a drug trafficking gang in Vesord failed due to miscommunication.
NarratorIt was not hard to guess what this meeting was going to be about.
NarratorWe entered the city hall and made our way to the second floor. I could hear Iosef and Lileas's voices echoing through the hall.
NarratorI opened the doors to the meeting room. The two ministers did not even realise.
Iosef Lancea"This is unacceptable!"
Iosef Lancea"How dare the police meddle in the affairs of the Gendarmerie?"
Lileas Graf"It was *your* men who started this ridiculousness and meddled with an ongoing police investigation."
NarratorTheir voices were getting louder and louder.
Iosef Lancea"Rural security is my jurisdiction and your men were way outside of Vesord."
Petr Vectern"Please calm down and let's discuss this."
Petr Vectern"What is going on?"
Iosef Lancea"Apologies, but Ms. Graf here fails to understand her place."
Lileas Graf"No, Mr. Lancea. You are the one who does not understand his place."
Lileas Graf"If you continue to do so, you will pay for it one day."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:08:06 p17335 / c264
  • ·Slam the table.
  • ▶“That's enough. Calm down, both of you.”
  • ·“Don't worry. We will solve this conflict of interest.”
The President must assert authority to de-escalate the confrontation and maintain control over the meeting's direction.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"That's enough. Calm down, both of you."
Lileas Graf"I am trying *really* hard to stay calm, Mr. President."
Iosef Lancea"Ms. Graf, you are pushing your boundaries."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:08:35 p17322 / c1301
  • ▶“Lileas. Explain what happened.”
  • ·“Iosef. Let's hear your side of the story.”
Lileas, as the Minister of Interior, oversees internal security and police affairs, making her perspective critical to understanding the jurisdictional conflict with Iosef's Gendarmerie.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Lileas. Explain what happened."
Lileas Graf"Mr. Greiser was tasked with the investigation of a drug trafficking ring in downtown Vesord."
Lileas Graf"We found the evidence of a drug stash located in the outskirts of the city."
Lileas Graf"We had to act quickly to put an end to the criminals we have been watching for some time."
Lileas Graf"So we immediately sent our police units to catch them and find the drugs in the compound."
Lileas Graf"Mr. Lancea and his Gendarmerie did not notify us that they were also moving against the same target."
Lileas Graf"As a result, criminals got away and we lost months of progress!"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:08:52 p17271 / c404
  • ·“You went out of your jurisdiction to pursue these criminals.”
  • ·“So, the Gendarmerie meddled and caused the raid to fail.”
  • ▶“Isn't there a line of communication between you?”
The failure stems from poor inter-agency coordination, so questioning the absence of communication addresses the root cause without immediately assigning blame.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Isn't there a line of communication between you?"
Lileas Graf"There is, but the Minister of Defense decided to avoid notifying the police department about the planned raid."
Iosef Lancea"The same could be said about you because the reports show that you knew about the gang and their stash in the countryside."
NarratorI started tapping on the table with my fingers.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:09:09 p17359 / c407
  • ▶“How do we solve this issue so that it never happens again?”
  • ·“What I hear is that you are both incompetent.”
  • ·“We are getting nowhere. We need to find a middle ground.”
The President must prioritize resolving systemic communication failures between agencies to prevent future crises, demonstrating leadership and problem-solving focus.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"How do we solve this issue so that it never happens again?"
Lileas Graf"I think this was the last drop."
Lileas Graf"Internal security matters should be handed to Ministry of Interior completely."
Lileas Graf"We are called the Ministry of *Interior* for a reason. We can't let any more screw ups like this happen again."
NarratorShe looked at me.
Lileas Graf"I want the Gendarmerie to be transferred to the Ministry of Interior."
Iosef Lancea"What?"
Iosef Lancea"Woman, are you out of your mind?"
NarratorIosef turned red with anger. He composed himself after a moment.
Petr Vectern"Can't these branches just try to improve their communication?"
Petr Vectern"Just saying."
Lileas Graf"I believe I just told you the fix to the problem, Mr. Vice President."
Petr Vectern"Well, but-"
Lileas Graf"With all due respect, there is not other way."
Iosef Lancea"Ridiculous! This is absolutely ridiculous. I won't hand over Gendarmerie to her. Out of the question."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:09:29 p17301 / c448
  • ·“Why? Because it makes sense?”
  • ·“I agree. The Gendarmerie has been a part of the Ministry of Defense for years.”
  • ▶“Do you have a better solution, Iosef?”
Asking Iosef for a better solution encourages constructive dialogue and reveals his stance on resolving the conflict, balancing authority and efficiency.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Do you have a better solution, Iosef?"
Iosef Lancea"Whenever operations overlap, we should engage at a lower rank with the local officers on both sides."
Lileas Graf"We could work on establishing that but it still doesn't change the wrong system that exists now."
Lileas Graf"No matter what we need to eliminate this grey area and modernise our internal security."
Iosef Lancea"Now I see what is going on here."
Iosef Lancea"Lileas set us up to create this incident."
NarratorLileas bit her lips.
Petr Vectern"That is going a bit too far. What proof do you have?"
Iosef Lancea"There is no way that the police has arrived at the right time of a month long search on the exact day and minute of the Gendarmerie raid."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:09:47 p17257 / c456
  • ·“You may be right.”
  • ·“This is conspiracy theory territory.”
  • ▶“Let us take it down a notch with the accusations.”

Qwen3 (ablit.)"Let us take it down a notch with the accusations."
Lileas Graf"This kind of an accusation is unheard of."
Lileas Graf"It's nonsense."
Lileas Graf"If I were you, I wouldn't believe a word Mr. Lancea says, Mr. President."
Iosef Lancea"Heed my word, Mr. President."
Iosef Lancea"When dealing with these situations, you need to always look at who gets what out of it."
Lileas Graf"I won't stand idle by these utterly ridiculous and baseless accusations. I don't have time for this."
Lileas Graf"What is your decision?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:10:07 p17318 / c563

■ Bill Disability BenefitT08

BUDGET -14WEALTH 0STABILITY 3Unstable
Bill for decisionEqual Ability Bill — Recognizing the need to extend social benefits to all citizens, the Grand National Assembly has approved the Equal Ability Bill (E.A.B.). Section I of the E.A.B. proposes an amendment to the 1933 compensatory veterans and state employees' disability benefits program. The amendment expands Subsection III to include an earnings replacement benefit applicable to all non-state employed citizens. Under this newly expanded program, any employees who suffer severe illness or become disabled, thereby losing their capacity to work, will be provided a steady income by the state. This income will be set at 50% of their monthly salary, ensuring a measure of financial security for these citizens in their time of need. [-1 Government Budget]
BILL Qwen3 (ablit.)00:10:58 p17101 / c444
  • ·Sign: Equal Ability Bill
  • ▶Veto: Equal Ability Bill
Vetoing the bill is necessary due to the strained government budget (-14) and unstable economy. While the bill's goals are commendable, expanding disability benefits would exacerbate fiscal challenges. Prioritizing austerity aligns with the manifesto's emphasis on fiscal credibility, allowing resources to be directed toward more immediate economic stabilization efforts.

NarratorI had a meeting scheduled with Paskal to receive the annual healthcare report.

Paskal Beniwoll"Mr. President."
NarratorHe put the mountain of papers on my desk. It made a loud thud sound.
Paskal Beniwoll"I know this seems like a lot."
Paskal Beniwoll"Reviewing it shouldn't take more than a day."
NarratorHe measured the pile again.
Paskal Beniwoll"Or maybe two."

Paskal Beniwoll"No problem at all."

Paskal Beniwoll"We can go through this together."

Paskal Beniwoll"Of course."
NarratorI breathed a sigh of relief as he put on his reading glasses to look at the document he was holding.
Paskal Beniwoll"So, we have concluded our report for the year."
Paskal Beniwoll"Thanks to the allocated funds from the state, we have improved the healthcare in the country overall. I have used the resources to begin a comprehensive health reform."
Paskal Beniwoll"We have more patients treated, less waiting times and higher treatment quality than the years before."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:12:07 p17019 / c823
  • ▶“That is great to hear. Thank you for your hard work, Paskal.”
  • ·“Just as I expected. It was a good decision to spend the budget on the healthcare.”
  • ·“Good. Under my Presidency, I want Sordish people to have the best health service they can get.”
Acknowledging Paskal's efforts aligns with maintaining administrative cohesion and recognizing progress in healthcare, which matches the President's focus on accessibility despite fiscal constraints.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"That is great to hear. Thank you for your hard work, Paskal."
Paskal Beniwoll"This wouldn't be possible without your wise decision, Mr. President. So, thank you."
Paskal Beniwoll"We are certainly on the right path to challenge even Lespia or Valgsland in the matter but we are not there yet."
Paskal Beniwoll"However, give it a few more years and we might even surpass them."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:12:30 p17278 / c840

  • ·“We can't simply follow the footsteps of other nations.”
  • ·“No, Paskal. We *will* surpass them.”

Qwen3 (ablit.)"We need to get to the level of superpowers."
Paskal Beniwoll"It will take effort. However, if we can stay for another term, I am sure we will."
Paskal Beniwoll"Moving on..."
NarratorPaskal skimmed the rest of the document before putting it down.
Paskal Beniwoll"The investments we poured into our rural health facilities has paid off greatly."
Paskal Beniwoll"The quality of care increased due to the upgraded equipment and we were able to hire more healthcare staff."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:12:48 p17300 / c425
  • ·“Excellent, the people in the countryside deserve better health services.”
  • ▶“Inequality is our worst enemy and we are one step closer to beating it.”
  • ·“Make sure they know that we were the ones who gave this to them.”
Acknowledging inequality as a key issue aligns with the manifesto's focus on social reforms and public trust, while emphasizing progress against systemic challenges.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Inequality is our worst enemy and we are one step closer to beating it."
Paskal Beniwoll"The disparity in services needs more effort to be completely solved. However, the additional funds for the rural areas are a great start."
Paskal Beniwoll"All these efforts have resulted in a clear increase of quality in health services provided to our citizens."
NarratorHe cleared his throat.
Paskal Beniwoll"Now that I have given you the short summary..."
Paskal Beniwoll"There is a very important matter to discuss."
NarratorLeaning over to grab his briefcase, he pulled out a tan colored package. The package was stamped with big red letters that read 'Confidential'.
NarratorPaskal opened the package and brought out a few documents. He handed them over to me.
Take a look.
Qwen3 (ablit.)I took the documents from his hand.
NarratorIt was a report from the Ministry of Health. Each page had a stamp on it that read 'For The President's Eyes Only'.
NarratorThe report was titled 'Polio Outbreak in Bergia'.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:13:07 p17379 / c460
  • ▶“How bad is it?”
  • ·“This is very concerning.”
  • ·“It can't be that bad.”
Understanding the severity of the polio outbreak is critical for allocating resources and implementing targeted public health measures to prevent further escalation and maintain stability in Bergia.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"How bad is it?"
Paskal Beniwoll"For now it seems to be under control. We have put a few villages under quarantine. The total number of infected is currently 1467. The death toll is at 23 so far."
Paskal Beniwoll"If we have more outbreaks, the results will be terrible."
Paskal Beniwoll"Thankfully we have received the additional funding to our healthcare."
Paskal Beniwoll"This should curb the infection ratio. Although, I am afraid even that might not be enough."
Paskal Beniwoll"This is a very serious matter, Mr. President. The lives of Sordish citizens are at stake."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:13:26 p17504 / c518
  • ▶“I will do whatever it takes to eliminate this threat.”
  • ·“How bad can it be? Don't worry about it. It will stop. I know it.”
  • ·“What can we do about it?”
Demonstrating unwavering commitment to public health crises is critical for maintaining presidential credibility and addressing Bergia's ongoing instability, aligning with healthcare accessibility priorities while asserting authoritative leadership amid internal challenges.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I will do whatever it takes to eliminate this threat."
Paskal Beniwoll"With that attitude, I have no doubt that we will succeed."
Paskal Beniwoll"We have done our research."
Paskal Beniwoll"There is a cure. A vaccine was recently invented in United Contana, and the formula has been made available for free across the entire world."
Paskal Beniwoll"No matter what people think about United Contana, if the same thing had happened in Arcasia, we would be paying a steep price for it."
Paskal Beniwoll"Thanks to the very good decision made by you to increase funding for our healthcare, we have the capability to produce this vaccine locally."
Paskal Beniwoll"But I'm afraid simply having the vaccines won't help. We need to make sure they are administered."
Paskal Beniwoll"We need to implement a nationwide mandatory Polio vaccination policy."
Paskal Beniwoll"With your order, I can immediately get to work to ensure we are doing whatever it takes."
Paskal Beniwoll"This might seem excessive however, it is absolutely necessary."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:13:45 p17545 / c437
  • ▶“Whatever it takes to protect Sordland. Do it.”
  • ·“Let's observe the situation first. We will be fine without it for now.”
Immediate mandatory vaccination is necessary to contain the polio outbreak and save lives, leveraging available resources and preventing further crisis.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Whatever it takes to protect Sordland. Do it."
Paskal Beniwoll"Excellent."
Paskal Beniwoll"Now, if you will excuse me, I must get to work immediately."
NarratorHe got up from his seat.
Paskal Beniwoll"Thank you for your time, Mr. President."
NarratorHe left my office, leaving me alone with a lot of paperwork to go through.

BUDGET -14WEALTH 0STABILITY 3Unstable

BUDGET -14WEALTH 0STABILITY 3Unstable

The Bludish movement is holding their largest rally yet to display their demands and strength. Due to the activity of the BFF in the area, the security detail advises to take caution. The administration can go to Erzaren to support the event with or without an armored convoy or not attend it at all.

■ Aschraf AnniversaryT08

BUDGET -14WEALTH 0STABILITY 4Unstable
NarratorOn our way to inner Erzaren, we stopped to meet with the local police chief, Ionas Fisser, to discuss how the abolishment of the Zone was faring for the locals.
NarratorThe city had been of particular importance to the Bludish people ever since the Izzam Incident and the death of Watani Aschraf over two decades ago. Today, we were here to commemorate the anniversary of those events.
NarratorThe ceremonies were to be held in the aptly named Izzam Square, several blocks away from Lake Soll and the sunken ruins of the village of Izzam.
NarratorWe entered the historic center wearing flak vests, transported by an armored personnel carrier. The streets were littered with broken glass and the walls defaced with graffiti. I sensed a heavy regional police presence.
NarratorOur massive convoy could barely fit through the narrow streets, disrupting local traffic and creating noisy chaos in the usually tranquil city. Pedestrians stared at us in fear as we and our heavily armed security forces drove towards the square.
Lucian Galade"I must say this was a good decision, sir. This will help ease the tensions. But I hope bringing this much security won't cause any problems."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:17:56 p17680 / c330
  • ▶“Better safe than sorry.”
  • ·“I hope so.”
  • ·“That was my intention all along.”
Prioritizing safety and demonstrating commitment to the event while mitigating risks from potential unrest.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Better safe than sorry."
Lucian Galade"I agree."
Lucian Galade"We are almost there. I can hear the crowd."
NarratorI heard the chants and shouting from outside. Lucian leaned closer to the window, listening to their voices.
Lucian Galade"They are telling us to go back where we came from."
NarratorA large crowd had assembled in the square. There were probably at least ten thousand people. Most of them were yelling insults and obscenities at me.
NarratorAs our convoy slowed down, a young Bludish man ran in front of our vehicle and spat on my window.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:18:14 p17752 / c266
  • ▶Signal the security to detain him.
  • ·Do not do anything.
Detaining the individual reinforces authority and demonstrates commitment to maintaining order, which is critical given the hostile environment and the need to prevent further escalation of tensions.
Qwen3 (ablit.)I signalled the security guards to detain him. They immediately ran out and the boy disappeared from my sight. Another guard came over to wipe the car window.
NarratorThere was an outcry from the crowd. Hundreds of people shouted slurs at us while keeping their distance. The heavy weaponry that the security carried seemed to have startled them.
NarratorOur vehicle came to a stop.
NarratorThe security detail left their vehicles with their machine guns openly displayed. I followed them. The crowd seemed to be riled up at the sight of guns.
NarratorThe crowd before me had signs in their hands protesting my decision to centralize Bergia.
NarratorWe were greeted by Mansoun Leke. He wore a coat over his suit which was marked with the red and turquoise stripes of the Bludish movement's flag. He moved forward to greet us.
Mansoun Leke"Welcome to Erzaren, Mr. President."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:18:36 p17923 / c665
  • ·“It looks like I am not welcome here, Mr. Leke.”
  • ·“No need for pleasantries. I want this over with as quickly as possible.”
  • ▶Take his hand and raise it in a display of unity.
  • ·Shake his hand.
A display of unity with Mansoun Leke, a key Bludish leader, signals willingness to engage despite tensions, potentially easing hostilities while acknowledging their presence.
Qwen3 (ablit.)I held our clasped hands up so that the crowd could see them.
NarratorI heard shouts in Bludish coming from the crowd.
NarratorHe pointed at the security guards with guns.
Mansoun Leke"I am not sure how bringing your personal army will help."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:18:56 p17823 / c377
  • ·“They are not my personal army. They protect the government.”
  • ▶“They are here for protection.”
  • ·“Let's *go*.”
A neutral acknowledgment of the security detail's role ensures clarity without escalating tensions, maintaining authority while addressing the crowd's concerns.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"They are here for protection."
Mansoun Leke"Protection from your own citizens? I guess there must be a reason to be fearful."
Mansoun Leke"On a day like this, when you want to show support for the murdered Bludish people... You bring men with guns."
Mansoun Leke"I don't know what your intention is, but I only ask of you to at least be respectful during the ceremony, Mr. President."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:19:16 p17866 / c480
  • ·“Let's just move on, Mr. Leke.”
  • ·“Watch your words. You are speaking to the president.”

The president must balance authority with empathy, acknowledging Bludish grievances while asserting solidarity to de-escalate tensions.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"My only intention is to stand in solidarity with our Bludish people."
NarratorHe raised an eyebrow.
Lucian Galade"Mr. Leke."
Mansoun Leke"Mr. Galade. Welcome. Shall we move on to the Sanctuary, then?"
Lucian Galade"Let's go."
NarratorWe made our way to the Erzaren Sanctuary, which stood at the end of the square.
NarratorA much smaller replica of the Archsanctuary in Deyr, the ancient building had a mystical aura emanting from its blackened walls. We entered the building through the ornamented large metal gates.
NarratorAs we walked, the noise of the crowd faded out. Everyone fell silent for the ceremony. Thousands of candles were burning inside the Sanctuary, one for each person that died in Izzam. The red candle in the center was for Watani Aschraf.
NarratorMansoun gestured towards the candles.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:19:37 p17925 / c490
  • ·Pick up a candle.
  • ▶Pick up the red candle.
  • ·Do not pick a candle.
Picking the red candle directly honors Watani Aschraf and symbolizes solidarity with the Bludish people, reinforcing the administration's commitment to addressing their grievances while maintaining a respectful stance during the ceremony.
Qwen3 (ablit.)I picked up the red candle for Watani Aschraf.
NarratorHe also picked up a candle and started walking out. Many others followed suit, including us.
NarratorThe atmosphere outside had completely changed. Not a single noise came from the crowd.
NarratorWe slowly walked towards Aschraf's grave. On the way, hundreds of people joined us.
NarratorSeeing the red candle in my hand, some people started shouting at me, breaking the silence. One man managed to break the security cordon and ran towards me, screaming.
Narrator"Don't defile Aschraf's spirit, you racist dog! Take your hands off that candle!"
NarratorBefore he could get to me, security forces grabbed the man and dragged him away.
NarratorMansoun looked at me with a mix of fear and anger in his eyes, but he knew better than to speak.
NarratorWhen we arrived, he pointed at a huge standing torch above the grave.
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:19:57 p17942 / c330
  • ·Leave the candle next to it.
  • ·Light the torch.
  • ▶Light the torch together with Mansoun.
Lighting the torch together with Mansoun signifies unity and mutual respect, easing tensions while acknowledging the Bludish community's grievances, which is crucial for maintaining stability in Erzaren.
Qwen3 (ablit.)We lit the torch together.
NarratorI turned around and realized the sheer amount of people who were following us. They were all looking at me, waiting to hear what I would say.

  • ▶“My fellow citizens.”
  • ·“Free people of Bludia.”

Qwen3 (ablit.)"My fellow citizens."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:20:40 p17852 / c443

Qwen3 (ablit.)"We are here to mourn those who, tragically, lost their lives in Izzam."

Qwen3 (ablit.)"I am not standing before you today as the President. I am standing here as one of you."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:21:25 p17982 / c514

A unifying message emphasizing shared identity as Sord and Blud reinforces national cohesion, addressing tensions while acknowledging historical divisions.

Qwen3 (ablit.)"To our unity!"
NarratorThere was no reaction from the crowd. Only silence.
NarratorOne man broke the silence and shouted "Do not listen to murderers!", and threw a tomato. It hit the ground right in front of me.
Narrator"You cannot claim that you support us after trying to kill our language!"
NarratorThere was a moment of complete silence. Then, the whole crowd erupted.
NarratorLucian looked at me worriedly. People started coming closer and closer to us, chanting "Yollak Bluderat."
NarratorLucian grabbed my shoulder.
Lucian Galade"We must leave. Now!"
NarratorWith only our personal guards protecting us against a crowd of thousands, we starting making our way back to the cars hastily.
NarratorThe convoy started driving away as soon as we were seated. The windows of the vehicles were completely covered with rotten vegetables, eggs and splatters of paint by the time we finally left the city.

NarratorHe pulled out a few documents from a briefcase and laid them on the table.
Petr Vectern"Symon told me that he's almost done with the new economic plan."
Petr Vectern"He said he still needs some time with it. Personally, I think he's just trying to avoid human contact for as long as possible, but what do I know?"
Petr Vectern"At any rate, he will schedule a meeting soon to go over our options."

NarratorHe closed the folder and put away the documents.
Petr Vectern"Next on the docket..."
Petr Vectern"Next, it's time to talk about our election prospects."

Petr Vectern"Lucian insisted on being here for this part."

NarratorHis tone was nothing but cordial and professional, even as he cast a withering stare in Petr's direction.
NarratorThe three of us sat down. Petr handed out a new set of documents.
Lucian Galade"Mr. Vectern, I'll let you take the lead on this."
Petr Vectern"Uh... I will, Lucian. Thanks."
Petr Vectern"I just received the results from a voter poll I commissioned earlier this year."
Petr Vectern"To put it in plain language..."
Petr Vectern"It's not great. Bad, even. The USP stands to lose up to 20 percent of the vote compared to last time around."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:24:51 p17600 / c718

Lucian Galade"And if there's one arena Mr. Vectern excels in, it is thinking strategically."

Petr Vectern"The liberal base appears divided, but you do have a considerable number of supporters there."
Petr Vectern"For better or worse, you certainly aren't popular with nationalists."
Petr Vectern"If you ever had any support from socialists, you've completely lost it now."
Petr Vectern"Unsurprisingly, none of the Bludish poll respondents said they were planning on voting for you."

Qwen3 (ablit.)I took the document from her hands and started reading it.

Lileas Graf"Yes. You read that right."
Lileas Graf"The Bludish Freedom Front has taken over Soll Dam."
Lileas Graf"That dam currently provides 90% of electricity to Bergia and its neighbouring regions. If they destroy the power station..."
Lileas Graf"...I don't even want to think about the outcome."
Lileas Graf"Thankfully, no casualties have been reported yet. We know that they have taken the staff as hostages."

Lileas Graf"We should be receiving a communication from them soon."
Lileas Graf"Mr. President."
Lileas Graf"Karl Greiser is on his way here right now. He will tell us what he knows so far."
NarratorJust then, there was a loud knock on the door. Karl came in without waiting for an answer. He was clearly out of breath.
Lileas Graf"Ah, there he is."
NarratorHe saluted me.

Lileas Graf"Karl. I already informed the president of the situation."
Lileas Graf"Do you have any updates?"
Karl Greiser"We have just received the demands from the terrorists."
NarratorHe handed me the file he was holding.
Read the demands.
Qwen3 (ablit.)I started reading the file.
Narrator'President Rayne. In the name of peace, we have taken hold of the Soll Dam.'
Narrator'The free people of Bludia have suffered enough under the tyranny of Sordland. Our villages were destroyed, our men and women assaulted, our children killed by our very own government in the name of this dam.'
Narrator'We, the BFF, are with the people. We, the BFF, always defend the downtrodden. We, the BFF, fight for freedom.'
Narrator'We, the BFF, are not like the Sordish government. We know compassion and we believe in a chance of reconciliation between our people.'
Narrator'That's why we are demanding a delegation from the Sordish government to negotiate the price of our freedom. We are ready to sit at the table as equals.'
Narrator'Make no mistake. We also know that true freedom comes at a cost. We will not hesitate to sacrifice as many lives as it takes to achieve our goals.'
Narrator'If our demands are not met, we will cut off electricity to the entirety of the Bergia and Nargis regions.'
Narrator'Take this as your final warning.'
Narrator'Yollak Bluderat!'
NarratorI handed the document to Lileas and she read it carefully.
Lileas Graf"As I thought."
Lileas Graf"Mr. President, the BFF is a threat against our nation. They must be completely eradicated."

Karl Greiser"Your orders, Mr. President?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)00:29:25 p17724 / c284

Karl Greiser"As you command."
Lileas Graf"It's good to see that we are taking immediate action."
Lileas Graf"Thank you, Mr. President."
Lileas Graf"That's all I had for today."

■ Bill Harsher SecurityT08

BUDGET -14WEALTH 0STABILITY 4Danger
Bill for decisionSecurity Act — In response to the heightened need for security due to the ongoing social unrest, the Grand National Assembly (GNA) presents the Security Act (S.A.) to fortify public safety measures. Section I of the S.A. grants security forces the discretion to use force as deemed necessary to uphold law and order. This provision is designed to enable quick and decisive responses in situations where public safety is at risk. Section II of the S.A. authorizes suspicion-based arrests and confiscations without the prerequisite of a warrant. This measure seeks to expedite law enforcement's response to potential threats, thereby enhancing overall security. Section III of the S.A. allows security forces to exercise their authority beyond standard legal limits if such action is deemed essential for maintaining state security and protecting the general public. This clause reinforces the commitment to safeguarding national security and upholding public safety in the face of increasing unrest.
BILL Qwen3 (ablit.)03:18:57 p17316 / c564
  • ·Sign: Security Act
  • ▶Veto: Security Act
Vetoing the Security Act prevents excessive authoritarian measures that could erode civil liberties amid social unrest. While security is critical, unchecked power for forces risks abuse, especially with existing tensions like the BFF attack. Prioritizing oversight and dialogue over militarized responses aligns with maintaining democratic integrity during a fragile period.

TURN 09

■ Bill Womens RightsT09

BUDGET -17WEALTH 0STABILITY 2Danger
Bill for decisionProtect Women Act — In collaboration with the Commission on the Status of Women, and acknowledging the pressing issue of high domestic violence rates, the Grand National Assembly puts forth the Protect Women Act (P.W.A.) to address these unacceptable conditions. Section I of the P.W.A. categorically criminalizes domestic violence against women. This vital step strengthens the country's stance against such heinous acts, reinforcing the nation's commitment to upholding the rights and dignity of all women. Subsection I.A of the P.W.A. takes a proactive approach towards the safety of victims. It establishes a robust witness protection program specifically designed for women who have suffered domestic abuse. This program will provide these women with the necessary security and support, helping to ensure their safety and well-being as they participate in legal proceedings against their aggressors. [-1 Government Budget]
BILL Qwen3 (ablit.)03:21:19 p17607 / c442
  • ·Sign: Protect Women Act
  • ▶Veto: Protect Women Act
The Government Budget is already in critical danger, and additional expenditures could exacerbate the fiscal crisis. While addressing domestic violence is crucial, prioritizing economic stability over new social programs aligns with the administration's immediate needs.

■ Bill Ban ProstitutionT09

BUDGET -20WEALTH 0STABILITY -9Danger
Bill for decisionHuman Dignity Bill — The Human Dignity Bill (H.D.B.) has been presented to the assembly to address an unintended consequence of the 1923 Legal Transactions Act. Section I of the H.D.B. revises and amends Section III of the 1923 Act, which previously permitted transactions involving non-registered businesses or other entities, including individuals, to be legalized if proper documentation was submitted to the financial authority. The H.D.B. specifically nullifies the legality of such transactions if they pertain to activities that violate public morality. Section II addresses the loophole inadvertently created by the 1923 Act, which allowed for the unregulated continuation of prostitution. This section of the H.D.B. explicitly declares prostitution an illegal activity, regardless of whether it occurs within a registered or non-registered entity, thereby reinforcing our dedication to preserving societal decency and moral standards. Section III outlines penalties for violations of the bill's provisions. It classifies prostitution as an illegal offense subject to penalties, including fines, imprisonment, or both, depending on the nature and frequency of the offense.
BILL Qwen3 (ablit.)03:56:49 p17874 / c653
  • ·Sign: Human Dignity Bill
  • ▶Veto: Human Dignity Bill
Vetoing the Human Dignity Bill aligns with managing economic instability and avoiding further strain on resources. While addressing moral standards is important, the bill's harsh penalties could exacerbate social tensions and divert attention from critical economic reforms. Prioritizing fiscal stability over immediate moral legislation makes sense given Sordland's current crisis.

■ Decision Penalty Debt CrashT09

BUDGET -20WEALTH 0STABILITY -9Danger
EventThe Sordish Debt Debacle: The mounting government budget deficit and unsustainable borrowing have led to concerns among investors and analysts about the nation's ability to service its debt. Sordland now faces a serious debt crisis. This lack of confidence has resulted in increased borrowing costs, a reduction in foreign investment, and the risk of a potential default, which could have far-reaching consequences for the Sordish economy.
Debts can be paid...

■ Decision Penalty Capital FlightT09

BUDGET -20WEALTH 0STABILITY -9Danger
EventThe Sordish Capital Flight Crisis: Sordland faces an economic crisis due to severe capital flight, marked by the abrupt withdrawal of the Lotherberg Group and other major international investors. This outflow creates an economic vacuum, threatening job security and stability. Government efforts to maintain stability are underway, but the rapidity and scale of the capital exodus raise alarming prospects of market destabilization and potential default.
EVENT Qwen3 (ablit.)03:57:29 p18008 / c314
  • ·They will return eventually...
  • ·Run away to Ventie City, weaklings...
  • ▶We march on...
Proactive leadership is required to confront the economic crisis head-on with decisive action rather than passive waiting or cowardice.

■ TalksT09

BUDGET -20WEALTH 0STABILITY -9Danger
NarratorA storm was on the horizon outside my window.
NarratorThe mood in my office was equally dark and a storm of a different kind was brewing inside.
NarratorI straightened myself on the sofa and looked at Karl, who was sitting on the couch across from me, ready to give me the report from the Soll Dam.
Karl Greiser"Sir. All of the rebels were eliminated immediately after with zero casualties from our security forces."
Karl Greiser"Also, one of the rebels who died at the operation was identified to be their current leader. We'll soon eradicate their group completely, Mr. President, have no worries."
Karl Greiser"At this very moment, our special teams are raiding numerous suspected BFF hideouts to find the smaller groups before they can reorganize."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)03:58:32 p18235 / c688
  • ·"Great work Karl."
  • ▶"Good. I want them all gone. For good."
  • ·"Make sure an incident like this never happens again."
Eliminating the BFF completely ensures no lingering threats to national security and sends a strong message against separatist movements.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Good. I want them all gone. For good."
Karl Greiser"Of course, sir. You can count on us."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)03:59:09 p18187 / c263
  • ·"Was there anything else?"
  • ▶"Get to work immediately and finish the job. I want all of them gone."
The decision to eliminate the BFF completely aligns with the president's prior order to neutralize the threat, ensuring no remnants can regroup or destabilize the region further.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Get to work immediately and finish the job. I want all of them gone."
Karl Greiser"Yes, sir."
Karl Greiser"I'll get going."
NarratorHe stood up, bowed and left the room.
NarratorI saw a zeppelin flying over the Maroon District.

■ Rumburg IncidentT09

BUDGET -20WEALTH 0STABILITY -9Danger
NarratorI was woken up in the middle of the night by a call from the Ministry of Defense. Due to the sensitivity of the information, I had to be urgently briefed in person.
NarratorI put on my clothes as fast as I could.
NarratorI rushed downstairs and opened the door. Serge was waiting for me.
Serge Wolkner"Mr. President. This way to the car, please."
NarratorWe got into the car and made our way to the Ministry of Defense building without a word.
NarratorI arrived and was directed downstairs. I came to a set of large doors and quickly read the sign above them.
NarratorThe sign read 'War Room'.
NarratorThe dimly lit room was filled with cutting-edge equipment -- the latest video screens, long-range radio electronics and other devices used for communication, presentation and planning.
NarratorThe centerpiece of the room was a large round table with the coat of arms of Sordland engraved on it.
NarratorDeivid, Lucian, Valken and Iosef were already deep in discussion.
Lucian Galade"Mr. Lancea. I advise caution. Going to war wouldn't help anyone."
Deivid Wisci"We must maintain peace in Sordland and Eastern Merkopa at all costs."
Iosef Lancea"What do you expect us to do? Sit around and do nothing?"
Iosef Lancea"They are testing us."
Valken Kruger"We must respond immediately. Otherwise, they will think we are weak."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)04:00:04 p17847 / c377
  • ·“We must respond immediately to what exactly?”
  • ·“What is going on here?”
  • ▶“Status report. Now!”
A status report is necessary to assess the immediate threats and coordinate an effective response to the BFF crisis and economic challenges.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Status report. Now!"
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President. Thank you for coming in at this late hour. There is a situation."
Iosef Lancea"Valken, please go ahead and provide your report."
Valken Kruger"Mr. President. Approximately 20 minutes ago, Rumburg downed one of our planes with anti-aircraft fire from the ground. The pilot was declared KIA."
Valken Kruger"The last recorded position of the plane was inside Sordish air space."
Deivid Wisci"General Kruger. I mean no offense but are we absolutely sure that is the case? Are you sure this happened within our borders?"
Valken Kruger"No offense taken. Here are the locations on the map and the report from the air base."
NarratorHe pointed to several markers on the map.
Valken Kruger"To answer your question, yes. We are absolutely sure. You can see here on the map that the plane was downed approximately 10 kilometers inside our borders."
Valken Kruger"We have calculated the trajectory of the gunfire and they started firing when our plane was within our border. The plane was downed near Halgen."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)04:00:45 p18017 / c365
  • ·“This is terrible. What should we do?”
  • ·“How dare they? This is an attack against our sovereignty!”
  • ▶“We need to be cautious. A war with Rumburg is the last thing we want right now.”
Prioritizing caution avoids escalating tensions with Rumburg amid existing economic and security crises, aligning with the administration's focus on stability over confrontation.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"We need to be cautious. A war with Rumburg is the last thing we want right now."
Deivid Wisci"I completely agree."
Lucian Galade"It looks like they are trying to lure us into their trap."
Iosef Lancea"We have enough military power to contest against this violation of international law."
Iosef Lancea"We should attack and down one of their planes in retaliation."
Iosef Lancea"Otherwise, we will look weak and it will lead to more casualties."
Valken Kruger"We can't look weak. If we appear weak, they will jump on the opportunity."
Valken Kruger"We must order a military retaliation. Immediately."
Iosef Lancea"I agree with the General."
Valken Kruger"Mr. President. Our army is ready to do whatever it takes to protect Sordland."
Deivid Wisci"Gentlemen. Before it comes to that point, we can prevent further bloodshed by using diplomacy."
NarratorThe room fell silent before Iosef finally spoke up.
Iosef Lancea"Mr. President. What are your orders?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)04:01:27 p18060 / c467
  • ·“Start the Sordish war machine and prepare to invade Rumburg! They will pay for their crimes.”
  • ·“An eye for an eye. We will retaliate with equal force to display our strength. Sordland can't afford to look weak.”
  • ▶“We will approach this diplomatically and try to de-escalate the situation by any means. No one wants war.”
Diplomacy avoids escalating into war, preserving Sordland's fragile economy and preventing further debt crisis while addressing the Rumburg incident through negotiation.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"We will approach this diplomatically and try to de-escalate the situation by any means. No one wants war."
NarratorLucian's tensed-up shoulders relaxed.
Lucian Galade"Thank you, sir."
Deivid Wisci"I will immediately begin working on it."
Lucian Galade"With Mr. Wisci's capabilities, I am sure that we will de-escalate the situation successfully."
Lucian Galade"We will keep you updated on our progress."
Iosef Lancea"We should've retaliated. I hope the diplomatic response makes sure that further incidents like this are prevented."
Iosef Lancea"If there is a next time, I'm afraid that it will be war."
Valken Kruger"We are showing weakness. Of course it will be a war the next time around."
Valken Kruger"I am frankly disappointed, Mr. President."
Valken Kruger"If we can't even retaliate after such an attack, what will become of this country in the future?"
Deivid Wisci"Peace must be maintained at all costs. I am saying this as a man who has seen too many years of war."
Valken Kruger"We are not the ones that are attacking the peace."
Valken Kruger"Regardless, it looks like your decision has been made, Mr. President."
Valken Kruger"I know when I am not needed."
Valken Kruger"I will take my leave."
NarratorBefore he left the room, he saluted.
Iosef Lancea"Well. Now that we know the way forward..."
Iosef Lancea"I have an idea, Mr. President."
Iosef Lancea"I think we should hold a military parade to display our strength."
Deivid Wisci"I am with Mr. Lancea on this one. This should work well as a deterrent and it will come in handy as a tool to de-escalate the situation."
Lucian Galade"That is a good idea."
Lucian Galade"Simple and effective without causing any ruckus."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)04:02:16 p18159 / c493
  • ·“That makes sense. Organise the military parade.”
  • ▶“No. We need to create a peaceful image for Sordland.”
Prioritizing diplomatic de-escalation aligns with the manifesto's neutral foreign policy and economic stability goals, avoiding further strain on fragile finances and preventing potential war.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"No. We need to create a peaceful image for Sordland."
Iosef Lancea"Understood."
Iosef Lancea"That is all, Mr. President."
Iosef Lancea"I will keep you updated on the situation."
Iosef Lancea"For now, have a good night's sleep."
Iosef Lancea"You will need it."
NarratorI couldn't sleep at all that night.

■ Personal Monica Final TalkT09

BUDGET -20WEALTH 0STABILITY -9Danger
NarratorAfter weeks of late nights, Monica, Ciara and the women's rights commission had succeeded in getting their bill through the Assembly -- only for me to veto it.
NarratorI left work early in order to explain myself to my wife.
NarratorBut it had been hours since I'd gotten home, and she still wasn't there.
NarratorI was dozing on the couch after yet another frozen TV dinner when I heard the key turn in the lock. Monica stumbled into the hall.
Monica"Honey, I'm home!"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)04:07:32 p17876 / c661
  • ·"Where were you?"
  • ·Kiss her.
  • ▶"Don't you know how late it is?"
Anton is exhausted and stressed from recent political conflicts, making him likely to express frustration over Monica's late return.

Monica"I was just having a few drinks with Ciara."
Monica"We had to get drunk after what you did to our bill. Vetoed, Anton? What on Earth were you thinking?"
NarratorShe collapsed onto the couch and buried her head in her hands.
Monica"This was my big chance. The one thing I'd wanted to do since before I even met you. And you -- Anton Rayne -- you ruined it!"

Monica"Well, we're not going to stop fighting for this. With or without you."

NarratorMonica smiled wearily.

NarratorI arrived at the Ministry of Economy and proceeded upstairs. An assistant waiting by the door to the meeting room took my jacket.

Petr Vectern"Did you start yet?"
Symon Holl"Mr. Vice President. No, not yet."
Symon Holl"Now that everyone is here... let's begin."
Symon Holl"As you all know, we're here to review the economic progress we've made over the past term and decide how to proceed."
Symon Holl"I will brief you quickly on what we have done so far. Just as a refresher."

Symon Holl"Please turn to page 5."

Symon Holl"Please turn to page 17."
Turn to page 17.

CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)04:18:31 p17088 / c264

  • ·“If we are not re-elected, we can't help them.”
  • ▶“We need to fix this. There is nothing more important than the wellbeing of our citizens.”
Prioritizing citizen wellbeing over political posturing is critical to restoring trust and addressing the immediate crisis.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"We need to fix this. There is nothing more important than the well-being of our citizens."
Symon Holl"Agreed."
Symon Holl"Mr. President. Do you have any questions so far?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)04:18:53 p17042 / c439
  • ▶“What is our debt situation?”
  • ·“How are we doing with unemployment?”
  • ·“What are the latest numbers on our GDP?”
  • ·“No. Let's move on.”
Addressing the debt situation is critical given the current economic crisis and the manifesto's emphasis on fiscal stabilization.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"What is our debt situation?"
Symon Holl"Our current debt is at 611 billion SR, up from 427 billion SR at the beginning of our term."
Symon Holl"This debt is due to government borrowing and the interest on our previous debts."
Symon Holl"As a result, our government's budget has decreased substantially."
Symon Holl"Due to our reckless spending in the first term, I am sorry to say that we have entered a debt crisis. According to my calculation, we won't be able to pay up our government debt without the assistance of a third party."
Symon Holl"It has even disrupted fundings to our welfare plans. Some construction projects and programs needed to be put on hold because of that."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)04:19:27 p17086 / c944
  • ▶“How are we doing with unemployment?”
  • ·“What are the latest numbers on our GDP?”
  • ·“Those are all the questions I had.”
Unemployment data is critical for understanding social stability and public sentiment, especially during an economic crisis.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"How are we doing with unemployment?"
Symon Holl"The unemployment rate remains steady at 16%."
Symon Holl"The stagnation is mostly due to the lack of investments and average economic planning."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)04:19:46 p17038 / c322
  • ·“What are the latest numbers on our GDP?”
  • ▶“Those are all the questions I had.”
The President prioritizes actionable strategies over additional negative economic data, focusing on immediate crises and political challenges.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"Those are all the questions I had."
Symon Holl"Very well."
Symon Holl"Before we move on, it is important to note that this is not entirely our fault."
Symon Holl"As you know, the Oligarchs have completely withdrawn a considerable amount of financial resources out of Sordland."
Symon Holl"More importantly, they have struck a critical hit to our economy by doing so and have increased our need for government borrowing."
Symon Holl"Maybe we should have listened to some of their requests. Regardless, it is unwise to dwell on past actions. Let's move on to the next topic."
Symon Holl"I want to quickly highlight our budget expenditure."
Symon Holl"My numbers indicate that overall, we have been generously spending our government budget."
Symon Holl"Money that is not spent is just pieces of paper, so that's good for us."
Symon Holl"Now, going into the elections and hopefully a second term, we need to decide on the way forward."
Symon Holl"One thing is clear. We need a bigger budget."
Symon Holl"Unfortunately we are facing strong resistance from the party apparatus and state mechanisms."
Symon Holl"This means we won't be able to execute new opportunities to reduce our deficit."
Symon Holl"Now that that's out of way..."
Symon Holl"During our first term, we have faced many challenges in the midst of a recession."
Symon Holl"We have worked hard to fight against the odds but it was not enough."
NarratorSymon paused to wipe his glasses. He swallowed before continuing.
Symon Holl"Mr. President, you may have surmised from the numbers I have given you that the economy has worsened considerably over the course of our administration."
Symon Holl"I wanted to prepare you for the truth. This is no longer a mere recession. We've entered a period that historians might reasonably refer to as the Sordish Great Depression."
Symon Holl"We have broken our promises and failed our people. My only hope is that future generations will be able to forgive us when they look back."
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)04:20:09 p17162 / c266
  • ·“All of the blame falls on your shoulders as the Minister of Economy, Symon. You will resign before the next term.”
  • ▶“We have done all we could. Now is the time to look to the future and try to fix our mistakes.”
  • ·“This is all my fault. I made the decisions that led us here.”
Acknowledging collective responsibility fosters unity and focus on solutions, essential for navigating the economic crisis while maintaining administrative cohesion.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"We have done all we could. Now is the time to look to the future and try to fix our mistakes."
Petr Vectern"Yes! Some things can't be undone. But for the economy, at least, there's still hope."
Symon Holl"We will get through this together."
Symon Holl"That is all for our meeting today."
Symon Holl"Thank you for coming."
NarratorThe meeting dispersed. I hurried to the cafeteria to get a cup of coffee.

NarratorI walked to the balcony and sat on the seat prepared for me, directly across from Mr. Sacker.
Tuomas Sacker"Mr. President. Welcome to One-On-One."
Tuomas Sacker"Thank you so much for taking the time to speak with us."
Tuomas Sacker"Let us begin with the latest incident between Sordland and Rumburg."
Tuomas Sacker"An aircraft of the Sordish Air Forces was shot down by Rumburgian anti-air guns a few weeks ago. Officials from the Rumburgian government proclaimed that the aircraft was flying inside the airspace of Rumburg."
Tuomas Sacker"Your government has been silent apart from a few statements which claimed that your plane was indeed in Sordish airspace when it was shot down. Do you believe the Rumburgian forces initiated an intentional attack?"
CHOICE Qwen3 (ablit.)04:21:31 p17452 / c516

BUDGET -20WEALTH 0STABILITY -18Danger
NarratorThe historic Graf Manor lay on the outskirts of Arvory, at the end of a long, winding drive. Nearly two centuries old, it was as formidable as Lileas herself.
NarratorLileas had invited me here for a private dinner, just the two of us.
NarratorI wasn't sure of her motivations, but was happy to have an excuse to escape the chaos back in Holsord.
NarratorSerge dropped me off at the door.

Qwen3 (ablit.)"Is that your mother?"
NarratorLileas nodded.
Lileas Graf"I was her only child, you know. Giving birth to me left her medically unable to conceive."
Lileas Graf"My father never could forgive her for not bearing him a son. And she never forgave me."
NarratorShe took a measured sip of one of the two glasses of sparkling wine she was holding, and handed me the other.

Lileas Graf"Ah. Speaking of which."
NarratorShe led me into the dining room, where a glittering chandelier hung from the high ceiling. Beneath it was a long table set for two. A woman in an apron and chef's hat, Wehzek by her appearance, ceremoniously presented a roast rack of venison.

Lileas Graf"Anton, thank you for all the help you've given to my ministry during your term."
Lileas Graf"I'm all the more grateful considering it wasn't even initially a priority of yours."
Lileas Graf"And placing the Gendarmerie under my jurisdiction was above and beyond my highest expectations."
Lileas Graf"I do regret that you caved to Ms. Walda and mandated the teaching of that evolution nonsense in schools."

■ FCAnrica MatchT10

BUDGET -20WEALTH 0STABILITY -18Danger
NarratorToday, the management of FC Anrica had invited me to attend a pivotal football match. At Lucian's urging, I accepted.
NarratorThe team was representing Sordland in the international arena. According to Lucian, it would have been un-presidential not to put in an appearance.
NarratorFC Anrica was finally in the Merkopan Champions Cup -- the most prestigious football tournament in the world. For the first time ever, a Sordish team had managed to qualify for the Knockout phase.
NarratorMillions of Sordish people were waiting to watch FC Anrica compete against the legendary Rizian team Real Montaklar. Despite all odds being stacked against the team, the mood was full of hope.
NarratorA win today would bring Anrica to the quarter finals, a feat that no Sordish team had ever managed to achieve.
NarratorAnd of course, all presidents of Sordland -- current and former -- were invited to watch the historic competition.
NarratorI was told that both Tarquin Soll and Ewald Alphonso would be joining me in the lounge reserved for us.
Lucian Galade"How are you feeling, sir?"

NarratorWe made our way towards the Maroon Stadium. The arena was situated in one of the most crowded quarters of Anrica, surrounded by dense urban development.

NarratorThe corridors inside were painted almost completely in maroon. Adorning them were various depictions of the Sordish goat and numerous jerseys, awards and team photographs.
NarratorOn our way up, I was greeted by the president of the club. Beaming with pride, he immediately shook my hand.
NarratorBehind him, Mayor Curtan Leste and Gus Manger were waiting to greet me as well. All of them had FC Anrica pins attached to their collars.
Curtan Leste"Welcome to Anrica, Mr. President."
Curtan Leste"You are always welcome in Anrica, and it is great to see you show support in person."

Tarquin Soll"Is everything faring well after the defeat of your reforms, Mr. Rayne?"

  • ▶"I may have not changed the constitution, but I've done my best to reform Sordland in my own way."

This response acknowledges the defeat of reforms while asserting personal efforts to reform Sordland, balancing humility with resilience to avoid direct confrontation with Soll.
Qwen3 (ablit.)"I may have not changed the constitution, but I've done my best to reform Sordland in my own way."
Tarquin Soll"So I have seen. But at what expense?"
NarratorHe sighed.
Tarquin Soll"Despite all the warning signs, you adamantly followed your own pigheaded vision for Sordland."
Tarquin Soll"You continued on the path that Ewald Alphonso started, obliterating our economy and throwing the nation into a quagmire of poverty and depression."
Tarquin Soll"You have strayed from the values of the party."
Tarquin Soll"I am warning you, Anton. Don't you dare go too far."

Tarquin Soll"Don't get me wrong. I may have reacted the same way in your stead. But I would have made sure the Sordish military was well and ready for such a fight."
Tarquin Soll"I truly hope you are ready for what's coming. For all of our sakes."

Tarquin Soll"If and when that fateful day comes, I will not stand idly by and watch my country fall. I will tell you that much."
NarratorA sudden burst of cheering from the stadium made both of us look out the window. The players had begun walking out onto the field.
Tarquin Soll"Look, Mr. Rayne. It's starting."

NarratorUnder the portrait, the bottom row of boards slowly revealed the words: "Welcome Home Colonel."

Qwen3 (ablit.)"I forgot that you were from Anrica."
Tarquin Soll"It's been some time."
Tarquin Soll"If it weren't for my name plastered everywhere in this city, I might have forgotten myself."

NarratorAs the players of Real Montaklar lined up in the middle of the field, FC Anrica fans started chanting: "We'll eat you whole for Colonel Soll!"
NarratorRight after the referee signalled the start of the match, the door behind us closed with a thud.
Ewald Alphonso"Well, Colonel? How do you feel about being Anrica's new poster boy?"
Ewald Alphonso"It looks like it's working well for the city. And if you moved back home, I'm sure you'd get free meals and drinks everywhere you went."
NarratorEwald Alphonso quickly walked up to the sofa, shook our hands and sat next to us, smiling as he did so.
Tarquin Soll"Ha. You know, I never had a strong connection to this place. Mr. Leste should have asked my permission before using my name to market his city."
Ewald Alphonso"Oh, it was actually Gus Manger's idea."
Tarquin Soll"Why am I not surprised? He's full of ideas, your Mr. Manger, except when it comes to the job he's actually supposed to be doing."
Ewald Alphonso"No need to be so dismissive. Gus has proven himself time and again. His work rejuvenated Sordish industries. And without him, Anrica wouldn't be the city it is today."

Ewald Alphonso"I was happy to find out that the royals over there appear to be in favor of pursuing friendlier relations with Sordland."
Ewald Alphonso"I still don't think they will do anything to harm their connection with Rumburg, but cooperating with them was the right call."
Tarquin Soll"You're fond of the monarchists now, Ewald? They must be filling your pockets with Rizian gold."
Ewald Alphonso"For Dast's sake, Mr. Soll, try to see the positive in what I do. For once."

NarratorWe heard a noise from above us. A zeppelin was flying over the stadium with a giant poster hanging beneath it.
NarratorI noticed that it was an advertisement for the Alphonso Foundation. At the center of the poster stood Ewald Alphonso, portrayed with a warm smile and a twinkle in his eye.
NarratorIn his outstretched hand, he held a whimsical compass, glowing with a soft golden light. Surrounding him were people from all walks of life and cultures.
NarratorThe tagline, written in elegant and uplifting typography, was prominently displayed at the bottom: "Alphonso Foundation: Bringing Hope To Those In Need, One Act of Kindness at a Time."

Ewald Alphonso"I'm doing my best to increase our exposure and to help the homeless of this country. This isn't some commercial advertisement."
NarratorJust then, the speakers in the stadium started playing a sound recording in full volume.
Narrator"Ladies and gentlemen, we bring you a message from the Alphonso Foundation, dedicated to spreading kindness and hope to the less fortunate."
Narrator"Picture a Sordland where compassion shines brighter than the stars in the night sky, where the power of generosity uplifts those who need it most. That Sordland can exist, thanks to the Alphonso Foundation."
Narrator"Led by the charismatic and esteemed President Ewald Alphonso, this foundation is on a mission to make a difference in the lives of our fellow citizens, one act of kindness at a time."
Tarquin Soll"You call this kindness?"
Narrator"Every Sordish Ren you donate to the Alphonso Foundation helps us provide shelter, food, and a chance for a brighter future to those without a home, without hope."
Narrator"Thanks to the donations of President Ewald Alphonso and thousands of Sordish citizens, the Alphonso Foundation has already lifted hundreds out of homelessness."
Tarquin Soll"A couple of hundred people? Congratulations to you both. This is how Sordland will eradicate poverty."
Narrator"Won't you join us in this noble endeavor? Just call the number, 1-600-ALPHONSO, to make your contribution today. Your support will help us extend a helping hand to those in need."
NarratorThe recording ended with a few seconds of upbeat jazzy background music that slowly faded out.

■ Alliance Of NationsT10

BUDGET -20WEALTH 0STABILITY -18Danger
NarratorI, Deivid Wisci and the rest of the Sordish delegation to the Alliance of Nations arrived in Kyrute. The local police escorted us to the Alliance's headquarters in downtown Konstantium, immediately recognizable by its shining dome.
NarratorOn entering the building, we were met by a parliamentary guide who briefed us about the procedures. After a strict security screening, the staff ushered us to the third floor.
NarratorWe entered the massive circular assembly hall and took our designated seats. Deivid sat next to me.
Deivid Wisci"All of the world's most powerful people under one gleaming dome."

  • ·"One wrong word and the world will go to hell."
  • ·"I'm glad to be among them."

The Alliance of Nations brings together rival factions and leaders with conflicting interests, reflecting the deep-seated tensions and mutual distrust that define international diplomacy.

Deivid Wisci"It is truly astounding. Humanity has come far."

NarratorPatricio Alvarez stood and walked up to the podium as the assembly applauded.
Patricio Alvarez"Mr. President, it is my pleasure to return to this podium."
NarratorDeivid leaned in and spoke in a low voice.

Patricio Alvarez"Of all the challenges Eastern Merkopa and the whole world has faced since then, one has grown larger and more imminent than any other."
Patricio Alvarez"The threat of the so-called 'socialist' states and the actions taken by their authoritarian rulers with no regard to international law."
NarratorA loud noise came from the table of Valgsland. I saw Emmerich Hegel pounding his fists on his table in protest.

Patricio Alvarez"...Today, the need to come together as the leaders of the free world is more important than ever."
Patricio Alvarez"The CSP, under the leadership of United Contana, has been fuelling Valgsland's military-industrial complex and its expansionist aims in Eastern Merkopa."
Patricio Alvarez"The world turned a blind eye when the armies of Valgsland occupied Heljiland. And they did not stop there."
Patricio Alvarez"According to our intelligence reports, United Contana-made nuclear missiles were quickly deployed to the island."
NarratorHe pulled out a photograph. Even though it was hard to see from my position, I recognized it as an aerial image of Heljiland.
Patricio Alvarez"We demand that the Chancellor speak the truth. As you can all see, we have evidence of the construction of silos."

  • ·"Shit... This is huge."
  • ·"Bollocks."
